Our world is certainly healthier but... slightly annoying.

France is a beautiful contry, visited by millions of people each year. But if you are invited to a French person's home, do you know the correct etiquette? Here are solme tips: 1. DON'T be late. Make sure you arrive a few minutes early and wait. 2. DON'T sit too close to people. Some people stand so close that I can smell what they had for breakfast. You're expected to leave at least two feet between you and the person you're speaking with. 3. DON'T cross your arms across your chest, unless you're a king or queen. People might think you're angry or bored.  4. DON'T ask personal questions. If you ask questions that are considered rude  'How much money do you make?' 'How old are you?' 'Are you married?'  you might not get an honest answer.

He told me that they're going to close three oil refineries next year. Because of the rise in crude prices, they have trouble selling their product. This problem could affect our storage depot, but he told me not worrying about that. They're setting up a pipeline. A team is working round the clock to fast-track the project. So, he promised we won't have any storage problems. As for the good news, he said since we are a good customer they're going to give us a 25% discount on the price per liter. I'll give you more details when I get back. Best, Idriss

Many years ago, I wanted to buy a marvellous concert grand.  It was the piano of my dreams!  It was a very ancient Steinway so the price was $200,000. In spite of my great disappointment, I called the owner again. I explained to him that since I became a professional pianist, I wanted to play the piano on a Steinway at concerts because I loved its excellent sound quality.  Then,  the old man told me that he has been expecting for a long time to find the right person to sell his lovely piano. I was that person! The piano was mine for $190,000.  Now, I think about this touching man whenever I play the piano.

I have been living in the South West of France since 2000 and I have discovered that each region has its custom which makes it special. So, I'd like to speak about a traditional dance called' La Danse sur Echasses' of that agricultural region where I am living. In ancient times, because it  was a marshy area, when shepherds were guardering their flocks of sheep, they had to use stilts for walking. Thanks to that elevating position of moving, they could take advantage of it. First, even in wetlands, they could easily look after their flocks. Then, they could see over the hills and so far away in the country that they never got lost. Unfortunately,little by littlle,shepherds,herds,woods began to disappear with the development of industrialization. Despite of that, the traditional stilts weren't all-forgotten and  became an important accessory of dancing! Nowadays, if you like traditional folk entertainment, you can attend an open-air show. What an engaging performance! I love watching these kind of dancers who are wearing bright colored costumes . How fascinating they are when they began to dance round in circles with those strange shoes!

The weakness of this book is that it's not very realistic in the context of crisis and difficulties to get a work. But finally, it will help some people to change a difficult work which is boring with another which will be more exciting ! It claims that the search for the perfect career is a lot easier that it seems !

In my office, I was always working very hard and I didn't have any promotion! One day, I needed some advice of other colleague. Her desk was near the director's desk and she was very appreciated by him. She told me I would meet the director to tell him my request. So, I did! I was very satisfied because he received me very well and listened to me with attention. So, he gave me a positive answer. I was very happy and I thanked him for his great help. In the same time, I decided to show better all that I was doing in my work and explain with more and more details to value my goals. It was successful! What wasn't my surprise when he promoted me!

I'm a young entrepreneur. I'm just 27 years old and I've just finished my studies in Management. I love read books, newspapers. Before, I bought so much books in libraries when I wanted to read one. I went to the library with my car and bought the book I needed. And after came back home. It took me a lot of time to get a book or a journal I needed to read. But since I've bought a tablet, I feel like all is now easier. For be informed about the economic situation, the companies situations, or the political situation where I project to invest around the world, I need to get some informations as quickly as possible. Before when I went to the kiosk to get a newspaper or a business magazine, that took me a lot of time. And now with my tablet, I just have to click on an icone in the screen of my tablet and, without taking time to go to the kiosk, I have my journal or my mazagine in my tablet and I can read it immediatly. What took me ten minutes before, takes me now just hum... one minute.%% I enjoy how the media with technology get us the life easier than before.

Antismoking efforts are hard to avoid nowadays in France. Since 5 years, it's not allowed to smoke in a public place. Smoking in a bar, a restaurant is prohibited. If the regulations were violated, smokers faced a fine of 68 euros. From a political point of view, this law had good intentions: to protect all the citizen from an dangerous atmosphere. We breathe better. Non-smokers are happy: they win:"it stinks of tobacco in here" they used to say. It's finished, pubs smell good, an so healthy air that a baby could have his night there. Even when it's raining, smokers goes out, in front of the bar or restaurant to continue to puff on their cigarettes. During the evening, every time we want to smoke, we quit the table, we get dressed again. New laws, new habits. A kind of smoker's brotherhood exists on sidewalks in the night: conviviality is not inside but outside! If you ask me, I love to light up when I have a drink in hand and it's more complicated, less comfortable now. In fact, going out "somewhere" it's to stay up in the street and the cold.

There are a lot of situation which are so awkward in life but if I have to pick one, I would speak about the party we had with Felicie and Pablo. At that time they were still together because now they broke up. It was in the winter time, six people were invited, in fact three couples. We started with appetizer and had a drink. But the situation quickly became awkward. Felicie was everytime very rude with Pablo, telling him very offensive things. For example she criticized the clothes he wears, the movie he likes to watch, the music he enjoys listening and last but not least said horrible things about his physical appearance. The other people, my wife and I had been so embarrassed that we had had just one desire: to escape. We didnt want to be rude with Pablo so we  stayed but it was very difficult to feel comfortable in that kind of situation. Finally, one years later they decided to break up and today I really think that it was the best solution for them. I hope we wont live again a party like that.

Is gender equality exist in France in 2014? I don't think so, in my country, there still are gender differences. Although, times are changed. The most important difference is between men and women's salery. Men win 10% more than women to the same work. I think that, same work, same salery. However, of course, men and women can't doing all the same job. There are physical differences who can't be changed. On the physical side, gender equality is not important because it's a fact, even if there are exceptions, I guess. Nowadays, roles between males and females are shared. The man takes care about his children, as the woman. He cookes too. I think equality can be improve, more over in the minds.

Last year,mid June,  it was a week day, I was wainting for my 8.10 train, for unknown reason this train was concealed a the very last moment. The next one scheduled 10 minutes later, was fully packed. I jumped on it but no more free avaible seat so I stood in the middle of the carriage with no possiblitily to grap me somewhere in case of a sudden tremor. Then with no understanding reason the train stopped suddenly the driver used his brakes fiercely, all of us in the middle felt,many were scared some screamed, others got a fit of giggles the situation was awkward but also funny, anybody was hurt just few bruises, and dust on clothes. Most of the people in the middle of the carriage were teenagers, students. Once up the train started again, the driver apologizes and said through his microphone the problem was because a fire on the tracks. We arrive to the terminus 20 minutes later all in good shape.

text: Hi Carrie, You missed the presentation of John that candidates on the new CEO position and I would like you give you my impressions on his speech. First, he gave a survey on his previous research topics and one thing he did well was to situate his work among the domain of work and compare it with other approaches. I had to admire his pedagogy when presenting a very simple graphic that summarize a non trivial research area. Then he gradually explained the specificity of his work and finally presented his algorithms that seem very innovative. However I have not be convinced by his speaking ability, too familarly most of the time for a future CEO. I also found his presentation too much technical such that most of the audience didn't understand the key points. I would have preferred that he presented how he would manage the institute when coming at the head of the team and how he could help people work together despite their various research themes. He did it at the end but it was so simplistic that he didn't convinced me at all.

Hello Harry, When you travel in the South West of France and arrive to Arcachon, you will be invited for a dinner by a few friends of mine. I understand that you are a trifle puzzled because each country has its own etiquette but don't worry. Staple rules are the same all over the world. Therefore, I just will give you some advices about a few manners which are perhaps specific for French people. First, you must arrive on time, neither earlier, nor later. As you know, a little gift from South Africa will be appreciated by your host; if they have children, don't forget that parents always like when guests have a thought for them. During the dinner, you will be in danger to be surprised by the food because eating habits are different, but don't bother! If your host serve a typical-like meal, for example oysters ( a speciality of Arcachon), he will know that it's really special and tell you that you may eat no one. Nevertheless, if you can, taste them: he will be happy by seeing that you are interested in our local food. In a nutshell, the golden rule consists in traveling being open minded and showing interest toward others.%%

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: I was responsible for the coordination between several teams  for the development of a software. The operational officer presented his requests : a reliable product, with no bugs and which works quickly. They didn't have time to lose because of a lack of IT efficiency. But the accountant warned us about the costs' limits : we had to respect the budget granted for these suppliers ! That's why I proposed to automate only the essential tasks, and to carry out manually all the other works. The CEO and the other members accepted my proposal.

What roles do males and females have in my country, I mean, in France? I think relationships between men and women exist with no contentious, in France. Of course, male chauvinism appears sometimes, but generally, males and females share tasks at home. No need having written rules, nor strict agendas. They are compatible and accept to change their role if it's necessary, but the problem is elsewhere. If you look at a picture of parliment. You can see the women are under-represented. A shame! A lot of improvements would be necessary to obtain an equal representation  males/females in high responsability jobs.

Who could say why there are still so many gender differences in France in 2013? The most visible gender difference is still at work. Most women work today but is is still difficult for them to reach the same level in the hierarchy and to have the same salary for an equal job. Mentality has changed of course. But most women have to take care of their children and it is not easy for them to invest themselves at work as much as men. Nowadays, women have more freedom but as a consequency, there are more and more women who are raising their children alone and are more prone to insecurity. However, which women would like to go back in the 1990s? Today, there are gender equality in the studies and women have much more opportunities than before. Gender differences are decreasing but there are still much room for improvements.

text: I took the floor in a medical congress to present a new organisational method to bring some help to patients. I had fifteen minutes to present 5 slides. I began with a joke about the place where the congress were. Then, I presentes myself and the three points that I was going to talk about : the key issues, how is working this new organisation, and the first results. I told the congressmates to keep their questions for the end of the presentation. After that, I went on with the slides and explain the differents points. I took care with my speed speech, because I knew I use to speak to fast. I pause sometimes after some important issue to be more compelling. After the last slide presenting a chart of the first results, I told they could ask any questions they would now or by mail.

Nowadays, if we want to be succesful, I think we have to work in teams. So the first thing is to build an effective team. How to build it? First we have to look for the good manager. A good manager is a person who can effectively listen to all members, discuss with all of them and bring up the better of each one. He must be impressive and a good communicator. He must analyse each idea and try to incorporate some of them in the company project.  He must keep in touch with all the team members but be careful: too many mails, too many meetings, too many calls can make the members boring. The contacts between each other must be precise, useful and short. . Each team member has different and unique skills. The main role for the manager is to discover what each team member can do. On the other hand the team members must commit to the project. To end, I think the leader must find ways after work to socialize with everyone, to make the members feeling as a family. Why not create team buildings outside the company?

In France, it's a tradition to invite people for a meal or a party. To make a first good impression, people are supposed to bring wine or flowers. You should also wait to eat until your hosts seat and start eating. It's very important, even if it seems obvious, not to speak the mouth full. In my country the meal is very important. For us it's a time where we can share thoughts, discuss with our family, have a good time. So if you or your child get up without saying the reason, or for the children without asking for an autorisation, it could appear very rude for the other guests. And if you want to show how respectful are your children, it's important to make them follow this advice. If you don't like what is in your plate, it's better to say that you can't eat because for example "I'm sorry but I can't digest that" than to say I don't like it. And the better way to please your host, is to eat the meal without saying anything, mostly when you don't like it. Last but not least, smile and enjoy the party.%%

I don't have a lot of  cash but I'm getting by. The biggest part of my monthly expenses is to reimburse the mortgage and the utilities bills for electricity, water, taxes. The second part is for my horse. Indeed, he lives in the stables and I have to pay his rent ! It is expensive enough and it increases faster than the cost of living. The third part is all the outgoings for ma car. I do many kilometers every day for my job. So, I have many big bills from the garage owner ! Finally, I have to pay food and all the stuff for everyday. At the end of the month, I wait with impatience my salary !

text: The increase of technology in our lives this last years has completely changed our way of life: even in a restaurant or during some important event you stay connected and focused on your smartphones, watching the news, sending sms, reading your emails.. There were supposed to allow us to have a more social life, but the fact is that we are now so dependent of this machines and of internet that we are not preoccupied anymore of the real life: meeting new people face to face, enjoying a discussion without looking at your phone every two minutes, find your way without a GPS, reading newspapers instead of an application from a newspaper... We forgot how to stay connected to real people in the real life. At the end in one way technology has helped us, on the other hand, it had make us completely dependant on something we might not be able to control anymore on day.

text: I recently attended a presentation about the urban development in France.The presenter was the former mayor of Dijon, an important city in France. He was an excellent speaker, like most of the politicians. He spoked , without staring at his notes and looked at people around all the time.His opening was excellent. His first joke, wich worked well was tremendous to grab attention and he gave the audience a very clear plan. On the other hand, he did'nt emphasized enough his main ideas. One thing he could have done differently was to release more facts and figures. He remained at a level of generality. If he had given us more examples, more visuals, or more charts, we would have better remember his main ideas.  As he developped the consequences of urban deccay an the positive perspectives of urban renovation, he delivered a boring speech, without real cities of flesh and blood.I'm still not sure to have understood the purpose of his statement. In the conclusion, he failed to summarize her keypoints.I'm not sure we learned a lot.

In my country of origin there still are enormous differences between genders. It is about an ex-communist country with traditional expectations and stereotypes well seated. There is still an important percentage of women staying at home at raising children and men working, taking decisions and being the king of the house. It's already been more than 20 years since the political system changed and things are changing for the better, women are beginning to fight for their independence and they are also starting to have their carrier and feminist expectations. For now women are still being payed less than men, women are still forbidden to occupy men's positions, like engineering or important political positions and I think that we are not going to have a president woman in the next 20 years.

First of all, I have to admire how the presenter outlined our problems, it was really clear, and gave us a complete overview of the companies issues. One other thing he did well was to offer examples for each key point, as well as pie charts, which was extremely helpful to understand exactly were we are. But I am convinced he did a great presentation and gave us all the key points to modify our way to work in the future.

Actually I consider that my life is pretty good: I have a roof over my head, food on the table. I can watch, hear, walk along the street in a free country. However I have a job that makes sense, life can be hard and stressful. Something is definitely missing in our western society: people always complain about their life. More we have everything we need, more our dissatisfaction rise up. The high quality of our life -with all modern conveniences- doesn't seem to be enough to smile more! What a paradox! Perhaps I'm wrong but I just search now to simplify my way of life...I don't dream about more money, I appreciate a hearty meal shared with friends or family more than buy a new car.Feeling of happiness isn't measured by material possessions. My social life isn't hectic and demanding,I hate every kind of obligations. In business it's important to network whenever possible. Fortunately, my lifestyle is far from those problems. What I need is absolutly less stress. And more time. I like the idea of "degrowth", this movement that advocate "voluntary simplicity ".

Currently, I'm going to a computer programming school. But it wasn't always like this. My initial job was banker. Prior the economic crisis, I studied Finances to became a banker. When the crisis came up and bubble burst, I stayed one year searching a job. It was really difficult for me but I tried to stay courageous. Then, I did a call-center job for few months. Subsequently, I went to England and I worked as commis waiter to improve my English. Soon afterward, I found a job as banker at CIC bank and I worked there for 3 years. But unfortunately, Bank changed much during economic crisis and currently i don't like how this job became. I decided to stop my job to follow Computer programming course. Following my school, I hope to be able to work as computer programmer. I'm very passionate about Computer for my youngness and i hope to realize my dream.

text: There was fluctuation between 2004 to 2008 in international book sales.Between 2004-2005 there was a dramatic rise to a figure of 2 million followed by a sharp drop to about 2.75 million.In 2005 book sales fell sharply to about 1 million to a level of 2007 when there was a sharp decrease in sales of about half a million. National sales figures: Between 2004-2005 book sales rose sharply to 1 million. 2005-2006:There was a constant level of sales at 1 million. 2006-2007:sales increased dramatically to around 3 million followed by a sharp drop to one and half million.

Last year, I went with my family in Vende to go at "Puy du Fou". It's a leisure park whose there are many shows in different times. For instance, there are the circus games in antiquity or a spectacle where the vikings attack or again we could visit a french castle in Renaissance times. However, for me the best spectacle is no one of these but it's a show called "Cinscnie". "Cinscnie" is the biggest night show in the world, it last 1h40. There are 1200 actors and dancers with fireworks. This show is very beautiful and this is for all of these reasons that the show is unique.

I was working as a chief financial officer for my company since 5 years and everything was going well. Three months ago, we hired a trainee to help me close my financial year. This day he was in my office and wanted me to explain him how the work goes in our company and how all the staff is. He was sitting in front of me and I was standing up with the door in my back. After explaining all the processes of the work, it's come the time to describe the staff. I was describing our general manager when I noticed that I seemed to be quiet embarrassed. I stopped my description and looked behind me: my boss was just here and was looking at me. He was really very upset. In fact my description was a little bit exaggerated, and I was joking about the way he spoke. I tried to explain it to my boss, and after warning me, he told me that my joke was quiet funny. Finally the issue wasn't as grave as I feared.

I have a really good life. I take care of the people at their home and they seems to be happy. I'm not married and I live in a comfortable house near my parents, so I get to spend time with them. I do not have stress at work and that makes me fell in good health. I haven't time to take summers off. As far as money goes, I occasionally get a little nervous because I have to pay the bill for the house and it's quite expensive. Fortunately, I don't have to live from paycheck to paycheck. The fact is I've got everything I need and more. However, sometimes I fell that someone is missing. You see, I'm single and sometimes I get bored. I wish I had kids but I have to find a girl. Anyway, my social life is rather slow. I wish I had more parties to go on and spend more time in the clubbing. It would be nice if I could meet someone. I do not have mid-life crisis and for me life is good and I'm Learning to enjoy the good things everyday.

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: My Smartphone is my best friend; It and me are inseparable. At 7 o'clock, he said me "wake up". My first reaction is to throw it in the bedroom. But finally I take it and talk to him ! "what about the weather ?" "What are my appointements today ?". Since I bought it, I forgot all phone numbers, all addresses, the subway plan. Since I bought it, I have no pen, no paper in my bag. I need to purchase something ? I note it on my smartphone ! It is my memory ! Until the black day, when a black screen don't awaked me in the morning, when the same black screen has forgot my doctor's appointement and don't say me to buy milk. It the same day that I realized that I need seriously to begin a cure detox. Then my friend and me are separate all day from 9 pm. Until 7 am of course.

My wife and I went recently to a performance art. It was a special music show. It was special for many reasons. First, the performance took place in a barn, in a farm. The atmosphere was cheerful. There were two singers. The first one was a woman. She sang several sings very funny and she was joking between each songs. The second singer was a man. It was unique. He sang songs with differents instruments as trumpet, sax, or guitar. He was changing his dress between each song, to be closer with the kind of song he was singing. That was very funny. He also imitated some well known french singers. He did it very well. We laughed a lot. We have liked very much this performance. It was well worth the money. After this evening, we wanted to go on to others performances. We had stopped to go out until this evening, owing to our schedules very busy.

One of the most fascinating performing art in Paris is the the world renowned Opera ballet corp.It's the heir of the royal ballet but it has its currently form since the Second Empire. Around one hundred dancers leaded by the Etoiles play the most famous clasical ballets, like The Swan Lake or Nutcracker. It's a marvelous experience to see dancer moving on the floor as lightly as a bird. There absolutely nothing else like these women wearing tutus and pointe shoes playing the Pas de deux accompagnied by Tchakovsky's great music. The Marius Petipa's choreography is amazing, the costumes are exquisite, the body language of the dancers is marvelous. If you get in the right mood, you feel being in a dream, you feel even being a part of the dream. It's truly a masterpiece. It's well worth his money and his time. It's an unique testimony to the french culture and ancient european way of life. And the fabulous landmark of the Opera itself makes this unique. Most of people who have never been to a ballet befor want to go back to such a spectacle. But afterward, il they go and see another company, they can be a bit disappointed.%% French dancers are probably unrivaled in the world.

French companies are facing aggressive competition of German firms. In order to deal with this new situation, we need to carry out a strategic analysis. I mean, we should evaluate our strengths and weakness. The first thing is to build an analyst team bringing together employees from all departments of our company. Each one should make a list of issues which seem being relevant and making sense. Our job, as managers, would be to make sure that all the major questions are covered. Then I suggest to appoint one person to be in charge of every main topic. He or her should gather information and ideas in his or her department and summarize it. Once we'll have got back all the surveys, we'll be able to write a guideline on the main issues. This paper will have to be release as broadly as possible. To be effective, it will have to be reviewed frequently. When the entire staff will identify our strengths and weaknesses, it will become more aware of the threats and it will accept more easily to make sacrifices which necessarily includes working harder and longer. From my point of view, this analysis built by the whole staff is the best way to prevent upcoming troubles.%%

On a legal point of view, there remains in France no differences between men and women. They can both play key leading roles in business and politics. But we have to come to terms with the fact that there are fewer women having top management responsabilities. Why ? It's probably due to the weight of history, to an old tradition of women helping the professional life of their husband. But that doesn't explain why, at the same level, women earn in average ten or twenty percent less than their husband. They usually pass the same exams than men, sometimes more brightly. They have got the same skills and are offen very talented.  They are good at running people and business. But they don't get to the top. Today, when you work in politics or business at A Level, you almost never hear chauvinist talks or thaughts. it's more subtle and insidious.The glass ceiling exists, but nobody is able to say what are the true reasons to explain this deplorable mess.

Why there are the differences between the genders? I like the questions. But I prefer the response. Generally, in the history, we have many examples. In any times and in all the country it is a good  subject for the writers, for the scientists, the artists. Women's comportment and men's comportment is not the same. The brains are probably different. But the differences attract, are necessary. The picture is complete, and the result is wonderful. Now, the democracy is on the top, we declare the liberty, we declare the equality  for women and man, but it is really the truth

In France, despite of the rules wich oblige employers to pay women and men equally for the same job, a very big salary diffrences remain. Indeed, men earn nearly 10% more than a women for exactly the same job and assignments despite of the law!! Is it normal and fair ? No it isn't. We, woman have a lot of fights to make before to get the equality. Despite of the law, men are always thinking that we don't deserve the same salary even if we work more !! How hypocrit is it ! What an unfair fact ! We have to fight for our rights and to change minds and habits!

Are women satisfied with their roles in UK society? Not yet. There is still some way to go before there can be true gender equality in the United Kingdom.As recently as 2010, men earned, on average, over 10 percent more than women. Many women complain of a "glass ceiling" in their chosen careers. They question why, simply because of their gender, they can rise only to a certain level, and find the positions above them almost always taken by men. Gender equality is still a big issue in the UK.

One new law has been taken by our government a few years ago concerning the amount of deposit when a person contracts a lease. Since the implementation of this new law, the management cannot ask a deposit that represents more than one month rent. The French government decided to set up this law, since future tenants were usually asked to let a deposit that represents three months of rent. However, a lot of persons were not able to pay such a deposit and they didnt have any access to accommodation. Only persons with a good salary were able to pay a such amount. From a social point of view, I can understand that accommodation is a right for everybody and if management ask for three months rent as a deposit, a lot of persons will not be able to pay and in consequence, the lease will be refused to them. On the other hand, one month rent is very few as a deposit since currently, a lot of persons who leave their accommodation let it in a very bad way and sometimes, the owner has to do a lot of wroks before finding a new tenant.

text: I recently attended a presentation about results and perspectives of the company where I have worked. The presentation was given by the director and all staff were gathered. To summarize he said : " The last years were difficult but the crisis is behind us. Things are turning arround. Results are dramatically better. They have incresed by 20%. The presentation was interesting. But, unfortunatly there were no charts or tables or other visuals to be really compelling. So if he had given some charts, I would have reminded the details of the presentation. At the end of the presentation, a meal was given. It was a good moment.

There is much to talk about my own life. Right now, I'm living well, I'm not stressed and I'm felling healthier because I'm doing sport and I'm losing weight. But I'm especially feeling like this also because I quit my job few months ago. I was very unhappy on my last job. I was bad paid for a very stressful job. And my former boss wasn't awarded me or giving me confidence. However, even with no job I feeling better now. And I have plans for my future. I will start a school, for one year, to change my activity. It gonna be very difficult on the beginning. Going again to school would be hard especially when your last course was seven years ago. But, I hope to get after my training, a well paid job, less stressed, and get opportunities to earn enough to build a family and travel all around the world.

In Cameroun, in this day and age, when you are invited in a dinner we have a lot of rules. Firstly it's a good manner to come with a gift, but not any kind of gift. You have to come with food and maybe something to drink. It's very important and polite. At the end of the dinner, the host has to bring you back your dishes with some food or salt in it. Secondly when you are waiting to begin the dinner, if the host has a whisky to serve, he will open it and put a tear of it on his hands and will shake your hands. This manner means that he is happy to have you in his house. Finally, when you'll arrive in your house you have to call the host to tell him that you are well, and to thank him for the lovely dinner.

I am now 43 years old, probably half time of my life ! I am at the head of the partnerships's department of the university of Montpellier. I have to work hard as I have to manage nearly 30 persons. I can say that there are a lot of work to be done and mostly in a very short time. There is a lot stress in my job. However, I still have a great social life. Many friends, many interets such as cultural events, exhibition, dance and so on. But what I am really keen of is travelling ! such an exiting experience meeting in each trip new people and discovering beautiful landscapes and monuments. Colors of India, colors of Canada at fall ! and many others things. I believe I found a good compromise in my life between work and private life. My job is very demanding but such interesting. It also allows me to get enough money to make all my dreams of travels become true. I am really happy to very good friends I can be confident with. Hope things will still go on that way for a long time !!

Is gender equality still questionable in 2013 in France? According to several researches, women hold more leadership position than in the past but still earn less than men for equal responsibility. Women tends to demonstrate higher skills however they continue being perceived as weaker than men. The maternity leave is generally seen as an opportunity for men to steel a job position without feeling remorse. Even though women have the right now to stand for better job, they are still required to fulfill their housewife duties without ever complaining about the burden of their complex daily life.

If you want create a successful project, there aren't miraculous recipes. Nevertheless, you have better to respect a few rules and to show common sense. First of all, you absolutely must carry out an accurate and in-depth analysis of your needs and to be aware of the kind of skills which are required. That's a first stable step, where making an error is forbidden. After that, try to incorporate individuals who will be able to get along and to collaborate with others, because a teamwork is like a soccer team. Individual skills don't form an efficient team: A good team spirit is the paramount key to success. Plus, you need a leader who heads the team, like a captain, and makes them to be willing to make a commitment to the project. That being said, you must never forget that, in order to be creative, colleagues have to be allowed to freely propose their ideas, with the guarantee that they will not be dismissed. They need to know that they have room for error! Many important discoveries stemmed from an error: Never forget that! After all, regular meetings will enable to coordinate proposals and to make decisions.%%

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: In my studies I used to make many presentations in english and spanish. I remember a presentation in english that I made with a colleague, this presentation was about rugby and british identity. I was in charge of the describing part of the differents rugby teams in Great Britain. I explained the history of each team, the symbols and the anthems. My colleague made the part that describes how rugby can reflect the british identity. Her part was more general than my mine. At the end we concluded that as French people we could believe that rugby has an important place in Great Britain but in reality football is most famous, in fact like in France. For this presentation we had good points, we made great slides with pictures and sound, we spoke clearly without reading our notes but we had also bad points, we didn't have time to review our presentation together and there was a bit too long for our teacher. To conclude this presentation was not perfect but I learnt a lot about rugby and it was the only grade for the course of British Civilization and I passed it.

The CEO concluded in saying that all had to take times to think, and he decided to plan an other meeting in two weeks.

text: The purpose of the meeting, was the financial health of the company. Indeed, we have been encountering some issues since the beginning of the year. There is no performing art that's unique in my city. However, when I was younger, I used to go with my family to the theater. Subsequently, I'm keen on french theater. That makes me relaxed. First of all, in most cases, plays are utterly funny and unpredictable. I remember one of them whose name's "Th ou caf". I was one of the first people who saw this play before it becomes very famous. I was suprised because of the stage actor performance. The acting was unrivaled. Moreover, the plot was credible. Actually, I'm not very interesting in plays with full of holes. I'm just not into it. Fortunately, I've never seen such a play. Other thing, theater is something you can feel, you can live. Not only during the play but also before, when the curtains fall down, and after, when you write your own review. Like I said, it makes me feel relaxed and it's also an opportunity to spend time with my friends or just to have a good night out without having to think.

topic: Finding a home for a wealthy client
text: When my best friend took up skydiving two years ago, I was very surprised because he was utterly terrified of heights since he was a kid. However, he wanted to overcome his phobia which had always prevented him even from taking an elevator! So, one day he decided to take some training in an air tunnel to get over his fear and unexpectedly, he loved that weird experience! As I didn't know that he liked pushing himself to the limit, I was really impressed. Anyway, I have to recognize that he had never struck me as an extreme sports type. But,  when he said to me that the first free fall was such a rush that I would have to try  it even if I am not a thrill-seeker, I was speechless! However I almost immediatly answered to him:'no way!', nowadays we are both members of a skydiving club! Who would have believed that!

One day, when I was in high school, we were having a math class in a classroom which was locked. So, we had to wait because the teacher was always coming late. My friend got an idea and persuaded me to hide inside a locker, and when the second friend who was late would come, he would give me a signal by coughing, so I would jump out of the locker to scare him. Well, my friend was actually an idiot because he told the other friend the plan, and coughed when our science teacher was walking around my hiding place. So, I jumped out of the locker doing "waaah" and nearly crashed into her. She was almost sixty, small and thin as a rake. She didn't even blink, but just gave me a quick cold look, and asked if I was all right with a face saying something like does this often happen to me. I just mumbled that I was sorry and she left.

In France we can be proud of Bouglione Circus. It'shorsebreakers, there are acrobats, there are riders and so on.The totality of the performance is built around horses. They are the only animals. It's an incredible mixed between the music and the acters.  If you love animals as I love, It's a total moment of happiness. I would like to convince you to see this show in Paris if you visit France.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: When I was a director of a social and cultural centre, every month I presented an update to the member of the board of associations and to the social workers who were working in that place.. At first I welcome them and always remember the agenda. Afterwards I asked them if there was a key point they wanted to discuss at the end of the agenda. I used statistical documents like chart and some slides for catching the attention of the audience. At each key points I left them to give their advice, sometimes it was a way to solve problems between two partners. However it was difficult to stop them and following to summarise their ideas. It was essential to do that for creating a team-building. At the end I summarised the key points with the new ideas, and I drew a plan of action for the centre. I think I was too serious and had some difficulties to make them laugh. Also my voice wasn't enough strong and I didn't use microphone, it was an error.

We use to spend a lot of time with other people. Those people can be our family, our colleagues and every person we meet outside at all. What's most important for me is to consider the other like a real person. That is to say, you have to smile, to be polite, to welcome the people you meet. Our society doesn't make ourselves be generous with others : life is hectic, for a lot of people making money is the main activity in life, people have difficulties to dicuss without shouting in general. Every day, I try to do a good action, that's my involvement to change that situation. People must accept the other. People must discover the other. People must consider the other. Like it was said during that lesson, life is short and we can't waste time. Be happy and feel free depend on how we consider the others.

text: French is my mother tongue. I remember when I was younger, I didn't understand anything in english. I said : "one day english language will be my second language!" In France, school try to initiate english tongue at the beginning of school. But it is not interesting and when you are young you don't realize this language is mandatory to have a better life for an international job or spend times with friends comes from all around the word. I recently moved in Australia and I spoke really fluently english for looking for a job and to be more sociable with australian people. But I knew that I will not understand all what they said. That's why , when I came back to France last summer , I joined "englishtown" to be better and make english my second "native" language.

At this time, I'm unemployed. I was fired 3 months ago, it was an economical redundancy. Of course, my incomes decreased a lot, almost I had another big issue. I earned monthly rentals from my tenant but since the end of last year, they don't pay the rent because they are broke. So, now, I have to pay off my monthly mortgage, the rent of my apartment and, in the same times, my incomes decrease about 50 %. Fortunately, i made some savings last years, so I'm in a good shape yet. Actually, I'm in touch with few companies for the position of Financial Manager. I hope that things are turning around and looking up soon.

Here in France, there are still a lot of manners to respect when you're are dinning. For instance, never put your elbows on the table, don't speak when you're mouth is full, use the proper cutlery,... The guests usually bring a little gift to the host, they don't come before the time they were expected to arrive because the host could be embarassed if he's not ready. But don't be late too. It's no use saying that speaking on the phone while eating is very impolite. Before drinking, you have to clean your lips, so you won't left marks of your mouth on the glass. Furthemore, the host would be happy if it doesn't remain any food on your plate. In fact, it means that you enjoyed your meal. Anoter advice, when you are discussing, don't be the only one who is talking, let other guests or the host to give their point of view.

In France, politness is very important, more than other good manners. Although people on street are extremely rude, you should remain polite. I will give you some of these habits. First and foremost, you have to greet all the time. For instance I am used to saying "Bonjour" to old people I run into on the street, even if I do not know them. Besides, people love greet each other, when they belong to the same group, community. The most striking example are the bus drivers. They all the day salute each other, just a hand sign, even though it is the five time they bump into. The other habit which I have notice is to say "please" and "thank you". Even if you give back a book, its owner will thank you. I haven't understood this usage yet and that still amazes me. What shocks me the most is the answer when people thank you, you can answer "c'est moi" which means "it is me who thank you" but for what?? Finally, if you use bus to move in the city, you have to know a frech rule. People have to get on the bus by the front of the vehicule and get off the the back door.%% With these three rules, you will look like respectful.

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: For sure not everyone is born to become a good leader: leadership is something you have inside you and can be seen very early by looking at the child's behaviors but it is also something you can learn and improve yourself by looking at your own manager. You need to have a strong self-confidence, because if you believe in yourself, in your capacities, people will believe in you and trust you. Which is the most important part. You also have to be able to express yourself and your ideas in front of a large audience, develop your charisma and the way you talk. Some people can't just do that, it doesn't mean they are not good on what they do of course. Be able to detect the strengths and weakness of your team, of your project and your own is also very important: don't underestimate yourself and people around you.

I try to make savings monthly but it's really difficult. In France, taxes has been dramatically increased since our new President's election. After paying my rent, I have to pay my nany (who takes care of my daughter every evening after school), the taxes, the utilities such as electricity, gasoil and my car's insurance, I don't have any extra money for leisure time. I have to pay englishtown as well and it is a very big monthly expense for me but I know that is very important to speak English nowadays to get a well-paid job. That's why I decided to invest in Learning this language. Fortunately, I have a job and hope that I won't loose it. To sum up, it's not really easy to make savings above all when you have a child. But I need to try because it's really important to make savings to cope with unemployment or other bad days.

In France, there are not currently one specific role for men and an other one for women. Usually, both are working, cleaning the house, raising children. But you can also find, old-fashionned family where wives stay at home and men go to work. Roles have been changing since the 70's when the MLF (Liberty Women Movement) was building. Feminists asked for equality between men and woman. They thought women have to be paid the same salary as a man; Men can also do the domestic work and raise children. They also fought for important women laws like contraception and abortion. As a feminist, I do think that equality between women and men is important. Each couple can decide how they want to live. In fact, a lot of men like to be at home with children, cooking and keeping clean the house. Mentality don't have entirely changed but we are in the good way.

Hello, I am from Madagascar and would like to tell you about manners and etiquette in my country. Since Madagascar has been a long time a french colony, our etiquette is similar to the french one. This is the case for example for the etiquette at table: You should wait for the signal of the hostess before beginning to eat. You should put the napkin on your lap while eating and put it at the left side of your plate, when you have finished. It is very impolite to talk with your mouth full or to phone at table. To bring a little gift for the host family would be kind. It is not necessary to send them a thank-you message after the party. Nevertheless, we also have traditional customs concerning New Year's greetings for example. The big family (uncles, aunts, cousins etc.) meet for a party in the house of the oldest person of the family, to whom they give some money and their best wishes for the New Year. So, if one day you have the occasion to go to Madagascar, I wish you a very nice stay there and hope you will quickly be conversant with our etiquette.%%

If you want to show up, do a job, collect a check, and go home with nobody noticing you for the rest of your life, then you should do everything you can to avoid office politics. Otherwise, if you have any ambition, you will need to learn how to deal with the inevitable power and respect struggles that occur in any social business environment. When there are money and reputation on the line, you can either be engaged or be a bystander. It's a fact of life. No matter how you try to hide, office politics will find you. Using office politics to get ahead of the game, doesn't mean making unethical decisions. In my opinion, you don't have to be manipulative, abusive or corrupt to get your promotion. You can use office politics positively by establishing effective relationships, building a good personal reputation, being fair to everyone no matter how small or big their roles in the company are, and influencing people positively. To put it in a nutshell, don't keep your head in the sand if you want to climb the corporate ladder.

I had been looking for jobs when my father suggested that I volunteer at a political organisation. What a fantatic experience! If only I had done that sooner, I would have realized that volunteering is a great way to network and meet peiple. Soon afterward, I had interviews at several companies whose employees had also volunteered. Following the interviews, I was offered a job with a large homebuilder. I was always determined to work in the real estate industry in some way, sharpe or form. I gladly took the and have remained passionate about my work ever since, although, frankly, things were difficult in the yeras after the buble burst. For the last five years, I have been negotiating purchase contracts at a successful real estate development company in the valley. As soon as the picks up enough, I hope to open my own development company.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: I attended a meeting the previous week. The points on the agenda were to talk about a possible merger of the company with one another, which was in another town. The CEO started by saying we should arrange a meeting with the board of our partners, in order to discuss about changes and such subjects. The CFO disagreed and suggested we should see with our executives the exact situation of our budget before. The COO argued we were running out of time, so it wasn't possible.They finally agree to arrange a meeting this week.

I advise you to observe all the other invitees and to copy their behaviour, I think that it the best way to learn and to avoid mistakes. First, turn your mobile off, it's very unpleasant to hear a ringing at the table and to interrupt the discussion. When you pour wine, don't fill fully your glass, you have to taste it first. Don't speak with food in the mouth, don't put your elbows on the table, don't drink too fast... You may bring for instance a bottle of wine, your host could sometimes open it during the meal to share and taste it, creating a conviviality moment and a conversation topic. Avoid not to let anyone else speak, but on an other side, take a part in the discussion, otherwise, people will believe that you aren't interested in the conversation topic. Don't forget to congratulate your host about the quality of the meal, it's always pleasant !

Many differences stay between male and female genders in my country. Many women work but they do also much more housework than men ! They always do ironing, shopping, laundry and other chores. They also have to educate children, particularly when they are babies or young children. But who knows ? Maybe someday men and women will have the same role in their house ? In fact, things evolve slowly. More and more men like to educate and look after their children and more an more men do house chores. But there is one place where the equality doesn't evolve enough, it is the world of work. Women earn 20% less than men and they often can't have jobs as important as men. However several laws to reduce this inequality have been voted. Why does nothing move in spite of laws ?

In my country, these days, men and women are equal. The women's movement of the 1970s has stopped definitively the "old" roles males and females. If I think it was necessary to fight for women's rights, time has really changed. It's not a shame to say, as a woman, I "just" want take care of my family. I'm leaving in Paris, which is well known all around the world for its Art life: we have a lot of different perfomers: you can see artists performing on the streets, everyday, for free, demonstrating their talents: singers, breakdancers, painters, musicians: you can see them in the most touristic areas such as Champs Elysees, but also in the subway! Our city is also famous for its "french can-can" cabarets such as "Moulin Rouge" or "Lido" or "Crazy Horse" (last one is much more sexy and less traditional by the way). You can see a show that lasts 2 hours with the most beautiful and talented dancers, not all of them are french: a lot if dancers come from Eastern Europe.. . But the show is french and is made essentially for the tourists, parisians often go in small theaters, which are less expensive!

I have took my driving lessons for more than 1 year now. I have spent 3.000 for it. I regret to have spend this amount on it, because I don't care about driving. One of my priorities is to moving for Australia. But I am not broke. Actually, I have enough savings to plan my trip for 1 year. Obviously, I am glad I have found some jobs for almost 4 years because my parents couldn't help me. I was not comfortable about borrow them some money, so I decided to work when I get 18. We have some troubles but we have a roof over our head and food on table. I believe that in few years, probably two or three, it will be better for us. My mother will not have her credit anymore, and we would probably be able to consume as before.

Since few years  economy crise rise in europe . Many people have lost  job and it's difficul for them. The bottom dropped out of Housing market and i lost my work as an achitect. I am unemployed .  Many Compagnies were shut down in the pass year. Fortunately my husband have a good salary. So we"re Lucky to have a roof over our heads and food on the table for children. But we don't increase our saving at the moment because the government  go up taxes. Many people couldn't pay taxes because  income fall dramatically and  somebody are oblige to abandon  houses. Infortunately thing don't seem to be turning around in France !

I have the chance to live in Paris and to have access to a very large range of cultural events. In my neighborhood, I have a famous concert hall that I discovered quite recently although I am living here since 10 years. I have very eclectic tastes in terms of music like pop-rock, metal, RnB or classic. The first time I went to this concert hall was quite by chance, I had a ticket given by a friend. What I felt during this classic concert was absolutely unbelievable! First, you have the ambiance of a large and impressive place, then the imposing presence of the orchestra and finally the power of the music that brings you in a disconnected world for 2 hours. From the first notes I had goosebumps, I was literally fascinated, time was suspended to the piano chords. I can't wait to go to the Beethoven concert tomorrow, even if I am currently listening Metallica!

In my country (Morocco), there is a law that imply that arab couples cannot be able to make a reservation in a hotel without the act of wedding. On the other hand, all western tourists can do it, and no matter if this tourist is with her girlfriend or a prostitute. One day, I was with my girlfriend on vacation and we were looking for a place where to sleep, it was very difficult because we are arabs and muslims. When you ask the hotel manager, he explain that the law is that way because of religious aspect : In Islam it's a sin to have a girlfriend and to sleep with her before getting married, so that's why we are not allowed to get a hotelroom reservation. Also, he said that we apply the law in each case, we are muslim, so we must respect the law of arab countries, and if you are european for example, then we will apply laws of your country and this in Morocco. They believe that if they apply islamic laws to the tourists, the economy will decrease swiftly. I can understand this point of view, wich is purely economic, but I think that we are a little bit hypocrite in Morocco, we have to whether seperate religion from the state or totally apply the islamic laws for every one.%%

text: I think that certain people have since their birth a natural leadership. We can see it in the playground of our schools when we see a kid who lead others, who decide le game they play or a new trend followed by other kids. But even if you don't have this natural capacity, you can learn or get it with the time. In general, a leader get certain qualities regarding to a "normal" individual: Natural qualities like humility, coherence, or courage; People skills like a good communication with others or capacity to have an opened mind; Professional skills like adaptability face to the change, an experience or an expertise on a domain. Personally the ones leaders who impressed me are those who are able to manage the big changes. They seem be not stressed when a person like me would be afraid.

Dear Mr Malcolm McDonald,  I am writing with regard to the breach of my contract by TransWorld Enterprises.  As you know, I worked during the two last weekends and the company has refused to pay for this overtime.  After having given it much thought and as for my lawyer's advice, I am considering pursuing this matter to the courts. Furthermore, I am planning to take the moral high ground.  As a result of the company objection to pay my due, I am asking for a financial compensation.  I reaffirm here my will to reach an agreement out of court. If the firm keeps not taking my claims into account, therefore I will be within my rights to institue legal proceedings against TransWorld Enterprises.  I look forward to hearing from you.  Yours sincerely.

The most important thing to me in life is my family. That's where I came from and where I belong to. I always concern about their health and hapiness. I'm from South Korea and I'm actually living in France. It's been about five years since I started living alone. In the beginning, both my family and me experienced some difficulties seperating from each other. Besides, just before I came to France, I have had a serious agoraphobia. One day, I decided to change my life and start a new life in a new country. That's why I came to France. It was a challenge and I was afraid at first, but now, I think I made a great decision. My family encourage me and trust me. I'm doing well alone thanks for this support. If I hadn't challenged to start from zero, I wouldn't be changed. Trust yourself in order to make a real difference in your life! Go for it!

Is the gender equality achived in France? Note sure. All the industrialized countries have seen many women reaching leading roles in business and politics but it have been a long and hard road to achieve this. Women are increasingly represented in the most importants roles of our society but most of them still do not perceive the benefits of their performance equally to men. In 2010, french men earned 19 percent more than women. In politic, France is very far behind other european countries with a bad average of 19% of women elected in national assembly. Unfortunately, only the poverty and unemployment seem to not be sexists since it is the two areas in wich women are equal to men in France.

text: Hi Christina, I'm going to review the presentation John did yesterday. The opening was perfect, the topic very clearly exposed, although it was very difficult, as you know. text: There's no denying that technology has made our lives easier in many respects. Over the past 20 years we have seen amazing accomplishments. For my part, my smartphone has definitely changed the way I live my life. There is not one thing my smartphone can't do for me. I can stay in touch with my friends through text messages, search the web, Skype, update my Facebook status, take pictures, write notes, read some media articles, check my heart rate, pay my bills and of course make phone calls which is its main function. I'm sure there is more I can add to this list. My phone is always on me because it has such a great impact on my life. I've become depedent on it. To be honest, I would have never thought that my daily grind would be handled by the small device I keep in my pocket.

I think , I have every thing I ever wanted. Good job, a lovely house in the suburbs surounded by a large flower garden and 2 charming kids a boy and a girl. What a good life I have. No real money worries, except the repayment of my house that I have to pay every month. But sometimes when weather is not good or when I am not in a good mood,  I am thinking about making a change. What can I change? Nothing my life is not totally perfect, but I feel happy so far. Few years ago, I got a mid life crisis, when I was obliged to change job, I got a stress for six months, time for fixing me in my new company. Now I work in this company for a 15 years, I got the chance to work one day a week off site. That's wonderful I appreciate this arrangement which gives me more free time for taking care of my children.

Sometimes I wonder why I chose this job. I'm an account for a small company. It's such a boring job. In fact, everyday it's the same routine, I have to make the same tasks. Contrary to me, my wife has the job she always dreamed of since she was a little girl. She is a teacher. But, fortunately, I have many things to be proud of. I have a family, I have bought a big house, and so on... Overall, I don't have to complain, but we should admit that we spent a lot of times at work (roughly 45 hours a week), so it's important to have a job which makes us feel satisfied. My family and I don't have to live from paycheck to paycheck, and we get the most important things we wanted. Finally, I think I should focus less on what I do for a living, and enjoy my free time.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: Last week, I attended a presentation about new phonetic technology. The presenter was one of my professor. Her voice was clear enough. By the way, one thing she could have done differently was speak more slowly. I would have understood better if she'd spoken slowly. I have to admire how she organized her presentation. First, she started by give an outline at the beginning. Her opening was compelling. Second, she emphasized key points so that everyone could understand important ideas. Third, she used visual and audio aids. These made us more interested by important ideas. Especially, I really liked the video which showed the demonstration of the machine. Finally, she concluded summarizing each key point. It was too bad that we couldn't have enough time to ask questions. If she'd left more time, we would have asked questions. Since the subject was interesting, there were many questions. Overall, I learned a lot from this presentation. I think she prepared very well.

Is 40 years old ? From my point of view it's not, but I just have to look backward and to talk about my childhood with some kids and suddenly I feel like a dinosaur. Quite everything now is same as in the world I use to live in when I was a kid ; quite everything ? Except perhaps the media. As far as media is concerned, everything has changed several times in the last four decades. Growing up, I was listening music on records ; then appeared tapes, and after CD's, and now most of the time we just have to download it from the Internet. In the '70s, the only screen was the black andamp; white one of the TV. Now, our world is full of screens : computers, tablets, smartphone, and soon even on glasses or watches ; we can find screens everywhere. 40 years ago, we were connected to our family, friends,neighbors and colleagues ; now we are connected to the World. That change has a name : it's called Media !

The model of society as we know it is doomed to disappear. The classic scheme of life was to work hard, earn a living, to borrow, consume and retire living. There is an urgent need to personal finances on hand. We are on the cusp of profound socio-economic changes which Westerners States and their citizens will face. These changes necessarily lead to new behaviors to adopt, especially in terms of money. The latest polls show a strong appetite for French to understand financial issues. It must be said that the impact of the crisis of sovereigh debt on their daily life are many : economic slowdown, obstacle to increasing purchasing power, rising unemployment, austerity...

I've been working for 20 years. Therefore I can say that I'm now  able to easily identify office politics. Initially I was the one to pretend that office politics are not a game to be played. I was rather considering that our abilities are essential to get promoted. Now my opinion has been changed. I definitely consider that life at work is a game that everyone should play. Otherwise you will never be integrated into your company. Basicaly everyone should consider that there is time every day to talk with colleagues in order to improve our relationship. By doing this, we will be more appreciated by our colleagues who can advocate us once it's necessary. But for me it's an effort as it's not part of my personality. Like many people I tend to only talk to  persons I like working with. But it's a fact that everyone needs to be a " team player ". It's more an more one of the first crieria we take into account when we assess people ( yearly people review). I'd like to focus on one important point as well. Our respective bosses are all overloaded and once something's happening, negatively impacting results of our company for insteance, they like to consider comments brought by some people in the team.%% Therefore we need to have someone willing to advocate us when our name is put on the table.

I've been working for 20 years. Therefore I can say that I'm now  able to easily identify office politics. Initially I was the one to pretend that office politics are not a game to be played. I was rather considering that our abilities are essential to get promoted. Now my opinion has been changed. I definitely consider that life at work is a game that everyone should play. Otherwise you will never be integrated into your company. Basicaly everyone should consider that there is time every day to talk with colleagues in order to improve our relationship. I have read one of the abundant self-help books published about the question of career paths, and I deem it's one of the best. First and foremost, it's very motivational because it will get you to realize that, if you're unsatisfied with your job, you can change, however old you are, and it's worth doing it, rather than being bored at work every day. Thanks to this book, you will no longer stalemate and you will learn how breaking the deadlock. Secondly, it suggests some easy questions which will help you to find what kind of job matches with your personality and your wishes. However, it's easier said than done and I think that the author's optimism is a little bit far-fetched. According to your age, your diplomas, your sector of activity, it's more or less hazardous and you must reflect well in order to avoid a blunder!%%

Presently , the financial situation of the most of French families are dropping out. The factors are the increase of the cost of living while the number of umemployed people is increasing . The families are seing the taxes going up while the benefits provided by their companies in reduction. If the things are not turning around many families won't be able to pay off their mortgage and bank loans. Even if it's difficult to be optimistic and if the bottom seems to fall out  we have to keep in mind that the most important is to have a roof over our head and food on the table but we have to hope that the situation will look up for the new generation .

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: I'm very passionate about new technology. Indeed, for 2 years now, I have a beautiful smart phone that I use few times in a day. I can tell that it replace totally my laptop. indeed, I use it to check my two counts of emails, one personal and one professional, reading some news thanks to certain apps, surfing my social networks like Facebook or Twitter, using the GPS or yet the camera. 5 years ago, I wouldn't have thought that a phone could do so many things. Now, I read every day news about the advancement of technology about smart phone because every day you have a new app or a new device who make your life easier. I'm afraid I'm become a sort of geek.

When I was young, we were dreaming of equality between men and women. Our values were love and sharing friendly relationnship betwen us, whatever our gender was. Now, in France, I'm observing real differences. Beyond the fact that our salaries (of us, women) is lower than this of men, why do young girls spend so much time to try to be a perfect picture of dreamy woman? Why women can't be as natural as the men in their everyday life? Why are we supposed to be beautiful, efficient, gentle, and everything a man can dream? At the same time, a lot of women are acting like men: do they imagine to be more integrated by avoiding their women sides? Personnaly, I've been trying to deal with these questions since my birthday! How be as free as a man (my brother for example)?

Paris is well known for its theaters. In a way it is the capital of cinema. Going to see a movie, an old one, or a new one is very easy. It is quite different in other French cities, as well as in other countries. Latin Quarter is the district I prefer because of its Champollion street: three theaters close together, and another one on the opposite street. You will always find what you are looking for. The biggest problem is to find time to see movies either at their coming out, or in different festivals (by issue, director, actors, period, country...). I wish I had been a usherette, except that they missed the beginning or the end of the movie, let alone their salary and, long ago, the commercial part of their job, which was selling ice-creams and sweets. In the States, you can see movies on TV but they are interrupted several times and it is just awful. A crime against art, authors and audience .

Five years ago, I met my girlfriend. She was 15 and I was 16, and her parents didn't let her go out a lot. One night, there was a big argument between my girlfriend and her father and she makes believe that she will go to sleep at one of her friend home and came to see me. During the evening, my girlfriend's cellphone rang. It was her parents.They had discovered she wasn't at her friend home and were worried. At this moment, her father took the phone and say to come back home. He was really, really ungry. It was awful! But the worse came when she told she was with her boyfriend..! After this call, we went at her home : it was the first time and met them... When we arrive, her mother opened the door, her father waited us in the livingroom. He wore a dark suit and was always really ungry. I wondered if I would survive to this appointment... We entered, took a seat on the sofa, and her father started talking to me, eyes in eyes, I was like frozen, I cannot move. Finally, I talked as sincerely as possible and, the huge tornado slowdown.%% Now, they parents just like me! But, when I thought about it, what an awkward situation!

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: After asking if everybody was in possession of the agenda, Joan explained that the purpose of the meeting was to talk about the merger between the company and Sunset. She also informed the participants of the resignation of two of the main executives. From her counterparts' feedback, she pointed that the main problem concerned communication between the two companies. The solution that emerged constitues in a team building. After explaining that she will have to travel back and forth and asking to be informed of every issue, Joan said that she'll come later with a follow-up of this idea.

In life, nothing interesting happens to those who stay sat with crossed arms. If you want have an rewarding life, you have to be active and hardworking. But, if it is necessary, it is not sufficient. You have to be persevering and deaf to unkind criticisms. Do not let anyone destroy your dream. It is not easy, especially when it comes from people who have a big authority (their points of view seem to be indisputable). Anyway, as I often say : 'keep going and NEVER quit'. And to finish, I want to quote Seneque (a brilliant antic philosopher) with one of his famous maxims: 'non quia difficilia sunt non audemus, sed quia non audemus difficilia sunt', which means 'it is not because things are difficult that we do not dare, but it is because we do not dare that things are difficult'. Or in very short: be ambitious!

text: I can remember that at the beginning of the meeting the main items were about the problems the Sunset makes face. Some solutions were adressed like the team building and a possible merger in order to increase the competitiveness of the company. They were also talking about replacements of some executives of the company. A lot of subjects were adressed like the budget, the merger, the deadlines and the rewards they could apply for the employees. The decision about the merger has been long to take, but it has seen the day, finally. As they talked about it seems that it was a smart and reasonnable move. Let's finish with the replacement of the CEO. The woman who was in charge of the meeting told her team she turned down the offer for that job Offer which was proposed by the board of directors.

Who knows ? Maybe someday we'll have gender equality . Next weekend we will vote for our mayors. In Paris, the two main candidates for mayor are women. That will be the first time that we will have a woman as a mayor. Last year the parlament voted a law to improve equality in politics. Women must be represented. Perhaps, was it the first step to achieve this equality. In many companies, men still earn ten percent more than women. Shouldn't women get equal pay for equal work? I am working as a teacher in a primary school and there are not so many men with this position. There should have been more men as teachers for young pupils. I admit that this job is not well paid, but it would be good for our pupils.

I am currently a student in a Business School and I want to become a CPA. Prior to that, I was studying accountancy at university for four years. After that I started working in an energy firm as Financial controller but all this wasn't enough to get the right to sit for CPA examination. So I have to go back again at school and spend one year Learning businness and audit. I hope by the end of the year I will obtain the right to sit or the exam. After passing my exams, I will have the rignht to apply in audit firms and works as auditor. Maybe I'll move to United States and get a good job. I hope in five years I'll be able to move in suburbs and live quietly with my family. But now I have to work and work hard.
