topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: When I started to learn English in school, it seemed to be totally useless for me. At that time, I didn't understood that the world was changing to become a global one and that I would later have to communicate with people coming from everywhere. That's the reason why, when I left school, I was fairly bad in English. But, starting my professional career,  I quickly understood that being proficient in English was now a mandatory. I was 25 years old when I joined the team of a museum as salesperson. There, almost thirty per cent of the customers were foreigners and since English is one of the most popular language in the world I immediately understood that I would have to use it not only with the British, Canadians and Americans customers but with most of the foreigners as well. English became step by step, not only necessary in my work, but in my daily life as well. When I turned 27, I joined a NGO working in Asia as volunteer. English was absolutely necessary to communicate with the people we were helping and since some of them became my friends I began to use English in a daily way.%%

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: Even if English has been incorporated into world culture, in my french life I don't use it every day. I fully intend to succeed to improve my level. Actually, I'm not very interested in the utilitarian side of a globish language. I read news of the world online.  How to understand the second degree, the intricacies of a language? In our society, to make more money, we need obviously to use English. Even it becomes an inevitable way to communicate easily with the major part of the earth,for commercial reason or ordinary conversation, I'm still not feel comfortable with the idea of a nibbling language that slowly makes disappear other languages. To conclude, I guess I will improve my skills because of the "natural" evolution of English in our world but what I really dream is an access to open mind and more circulation of various ideas, with accurate thoughts...

Last summer, a new law was approved in France. The marriage between two men or two women is now allowed. During all the process to the approval, the country had been being in trouble. This law was an engagement that our new Prsident took during the campaign before his election in spring of 2012. Hadn't this law been wroten, the president would have been considered like a lyer by all the peoples who had voted for him. But on the contrary, preparing that law had launched a great movement : "The family for all". Every sunday before the vote of the law, there have been giant meetings in each region. More than a million of peoples meet to defend the princip of the traditionnal family. I personnally think it's an important debate and we didn't have enough time to discuss. Idon't know if I agree with this law or not. I think that those persons must have the same rights than the others but I think too that the marriage represents more than a Right. Into the wild, to give life requires a male and a female. I hope that the following of this law is not to change this statement.%%

Several things are important for me : which is the most important for me is to be happy, and so to be healthy because it is a condition to be happy. I try to think about it all the time in order to maximize my life but it isn't easy at all because I often forget it when problems happen. What is important too for me is to be outdoor and in a quiet place to not be too much bothered by some people. I would like to do a job which pleases me because I think it is very important to not be depressed by an boring, tiring or stressing job. I believe that the most important things are not the same for all people but that being happy is certainly a goal for many people.

I had been studying accounting for 5 years at University. I've just finished my studies and currently, I'm looking for a part-time job. Previsouly, I lived with my parents. My plans for the future ? I'd like to be sales executive. That's the reason why I'm going to be studying an MBA in September. Afterwards, I'll be able to apply almost everywhere, and I shouldn't have any problem to be hired. Ideally, I'd earn more than 100,000$ a year and spend my time abroad. Frankly, I don't want to live a quiet life. I'd rather have stress and be busy. Actually, the economy hasn't affected my career and I hope it won't. Anyway, I'll have a fallback career. Everybody needs an accountant. However, I'm goal-oriented not to go back into this borring job, if I may say so. Prior to working in the business community, I have to learn, to speak a fluent English, and to become a thick-skinned!

I am a lucky man. I get a very good job that I love and my wife found her dream job "public relation" on a big company. I very often outside and fare from my family, and wife work lately, but it's the price to pay to live your passion. My wife, my daughter, and myself are all in good health and we have got enough money to pay off us loan and get a food on the table. I do not go to paycheck to paycheck but we are not owner yet. We are looking for a piece of earth to build a modern house. Of course, we have to take care about charges and expenses, because life cost rise steadly . I have a certain amount of stress from expense, but as far as money goes we make projects. I know that we have exactly what always wanted, and we realize how we are lucky.

The competition being hard , the customers requiring more and more the upper management of the most companies must take in consideration external and internal threaths. The main external threaths are competition and economical environment, the main internal threaths are : the lack of trust and motivation from the employees ; a bad organisation and communication within the company. The internal threaths can be real weaknesses for the company and affect the expansion, competitivity and profits. Nowadays some solutions are put in place to resolve this kind of weaknesses. Brainstorming and team building are the most current methods used to improve communication and trust inside of the company. Also it's really important for the managers to encourage the motivation and commitment of the employees. Involving people into a project it's a critical success factor and requires to lead interesting meetings, to discuss and listen ideas.  In conclusion any company must consider their employees like a first asset, must promote a corporate culture. The key of the success is the communication and consideration between individuals.

text: The CEO has decided that there will not have any merge between the compagny. However the CFO was totally disagree about the decision of CEO. The explanation of CFO was that a merge could improve the financial situation or our compagny. Also to win any customers and improve innovation. The board agreed with the CEO. The solution for the CEO is firing any employees to stay competitive. But the CFO expect to see a great strike and having any complains from customers. The trade union will be strong: said the COO. The board decided to shedule an other meeting tu study the possibility having other possibilities to reduce the costs making.

As for me and my husband, both retired, we must consider us as very lucky people. I am worrying about my son. He decided to buy a flat, which was a rather good idea at a moment when the housing market dropped off. The problem is that when you buy something you have to sell an other one, and the dropping out is the same in both situations. So, we had to help him. It was a pleasure to lend him money even if I am not sure at all that he will be able to pay us back. An other issue is that his 'partner' (in fact the mother of their little child) earns much more money than him. Being a civil servant, she has an employment security which is not his.

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: In this report I will talk about two mobiles devices that has affected my life, my new laptop and smartphone.First of all, let's talk about my new laptop that I received at Christmas a few days ago. I really liked it is small and light with a touch screen. I really enjoyed using it at the moment even though I am not quite familiar with the new version of Microsoft. As regards to my smartphone, which I have since a more long time it really helps me to be connect all the time. Thus, I can check immeadiately all my e-mails, or also news feed on Facebook. I guess we are just in a generation, where the need to be connect at any hour of the day is important in order to not miss any gossips.

We are going to adopt in my country a very strange law relating to the right to abort. This right was granted to women in 1976 provided their pregnancy was less than 10 weeks . Nothing else was required and in particular the position that the father may have has not been taken into consideration. It is a right that women have Some associations since 1976 have raised relevant issues following the implementation of this law . Among others the fact that the number of abortions was supposed to decrease while it remains stable at 200 000 per year or the need to properly inform those who have decided to abort about all the aspects of an abortion. Now our new government wants to adopt a law that will forbid any kind of information which could be considered as 'against abortion' , typically some websites , family advisors or even priests . Apparently authorities are fearing a new position of a significant part of the population who consider that we have gone to far as basically we can do almost anything that we want with the 1976 law and its subsequent amendments. It looks pretty strange to face such a situation in a country that claims that it is the country of the human rights and freedom of expression !%%!

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: Two days ago I participated to one of the worst presentations I've ever seen. One of my colleagues gave us a presentation about one of the strategic projects for next year. First of all he didn't made any introduction and he didn't had a map of his presentation. This is why all the audience didn't understand at the beginning what he was talking about. When he started to detail the gains and the market share he did had some very compelling pie charts and I liked this because for me was very clear how we should approach this project. He also summarized the key points but his finish was very bad structured. He forgot to save some time for the questions and no spectacular information were shared. However, I think that we all understood that we where there to share a very important project for our business continuity.

In France, when you go to a dinner party. The good manners are : Don't arrive in time, you have to have 10 or 15 minutes late. Bringing a gift to the host like a bottle of wine or a bouquet of flowers. Then, at the table, men are placed between two women. Normally, men have to take care of her neighbor and make the conversation with her. The napkin is putted down on lap. Don't elbows on the table. Forks are on the left of the plate and knives are in other side. We don't drink before to eat and this is the host who will invite to begin. After that, guests serve by themselves but men are in charge to serve women just as to fill their glasses. Next, we don't talk with the mouth full and we don't cut his salad with a knife, neither his omelette, nor his pasta.

I graduated from college with a business administration diploma. I started as chief accountant assistant in the food processing industry and afterwards I was given the opportunity to move to the IT department as a project leader to implement a general ledger. After one year and a half, I was offered a new job in the controlling department. First of all, I was responsible for division controlling for the catering products and then I moved to the retail division.I really loved this job as I worked with the marketing departments and the key accounts as well. After 11 years of a fruitful experience in the same company, I decided to look for another job and I was recruited as a planning and control manager in the hygiene industry. I managed a team of 4 people and reviewed results with finance and divisions.I also supervised forecasts and budgets. Five years later, there was another job opportunity and I started as financial controller for a cluster of five countries. This a very exciting job where I used to work with local controllers and division managers as well. Unfortunately, due to bad results in Europe, the company decided to suppress my job and I had to leave the company end of 2011.%% Since then, I have not found an unlimited term contract yet but only short term contracts jobs in the pharmaceutical industry.

Are the roles of males and females the same nowadays? Even if there have been a lot of evolution since the early 1900's, I think that there is still inequality between women and men in France.  Regarding workplace, do you really think men and women are equal? Well you wish. Men still in majority have better job with more responsibility and earn more money than women. However, this tend to change with time. We observe more and more big companies' CEO as women. Yet in 1900's, women didn't go to school or didn't have a degree as high as men because they used to have children earlier. In 2013, women are more focused in their career so they spend more time at school and have kids later.  At home, the roles have changed. More women work full time as their husband. Do you remember how it was before WWII? Women stayed at home taking care of their children.  Do you think women are happy with this evolution. Probably yes, but it needs to be improved still.

text: When I was young I loved horses, and I took the first opportunity for riding one. As a result of my passion, my father bought me a horse and I could move around our house in our beautiful the country when and where I wanted ! After many years, when I earned money enough, I went to stables for taking riding horse lessons with an instructor who was a former famous jumper. He allowed me to have a good level in show jumping. But I had also many possibilities to attend serious accidents, especially the death of a horse in a show jumping competition. This accident made me consider that this sport was dangerous also for horses. Afterwards I was afraid for my horse and gradually this anxiety hindered me to riding properly. At the end I stopped competition and show jumping. Now I do only dressage.

Right now,  I'm in High School,thus, I don't have any job career. However, if we consider babysiting as a job then I've been doing that some months earlier. My plans for the future ? Well after High School I hope I'll be able to go to an American University so that subsequently I could work in America and perhaps I could be a movie translator ! Nevertheless, money is a big trouble as studying in the USA is really expensive, that's why prior to that I would like to go to England and work as an au pair girl so that I could take care of children, take English classes and be paid. I have a master degree in computer science, and a strong background in physics. During a one year period, when the internet bubble bursted, despite a lot of interview, I didn't find a job, and I fallback in an internship position.  After,  I started to work as a developer in a small software company. Subsequently, I change for a consultant position in Sogeti Marseille. I've been in charged of the migration of mac application on windows platform for the french train company. During the two following years, I did several mission as a consultant, I had during this perio the opportunity to develop a Java expertise in different business environment. I spend one year at the French biggest Telecom company Orange. At this time, I was looking for a better position in a bank or assurance company, when a head hunter contact me for developer position in the Geneva area. I've immediately been there to have an interview, I accepted the position, and by the end of the year I moved to Geneva. I've been working for six years for the same company.

Even if the equality between men and women is much better than the generations before us, we are very far to have reach the equality in France in 2013. The average women salary is around 10 % less than man, butthis gap decrease substancialy within the 10 last years. In many sectors, if you are a woman, it's much more difficult to have a job, specially in politics, big boss of big companies...Lots of sectors are still very machos and managed between males. In politics, for instance, they had to create a low to increase artificially the number of women in the government to accelerate the process because naturally ti's impossible. What a shame at the 21 century !!! And this situation is much more unbearable that women have better education and succeed better at school that male. Things might change faster. Pascale

Currently, I'm working in a music school as head teacher. I had an extensive experience, a solid background in music teaching and I was recognized to get manager of the music school. If I may say so, I'd always been passionate about music before I decided to be a musician. Frankly, my teacher has given me a hard time. Apparently he was right because I was recognized for my hard work with an award. However my parents worried about my future because of the money. I was not aware regarding difficulties but I was not in it for the money. Ideally I could perform in concert and earn money enough but realistically I thought I'll be managing my own music school. What would happen if the economy falls drastically and I had to close my store ? I suppose I'd go back to my concert's business travelling around the world. Frankly I'm not sure where I'll be in 5 years but in those days I'm doing well playing and teaching music. Things are always looking up. I guess so.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: It was a short meeting, however we have all been surprised by the agenda. Our Head manager announced that she has decided to leave the company by the end of the year. Since the two companies have been integrated the Board has decided that they not need two Head Managers. They proposed her a new position but she turned it down. The CEO will present the new Manager, next week to the entire department. He will be in charge of the two companies and will flying back and forth between Paris and London. She insisted on the fact that this information is confidential and should not be spread before the CEOs meeting. We told her that we really regret her.

text: Today, the items which has a big impact in my life are certainly my computer (laptop) and my smartphone. I don't even know how my life before these tools was. My everyday routine is simple. I wake up in the morning by checking my email in my smartphone, and for some email I respond directly by this smartphone. In my office I use my laptop to my job and run my own business. I have an e-commerce business, so I need to be connected every time to be able to respond to an email. At the end of the day, I back home with my laptop because it is portable. And before sleeping, I check a last time my email and my e-commerce using my smartphone or my computer if it is switch on.

I graduated from college in literature. Consequently I had to options to start my career. the first one was to work in a library and the second one in a bookshop. That's how, step by step, I took my actual position as shop manager in a museum. Prior to be a manager, I began being a seller for few years. Because of some skills I got the opportunity to take the responsibility of the supply. At the same time, I was working as a volunteer in a NGO in quality of webmaster. That knowledge helped me to get another position in the company creating a e-trade website and starting a wholesaling business. Now that I'm manager for more than 5 years and opened 2 stores I feel like I have quite nothing to learn anymore in here. That's why I planned to move abroad, either in North-America or in Asia. Definitely the beginning there will be hard and prior to get quite a same position as in here, I'll have to accept some fallback job such as seller, pizzaiolo or cashier. But it's a good experience to have to start again from the first step !%%

In France, as in the other Occident contries, the gender differences is a big issue. Indeed, an important value in my country is the equality and that inclue the same treatment for men and women. Moreover this fact is related in the Constituion, that proves its importance. However, despite this wish of equality, women have still worst social positions, have few political representation and their salary is lower than this of the men even if the job and the responsabilities are the same. Why are these problems recurrent ? Because the gender role's vision is rigid in our society, above all in the economic and political sphere. Fortunaltely, the will of change is present and more and more laws help the women to have the same rights. Should'nt we delight of all these efforts ? Of course, but at first, it's the old-fashioned mentality which must disappear.

In France, since two years the bottom is dropping out of the national economy. A lot of people are loosing their jobs, taxes and prices are increasing too much. We can see that more more people are pessimistic and sometimes even depressive.  The national debt is increasing every year, our country is becoming impoverished and no serious plans are developped. The media are also responsible for this bad state of mind, they are always speaking about all that and I am sure that they could, from time to time, speak about positive things. That would help to keep a dynamic and positive attitude. So when you have a roof over your head and food on the table you can consider that all is fine for you. You can learn to be happy and enjoy being without too many problems.

Are there still many gender differences in France ? I think there will always be. After the world war II, mentalities have changed. In 1944, women can vote as men, they become more emancipated. Today, women can be independant, earn money and work like men. However, the equality is not for tomorrow. There are still many traditions in France. The "traditionnal" woman's place is at home. But this housewife's role doesn't inhibit a woman to work. Unfortunately, in 2013 men still ear 16% more than women ! Nowaday, men are more thoughtful with women : they help for cooking, chores... Nevertheless, the division is not equal and many women have a "double day" when they go back home. But some question are more complex: the custody of the children in case of divorce, for instance, is generally accord to the women. Are the men unable to take care of children ? I don't think so. So, is the fully emancipation of women possible ? There will always be a difference between men and women, in one way it can be unfair, and in other way it is just justified.

All of us want to have a career path that improves the likelihood of happiness. Is it realistic? In her latest book called 'How to Make a Career Out of Courage', Judith Wight claims we can match our job and our personality. Change their career path is a common occurrence in life. Statistically, it occurs about five to seven times in a lifespan. What is important is not to make too much mistakes. According to Judith Wight, it's easy in just two simple steps. First, look at ourself and ask ourself what would us make happy at work. Then, let's go online and take some of the many career tests to discover what kind of job is bound to match with our wishes and skills. It's likely to be easier said than done...

When I started my career, I believed that people was promoted based on talent. After few years, I changed my opinion when I became aware of the importance of office politics. In fact, my colleague had developed his network when I worked hard. In the result, he got the promotion; I didn't. I felt that it was unjust. Later, I understood that he succeeded because he was more than just talented and an expert at office politics. By contrast, my work wasn't sufficient and I neglected to strengthen my networking. Obviously, I was in bad position when it was time for a promotion. Since then, I decided to change my strategy. I've tried to integrate the game. First, I started to collaborate with my colleague who got the promotion. After, I asked him for advice. He explained to me that the best way to achieve my goal consisted of getting to know my colleagues and to show them respect. He told me not to forget to communicate regularly to my boss because he or she can be my biggest supporter. I admitted that I was uncomfortable practicing office politics but now I enjoy doing it and even giving advice to other colleagues.%%

When I was growing up, when I wanted to read a novel I must buy a book. If I wanted to be on top of information about word life I must buy a newspaper; when I wanted to learn geography or history at school, I must buy notebooks. Nowadays, only forty years later everything has changed: you don't need anymore papers. With only one tablet, you can do what you want. You can download all books you'd like to read; you can directly purchase clothes; you can book a table in a restaurant; you can look for a new job; you can listen to music; you can order a taxi. In a while you can do everything that you need in your every day life. Yes! it is very simple for everyone but for me, never beats a good book, I mean, I need this contact with the paper, it's sensual. Never beats a good concert, with all the instruments and players If you love music like me. So I believe that progress is good for the ability to accede quickly to everything but I need to keep few of uses. When I was growing up, when I wanted to read a novel I must buy a book. If I wanted to be on top of information about word life I must buy a newspaper; when I wanted to learn geography or history at school, I must buy notebooks. Nowadays, only forty years later everything has changed: you don't need anymore papers. With only one tablet, you can do what you want. You can download all books you'd like to read; you can directly purchase clothes; you can book a table in a restaurant; you can look for a new job; you can listen to music; you can order a taxi. In a while you can do everything that you need in your every day life. Yes! it is very simple for everyone but for me, never beats a good book, I mean, I need this contact with the paper, it's sensual. Never beats a good concert, with all the instruments and players If you love music like me. So I believe that progress is good for the ability to accede quickly to everything but I need to keep few of uses. Broadband definitely is a fantastic way of exchange.%% You can communicate everywhere with everyone in the world in a split second.

I remember that a friend of mine, who lives abroad, came to France for business. He asked me how he had to behave at the table with his French partners. Here was my advice : In France, its essential to respect good manners when someone invites you at home or to a restaurant. Its important to follow these rules : 1) Before meal: You have to wait to sit down until your host invites you to do so and indicates your place. After sitting down, place your napkin on your lap. Put the two hands on the table, on each side of the cutlery. Its impolite to put your elbows on the table. 2) During the meal: Do not take bread before the 1st dish has been served. You're supposed to wait for your host to begin eating. Use the cutlery the most distant from the plate first to finish with the closest ones. It's shocking to talk with your mouth full. Do not reach out to take something that is far from you, but to ask politely your neighbours of table. It's rather impolite to use your phone at the table. 3) After meal: Do not put down your cutlery on the table, but on the plate in a parallel way.%% It's polite to place the napkin not folded on table when you finish eating. You must wait until your host invites you to leave the table.

Do gender differences remain in my country ? May I question myself about it ? I don't think so. Even if things have improved those past years, some differences between men and women remain the same. The most part of the population is ready to reach for equality but no one agree about the way to reach it. While some want to split the bill, others consider that men have to pay it. Within a family, everyone is capable of cleaning the house. So why it is mainly done by women ? What to think about salaries ? The pattern looks the same, but things have definitely changed. First of all, women doesn't have to ask permission to live their life, second of all, many acts are result of decisions between men and women, but a lot remains to be done mainly through companies.

My first thought is that to run a company with efficiency it is necessary to have a good analysis of the strenghts and weaknesses of it, that should be done through a brainstorming including the staff: employees and management and after hearing the different proposals and feedback of everyone, choose the best decisions to achieve the goal. What is important is to be clear for that : it is necessary to identify specific needs, to organize a schedule, to evaluate the real opportunities and threats, have every one on the same page. Something that can help a solid team: using instant messaging, having meetings to discuss the issues between them, exchanging ideas along the process and the most important giving to each a specific task.

text: Hi Stuart, I saw the presentation you've made today after lunch. I think a little feedback could be very useful. Let's begin with good things. Your opening was quite proper : a clear kick-off sentence, a complet introduction (your name and function, the goal of meeting), and a plan of your presentation. Great! Keep that. Let's move on to points you have to improve. You didn't keep enough visual contact with audience and there was too much unsureness in your words. In general way you were a little muddled. That's too bad because you were less clear and you wasted a precious time. Therefore, that was difficult to follow your points. To finish, your conclusion was flat and sloppy (but that was a direct result of you poor time management). Maybe it's hard to hear all of these bad points. But I think you should know what you've to change, thus you'll be able to make good presentations in the future.

I've once learnt that life can really be tough, nevertheless you must face all your troubles, you should not give up as it would get harder and harder. The best things in my life are my friends because they help to escape a little bit to my family situation! The reason why I've learnt the previous lesson is because four years ago my parents got divorced and at first it was not that bad, however since a year or two it got worse. I felt and still feel really bad, sad, depressed. I try as hard as I can to fight everything but it is really really rough ! One advice I would give to the people in the same situation is to talk to someone, it could be your parents or anyone, just talk about your life, your problems because if you don't it will ruin your life ! That's what is happening to me right now, I hide everything to everyone because I don't want to complain as I know that there are situations way worse than mine ! As a conclusion we can say that it is like "Do what I say but not what I do !%% "

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: To write about the role of English in my life is very easy. Indeed English played a major role in my life. Four years ago, with one of my friends, we decided to move for our vacation to Hungary. We planned to visit the beautiful city of Budapest and in the same time to enjoy a big festival of music called "the Sziget festival". After one week I met a little blond girl. She was cute, smart and a big smile was every time shining on her face. I started to fall in love. But I had to go through a big hardship because I had totally forgotten how to speak English. I tried to manage with my Englishs remains but as soon as I was back to France, I decided to take back my old schoolbook and tried to practice. I was keen on to improve my English just to be able to speak with her on Skype. I really wanted her to know me better and how to do that without speaking English proficiently. I've learned a lot and as you can see I'm still learning. But now it's for another purpose because we got married and we have a wonderful little boy.%%

The author explains how it's important to seek for the position that suit you.A customer was getting a promotion but she was still dissastified.That's why you have to determinate the appropriate job according to your personality.It will avoid to get the wrong direction and to not feel lost during your career. The author suggests two ways to define it: -Try to understand yourself, what you like. You can use a mirror and talk to yourself about why you like your work. Observe your environement (hobbies and work) and define how you act with people. Learn what jobs are best suited to your personality and interests. -Then use The Internet and do a career test. You can also ask yourself about what you prefer: working alone or in a team,work with ideas or objects...

The main purpose of this excerpt is to provide some information about how people should react when they face a career path confusion. The author uses an interesting way to explain that workers must focus on their feelings, hobbies or needs to determine which job could make them happier, which job could correspond the most with their personality. Even though it is likely not so difficult to find such a position, I guess the author forgot many important points, especially in the current crisis which has been affecting all the industries. Obviously, people must choose a position which will satisfy their needs, but according to their skills and experiences, not only feelings. When you apply for a position, the motivation is absolutely not enough for your employer, you have to have at your disposal a real background, the requested diplomas and sometimes must be strongly recommended by another people.

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: Technology has completely changed my professional life, not so much my private life. Let me elaborate a little bit what I want to say. When I joined my company, thirty years ago, email or cell phone did not exist yet and we spent hours on the phone spending our time at our desk . Today , thanks to emails or cell phones, you can be reached almost wherever you are . However this is a double edged sword as there is a tendency to consider that you are supposed to be very reactive , much more than in the past because you have got new tools. An other trend is the over use of audio conferences involving people who are using their cell phones (because they are travelling or for any other reason). To me this should be an exception as it is poor communication. In my private life, I do not think technology has really changed anything.

text: The last sales meeting where I attempted , was really clear about the target which must be achieved by the sales department next year. I heard with pleasure the presentation of our manager about the difficulties meet over this year and the solutions taken to keep the customers. Also the presence of the CEO to this sales meeting showed us that the sales department had a real strategic mission in our company and we had to carry on our effort. However I have some remarks about this sales meeting . Firstly if our manager had announced the key points at the opening of the meeting it would have been easier for us to be more attentive; secondly if each sales representative had presented their sales action over the year it would have been more constructive. Thirdly if our manager had let the sales representatives talk it would have allowed to the CEO to show their commitment. Except the below bad points , this sales meeting remain positive and  it will be usefull for the next year.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: As the meetig began, the CEO ask for the attention of everybody to focus on the first item of the agenda : the company have financial problem and a solution was necessary. The CFO agreed him and proposed that the best way to get out of this is to agree a merger  with our biggest competitor. But the COO disagreed, saying that however, we are still the best in technology and we should try to find another solution. They can't deny that the marketing on the products was a failure. At the end of the meeting, the CEO concluded that we shouldn't take a such big decision so quickly and decided to schedule another meeting for last week in order to let everybody think about it.

In France, we have a lot of musical comedies and plays in each city. Musical comedies take place all over the country, as a tour in the biggest towns. My family loves to see these shows. We love the way the production is implemented, and all of the details they had to think of. The costumes are absolutely exquisites, the dancing and the play of the characters are marvellous as well. We love to take part of the adventure they share with this kind of show. On the other hand, most of the plays talk about society aspects or social behaviours, in a humoristic context. If you want to see a play, you have to be prepared to laugh during all the show. It's amazing how the characters make fun about a society or social subject just by their play. I think all these shows are some interesting activities to do to have fun and share good moments with friends, and family. We used to first think of the cinema or the bowling, but we don't have to forget the others events our city can bring us.

In France, since years, things aren't looking up. The national economic situation is falling down. Certainly, due to the economic crisis, but maybe the management of the country would have could be better during the 10 latest years. Time of work decreased, it may be a good thing for some people, but it also decreased the "purchasing power". In addition, the overtime disappeared because of stupid laws. People, who wanted work more to earn more money, aren't allowed to work more. In France the medical system is one of the better system in the world. Social security and mutual healths afford to french citizens to heal properly without going broke.

Six years ago, I decided to change completely my life. At that time I worked in the first pharmaceutical company of the world. I had a good job, I was a team manager and I had a high salary. My wife didn't need to work because of my good income. She could take care of our three children. We had a comfortable house with a very large garden in the suburbs. This area was quiet. So we had everything we hoped before. Many of our friends thought our life was enjoyable. But I also had a lot of stress, I worked all the day, early in the morning until late in the evening, six days per week. I was burn out. I was sure I missed something very important in my life. I understood, I never had time to spent with my family and to share the elementary things of the life, for example going together to the cinema or to a concert, or even talking about what our children wanted to do in the future. So I decided to quit my job. I now work on my own. I decide my schedule. I take time when my children need it and I finaly feel much better.%%

topic: Finding a home for a wealthy client
text: I took up skiing when I was just a kid. It was extremely enjoyable, and I gradually became a more-than-competent skier. I continued to practice over the next few years, getting better and better, and before I knew it, I found myself downhill racing. I even won some regional competitions. Then, when I was 18, I had a horrible accident  I hit a tree and broke both my legs. It was a terrifying experience; I didn't ski for a long time after that. However, after I had been away from skiing for a long time, I knew that I would always regret it if I didn't face my fears, so I started skiing again. It was difficult at first, but little by little I felt better and improved. I think I've gotten over most of my fears now. I enjoy skiing again, but I don't race.

When I was growing up, in the middle 1960s, internet didn't be found, regardless general public didn't use it in France. The only media we knew were books, newspapers and television; however, they were less widespread than today. It's difficult to imagine, but everybody didn't own a television. Subsequently, we really were less informed, and especially less quickly, about what happened in the world. Dictator's life was far easier... Internet was a groundbreaking invention, which changed the world as deeply as the industrial revolution did in the last 19th century. As for me, internet totally changed my way of learning. Nowadays, if I search for an information, at any time, about any topic, I take my laptop and I immediately access to any article, anywhere in the world. It's absolutely fantastic! In the past, I must go to the library and, with luck, I could consult a few books and nothing else. When I was a schoolboy, I learned English with a book; today, I curl up on my couch and I connect to Englistown. I study by watching video clips, and my private teacher lives in Capetown! The only thing that internet didn't change for me is my way of reading books: I continue to prefer paper.%%

I remember when I growing up, I liked reading anything : newspaper; novels; travel magazines; and my favorite, historical fiction. At the time, books was printed exclusively on paper. So, it was necessary to move in library or bookshop in order to procure things to read. What the pride I felt at having on my shelves all the books I'd read. In the past, every day, all the membres of my family watched black and white TV together. We couldn't choose our program because there was a single channel. One day in the 1985s, my parents decided to buy a color TV. What delight to have this modern device. In the same time, I was pretty sad to give up our old TV. Somewhere along the way, Internet became more and more an important communication media. So, I've started to read news online with my laptop. In France, when you are arriving into the restaurant you are expected to go first before women if you are a man, because all the people inside will like at you and not the woman. So, at the table, you have to let seat the woman first. It's bad manners to put elbows or hats on the table. Manners have to do with showing respect toward others.You are supposed to wait every seat before eating.What is proper is to not talk with a mouth full.You are expected to stand up if a woman was to get up to go to the restroom. After sitting down to eat, place your napkin on your lap.Remember,never use you cell phone at the dinner table.

I like my life. Philosophically, I think I am always at the place I must be at a given time : because what I am, what I do is linked directly with my previous thoughts, behaviors, and actions. Of course, like every human being, I often dream about having a better position, a bigger income so I could purchase a bigger house and car. However, in twenty years in the same company, I had five different positions and each one was better than the one before. I don't see why this evolution should be different in the future: I always try to do my best. Nevertheless, I have two challenges: to master my level of stress in keeping a very regular practice of sports and to have a more satisfacting social life, I mean, to bind ou rebind deeper links with my friends and maybe to found a family.

topic: Helping a friend find a job
text: The meeting began with the speech of the CEO: the purpose of this meeting was the competitiveness of the company. The CFO proposal was to merge with the major competitor, but the COO disagreed and said that the only way to solve the problem was to fire employees. He continued saying that the company had to change inside troubles like communication and dead lines. The CEO was not totally on the same page: her idea was not to fire a lot of employee but to improve their work by rewarding them. At the end of discussions, the board accepted a try of 2 month with an obligation to increase the competitveness and programmed another meeting after this date.

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: First of all, I have to confess that I'm French... and it leads to a double handicap in English learning: Foreign language teaching is deservedly famous for being dreadful, and the French tend to think they are still a great nation and their language must be universal, because they forget that the Age of Enlightenment is over! For that matter, we often hear say that our language has to be defended! Therefore, I must confess that, for a too long time, as a good stupid French, I have been defending my language against the alleged English threat by refusing to use it. Plus, I taught French literature, and I didn't need to speak English, so that I forgot the little I knew! It wasn't a problem  until I became a headmaster in a high school... Indeed, I had better to be able to speak English because I meet foreign teachers when we organize students exchanges or Singaporean parents who arrive from their country to work at an air base near my school. Besides, I thought it was a pity to be unable to speak English nowadays. How can I travel when I retire in a few years? Therefore, I decided to join EF five months ago, and I regret not to have did earlier because it's gripping!%%

We have a law in France that forbid to take sans on a beach. That law looks really strange to the American, Canadian and British people who come in Normandy each to visit the landing beaches. To bring back some sand from Utah, Omaha, Gold, Juno or Sword beach is like to bring back a bit of the soil of France where their dad or uncle landed and sometime died. However, it's forbidden in France to take out some sand from a beach a law that has been voted to protect the coast and the beaches. Of course some hundreds of visitors taking a handful of sand will never destroy a beach but if everybody would do that and without any limit then some other ones would take enough to make a beach in there garden or to make concrete in their home. Such a law looks really strange but it's necessary to preserve the environment.

When you receive an invitation for a party, you must reply relatively quickly because your host can think you're waiting for a better invitation. At the party, it's thoughtful to bring a small gift , bottle of wine or flowers  to your hostess. You must be polite with the other friends and introduce yourself each other. When person are sit down,  if a woman or an elderly person come,  all the gentlemen would stand up. Don't take or receive phonecall, or send text messages with your smartphone . At dinner table, eat carefuly, slowly and  use cultery correctly .  Don't slurp ! Don't yell  or don't talk about politic, religion  or money. It"s impolite to get up for going to the restroom when you are on the table. Put your napkin on your lap and don't put your elbows on the table. When you live the party,  said how happy you are and few days after send a short thank you note or email to your host.

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: My grandfather, whom I never knew, was American which is not enough to speak English, let alone speaking fluently! So, as most French pupils and students of my generation I spent 7 years trying to learn this foreign language. We were brave enough to face stern or boring teachers, and sometimes both. Learning English was a chore except during the last year with a young American lecteur, hired to speak with us (to speak, not only to listen or try to learn by heart irregular verbs!). Now I wonder how it was possible to make it boring.  The language teaching in France is a pure scandal! I should say was because young people seem as gifted now as young German or Norwegian. Anyway I am ashamed to admit that I followed the scandalous example of my teachers. When I was 21, I went to Algeria as volunteer. There I was supposed to get a position of primary school teacher. The inspector found out that my mother had an American name so he decided that I could teach English. I accepted just because there were less hours in the college than in the primary level. Poor pupils I am ashamed, I say !%%

Today, there are hundreds of networks through cable in high definition or even in 3D. There are more and more e-media and less and less newspapers and magazines.But it's not because people want to read news on tablets, it's because traditionnal newspapers are very expensive to produce. And what about the informations broadcast ? Are there more accurate, more interesting than before ? I don't think so. What is called information today is often rumors or poor ideas. I'm still very attached to paper and to real books. E-medias are cold and make me often angry because you have to click to go to another page or to delete ads...On the other hand, paper is "warm" : it's relaxing, it's as if I had a direct link with the author, with his mind without the barrier of the screen.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: Mr Smith came to us to give  presentation about new solar products of that new brand. I liked the way he spoke during that presentation. His voice was strong enough to be heard by everybody in this large conference room, and he emphasized very well on key points. his eyes ran on the whole audience, making no one feeling left apart. He used some clear charts, that made us aware of his aims, even without being solar products specialists However, he could have brought some of those products, to make the audience more attentive, because he spoke during 3 hours, at least. At the end we were all not so careful to his points. Making his talk more concrete with products he was talking about could have given his presentation a much more living face. The projector was not working very well. Mr Smith could have checked it before his presentation began, it was difficult for rear seats to see clearly dark areas on the screen.

In Marrakech, a city of Morocco, there is a square called Jemaa el Fna square, where you can enjoy lots of performings. In fact, there are snake charmers who make their snakes moving along with a flute. But it's not the music made by the flute that makes them moving, in fact snakes don't have ears so they can't hear anything. It's the movements that the charmer makes with the flute that make the snakes moving as if they are dancing. You can also see fire-eaters that are performing. We really have the impression that the fire is coming out of their mouths. There are many other tricks! For instance, there are apes obeying to what their keepers tell them, it really makes us feel that those animals understand the human language. There are also story-tellers, acrobats, tattoo artists using henna to make their tattoos,... There is such a magical atmosphere in Jemaa el Fna square with all these performers surrounding by people staring at them with wonderment!

text: I remember the time when my company offered me my first smartphone. It's completely changed my life. For my job I had to travel in a large area and I had to keep in touch with my boss all the time. So I had often to stop at a phone cabine to call him and there was often a queue. I had to wait long minutes before to access the cabin. When I've had my smartphone I could call him and my clients everywhere at anytime. Those are the avantages but there are also downsides. My boss sometimes calls me very early in the morning and even very late in the evening. I have no more family life and I don't know how to explain him. You can understand why my wife is sometimes angry against me.

It's not really a funny game for me to participate to the office politics. However, even in a small company like the one I'm working for, it's absolutely impossible to avoid it. During years my boss used to be like a mentor for me and I was following most of his advices and agreeing to many of his points of view. For me it was not a strategy but a kind of respect toward him. With time, the more I increased in the company, the more I understood that behind the smiles we can find a large deal of hypocrisy. My mentor was able to use my skills and my support against some other colleagues, not to the benefit of the company, but just to get a higher position in it and whenever it was possible to the detriment of those he was considering as enemies. Now, I know how to share things and I prefer to follow the one who seems to be right rather than the one who can be considered as an ally.

Given that, I am still a student I will discuss about office politics in school. To my mind, office politics are not really used in University, we do not really see students using different tactics to be successful. Everything is based on talent and the amount of work you intent to dedicate in order to be at the top of your class. By contrast, I think in high school students were using more spin tactics in the aim to be popular or be elected as a class representative. I guess some people just master these tactics since a long time, in order to obtain what they desire. As regards my thinking of office politics, I think that to employ successful strategies you need to be very smart and not show your game like in a poker game. The bedrock of office politics lie on the ability to master the environment and the people surrounding yourself without appearing too ambitious and selfish.

My personal finances are currently changing. Untill now I pursued my studies and my parents helped me by paying my rent. With my grant I managed to keep my finances in good shape. I wasn't well-off but it was enough if I was thrifty. I even could go out few times a month or have some parties with my friends (it's cheaper and nicer when it's made "at home"). I am pricing coordinator within a forwarder, my job requests somes qualities : reactivity , accurate, commitment . Sometimes , it can be stressful . Also I get married and mother of one child. My life is simple but not easy, like all mothers who work I have to manage my personnal and professional life. Frequently, I wonder whether I am a good mother and enought dedicated in my job. To stop this kind of questions and not stress me more, I remind me that the most important is to do everyday your best to be with your family and to be involved in your job. I like my life even if it 's not hectic , I am really satisfied of my job and the way to educate my daughter. I don't meet financial worries , I have a roof over my head and save a money. I consider that the happiness begins to be satisfied of simple things like that.

The most important thing to me in life is my family . At once, it makes me anxious. When I see my youngest grandson, not yet two years old, I am fascinated by his potential (like any child). All children have the ability to learn quickly so many things, not only language or physical aptitude but also interest for the others, and moral feelings. So, when I see some (better said most) of them engrossed by stupid video games or trapped in consumer society I am afraid. I would like him to be brave, and ready to fight for generous ideals, which is not exactly wide-spread nowadays.  I wonder why I need to dream the best for a child when I see what I did myself. Certainly not enough, with many doubts, lack of enthusiasm, lot of errors. Anyway, what I learnt is to accept myself and tolerate people as they are, no matter their ideology, beliefs and even behaviour. Don't judge people is my recent advice!

Do women have the same role than man today? In France as in the European countries, there is a big evolution about the position of woman in the society. women rights have been reinforced such as in a company. Progress has to be continu  to allow woman having the same rights than man. However, we need to admit a woman is different from a man, we couldn't compare man and woman in each domain (in sport for exemple)

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: I decided to learn English when I became aware that it was tremendously important for business and travel. So, since a few years, I tried to study English by myself but It was difficult for me to be self-motivated. Plus, I had no opportunities to talk with others English speaking people. Fortunately, last year one friend of mine recommended me to enrol in online language training like Englishtown. According to him, this online platform was going to allow me to improve my English skills more easily and more efficiently. At first, I didn't believe him. But, finally I dared to overcome my misgivings. It has been convenient for me to follow my courses whenever I have wanted and from anywhere. Now, I realize that English has improved my life by allowing me to meet other English speakers from all over the world and communicate with them. Even though the learning process has frequently been frustrating and painful, I'm proud of the progress I've made.

Dear Sir, I am writing regarding the overtime I am due . I had to work extra hours during two weekends and I object that you refuse to pay this overtime. My contract clearly states that I should be compensated for my weekend work. Furthermore, you argue that these extra hours weren't your fault, this argument can't be considered as nothing in my contract allows you to refuse to pay me for something the company is not responsible of.  Consequently I believe I am within my rights to claim being paid for the two weekends I had to work. Should you refuse to pay, I would consider the possibility of suing the company for damages. I hope there is an opportunity to settle out of court. Yours sincerely MAP

text: I recently gave a short presention of the sales figures. Helped with charts, it was not enough to explain why the situation didn't go on increasing, I mean our benefits. Since the merger, our team is a little confused and ask a lot of questions. They could have think that the news sale figures was due these changements. Fortunatly, in sharing and comparing the data from the last year until now, through the merger, the executives was able to have a clearly understanding. Now, they will be focused to their current clients with a good customer relationship management, leaving the prospective actions to the other company. I have tried to be focused on my voice, to speak correctly. Beginning to sumarize my presentation in only 3 key points, I had spend most of my time to explain the figures, then it was very easy to tallk about the two key points left. Once each one of them explained, the questions could have been resolved, more efficiently too.

I started my career at KPMG and was specialized in the industrial business. Prior to that, I studied in a French business school that includes internship training in their program. After having worked a few years as an external consultant, I needed to be involved in a long-term basis in company so as to have a real added value for the business. Subsequently, I joined the finance team of a small IT company. Unfortunately,  the economy was already influencing some client investment policies and the sales fell drastically. At this bad period for the company, we had no choice but fire some people to lighten salary expenses. This had a significant impact on the staff moral but frankly, it also had shown very good elements in our team and high potentials for the future. I deeply hope that when the market picks up, we could hire again and go back into normal framework.

In my Country (France) people who are smoking aren't allowed to smoke in night clubs , bar and restaurant. Nowadays, It's against the law to smoke in those publics places. It was difficult for a lot of persons to be agree whith that law because they was too addicted about cigarettes. I think it 's still tought for them to follow that law. From my point of view , I agree about it! Because I don't smoke and hate respirate that smog when I eat or I dancing on the floor. I'm a really healthy person and I care about my healt thus it's important for me that this law was applicated. But in other hand , I can understand for smokers it is not easy for them to stop smoking directly when they come in a restaurant and just eat without their cigarette.

When I was young, we didn't have a TV at home and we used a radio to stay informed of what happened in the world. I remember that it enabled to listen to news by accomplishing daily tasks. After, we bought a TV, which became our biggest source of information. We ate in front of the television news, and we enjoyed the possibility to watch images illustrating the news enabling us to understand them better. The newspapers offered the same qualities, but it represented a cost to buy them daily and we couldn't do anything else by reading them. Nowadays, I use the web to get informed. As a matter of fact, I think that it brings almost all advantages : we have a infinite and free source of information, we can look at different sites to examine all kinds of thinkings enabling us to create one's own opinion. We can even sometimes let a comment or read the remarks of other readers, creating sometimes interesting debates and identify better what is at stake.

Smoking can harm the health and the government tries to limit as much as possible its consumption. Every year, new laws are voted to discourage consumers to go on. First, in a financial point of view, taxes on packs of cigarettes are often increased : the rising percentage of the prices is significant, so a lot of smokers complain that smoking is becoming a luxury. Furthermore, one tries to disgust consumer from tobacco : some advertisings make them feel guilty, and the producers are obliged to display on the packs shocking images revealing tobacco disastrous effects on the body. At last, smoking has been forbidden in more and more public places (restaurants, coffees...). Some smokers consider that such legislation represent a blow to individual rights, but the others have the right not to breathe smoke. As a matter of fact, non-smokers must be protected from passive smoking, which can have bad consequences.

When I was a child we had no TV at home. So I was used to read a lot, in fact all the time! Now I'm still a avid reader. Anywhere I go, libraries and bookshops are my favourite places.I like watching books covers, it attracts me irresistibly, I like the smell of the paper. When I travel, I never forget to take a book. I can share it with my friends. I never have technological problems, it works always, I just have to read. I never try to read my novels on a tablet. However I can read news from all the world, or gardening blogs or find incredible recipes on an electronic support. At the moment, I have not an e-reader nor a tablet. So my experiences are limited to read on my computer. What I regret is that takes time: internet is like an infinite galaxy. Often, I have the sensation when I switch off the computer, after two or more hours surfing, reading a lot of informations, that nothing really substantial stays in my brain. Nowadays  new language and new expressions come from the new technologies. My reading habits have changed but not that much!%%

So my experiences are limited to read on my computer. What I regret is that takes time: internet is like an infinite galaxy. Often, I have the sensation when I switch off the computer, after two or more hours surfing, reading a lot of informations, that nothing really substantial stays in my brain. Nowadays  new language and new expressions come from the new technologies. My reading habits have changed but not that much!%%

In this day and age, despite what sometimes people use to say (especially elderly people), the etiquette still exists. In France, like in other countries, when you are invited at someone's or somewhere, for dinner, or an other special occasion, there are some rules that you have to respect. First, don't be late, or too late: when you are invited at someone's place, the best is to arrived few minutes after the scheduled hour, but not more than fifteen minutes. If you are going to an event, like, let's say, in an Art Gallery, for some kind of event, just be at time, or five minutes late. Then, an other advice is to bring something, a little present, when you're invited for dinner somewhere (except if you're going to the restaurant). The most common things are a bouquet of flowers, or a bottle of wine, but other things are also appreciated, like some special food, to taste at the aperitif. Of course, in all cases, turn off your phone before going to your invitation, because it is ill-considered, and very rude. Then, when comes the time of start eating, wait for everybody to be served before starting, it's respectful.%% Well, now you're ready to spend a good time at your next invitation. Last step : book a flight to France to taste some nice food !

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: I live in France and in this country, french have the reputation to be the worst in langages in Europe. In france, generally, we began to learn english in the equivalent of your middle school, so when we're 11 years old. In my mind, this is too late, we could start sooner. The sooner is learning a language, the more we'll become fluent. Then, we continue to learn english class by class, even if this is not one of the biggest subjects. In fact, professors still superficial; they only learn us basis. Arrived in university, the learning of english continue but depend strongly on the speciality we choose. I explain, for example, I choose to study the pharmacy speciality and when I look at lessons and at the frequency, I think the school system decided that pharmacist doesn't need to be expert in english (about one lesson useless per two weeks). However when we decide to choose, for example, marketing speciality, we have inevitably more lessons, more useful and more frequent. In my case, currently I make an internship and I will start a job in september. The knowledge of the language in my job is mandatory, so, I try to make up for the lost french time.%%

text: Among all the extreme sports I know,  I would like take up, one of them which has always attracted me and by a long shot; the skydiving. Indeed, for a long time one of my brothers-in-law had been a skydiving instructor. One day Ill practical it, I need only a few time and to decrease my fear of heights. However, Im trying to describe the feeling of my mother-in-law who had practiced once, and who didnt think she would be able to do it. Just after that the plane took off but mainly at moment when youre going to jump out of the plane. You feel all adrenaline running through your body, a slightly sensation of fear especially when you are shaking in your shoes, face to the open door, normal its your first time, but what a rush just after jumped out, and during the free fall. The breakneck speed was absolutely amazing; she was never felt so free. And if she felt safer in the air, she said me the last thing you dont must forget is opening your parachute before the landing, but on top of that in general youre not alone the first time, your instructor is present.%%

In my country proper etiquette is going rarer. Maybe isn't it adapted to this day and age ? Anyway, being polite to a dinner still remains very important, especially in France, where we often sit down a very long time to eat. Even if, I guess, to put the napkin on the laps or to use the right cutleries with all kind of forks, is not expected from people anymore, waiting for the hostess seated before starting to eat still matters a lot ! However, I must admit that, most of the time, only basic manners are respected (like don't speaking with mouth full or not receive calls during the meal, that's to say no bad manners), while the utterly etiquette is typically applied for formal parties, by the Elderlies or in traditionnal families. Finally, when we want to thank the hostess, we generally offer a small gift (like a bouquet of flower, chocolates or a bottle of wine) when arriving, and after congratulate her for the reception, so that she's reassured about the success of her dinner !

Since the financial crisis of 2008, the economical situation in  France is very depressed. The bottom droped out of our manufacturing industry.Lot of people have lost their jobs and standard of living has decreased on average. The key factor is our poor productivity. Our wages and our public expenditure are to high. That becomes difficult to compete with other country, includind EU states, especially Germany. Unfortuantely, french governenement didn't pass the bill which are needed to improve our economical weakness. It's a serious concern for France, but, fortunately, as a lawyer partner in a pratctice whom most of clients are public communities, i haven't been personally affectected by the general depression. Thanks to economic growth,  coming back in the rest of the World, in particular in the USA, we can hope that the situation is turning around now and that our country is looking up and is going to recover shortly.

In 2006, a law was voted by the french government that forbid smoking in public places. If you don't respect this law, we committed a felony, this is a infringement to the law and have to pay a fine of sixty eight euros. Personally, I think this is a good decision of our government. Indeed, this law oblige smokers to respect people around them. Before this law, we could be in a bus and support the awful smells of smoking. It was constraining. Moreover, this law permit a lot of smokers to stop their bad custom by forcing them to go outside for smoke, even for one cigarette. To my mind it's globally a good thing but in the other side, I think that some smokers have a real problem with their abduction, I mean this is like a medical problem, like they're sick by smoking. In general, they're people stressed everytime. I think that government shuld think about a psychological monitoring for treat these people fundamentally.

Once, when I worked at a big clothing shop as a sales assistant. I was advising a woman for a shirt for her husband. She was quite tall, she had blond hair and blue eyes. She was a very beautiful woman. However, she was a little fat, so I thought that she was pregnant. I ask her when the baby will comes but her face changed and she got very upset. She said that she wasn't pregnant, so, I said that I was very sorry many times but she didn't hear anything and she walked away. It was very awkward.

When I decided to learn and to play classical music I knew that I couldn't waste time anymore. I learned piano very young and when I was twelve years old I changed for the classical guitar. That was deadly serious, studying a lot and performing later. I had a hard time but I never wasted time worrying and never gave up. What does matter, though, is that I became a professional and performed in concert. Every day was important and I learned how to maximize each one. Every musical piece is important and I do play as if it were my last music. The classical music repertory is way too large to all play and people who like this music is huge. I have to do my best to make people happy or to motivate others about this music. That sounds great to me because I found what works for me and I want badly to share with the most of people. If I should give an advice to young musicians, it'd be : work hard, don't waste time and never give up - what does matter is that you show your generosity and your feeling through your music.%%

I was at home with my parents when, suddenly someone knock to the door. A childhood friend of my father came in the living room with a young woman to his arm. He had certainly 60 th years old , like my father. We drunk champagne and We were getting along well until I asked my question.... I thought she was her daughter, so I asked her whether his father was as friendly as mine.She answered me that Jerry was her husband , and they get married last year!!  I can't believe it,the girl was his wife!! I was so embarrassed when I realized I was being totally offensive. It was really awkward!! I had putt my foot in my mouth! I usually never make social blunders like that!!! Of course , I made my apologizes and they accepted them! They were as embarrassed as I was, but everybody understood and we had spent a very good evening!

Are men and women equal in France ? Certainly not, but things are going better. French law prohibit the differences in salary based on gender, but in fact, there still exist some enterprises who don't play the game. Did men always have the good part ? Not really. As the world as changed since World War 2, and particularly since the 60's, women get more and more involved in working life. They get more responsabilities, more power, and really more exigent with men. Nowadays, being a man is not so easy. You have to be careful, but not too much, you have to be romantic, but not too much, you have to be a real male, but not too much either... Furthermore, competition at work is harder. Women have the same capacities but they have a great "joker" in their pocket: they are charming and executives men often want to be protector, or wathever, and they pay more attention, they are thoughful, and sometimes can be influenced to give more opportunities to women, for the same quality of job.

In France, it has been applied a law forbiding to smoke in public areas like restaurant , bar, train bus station , transports and school. This law has been put in place the first February 2007. We can consider this law has been well accepted by french people. Even if this hasn't dicreased the number of smokers, this law has had a real benefit for all people. No-smokers are not longer troubled by the cigaret's odors and now smokers couldn't consider now to smoke in a bar or in their office. In my view, this kind of law is really usefull for our society ,  when this law has been applied I was smoker and I've never considered this law like unacceptable but like a new way of life. From my point of view the persons who haven't yet accepted this law and carry-on to criticize are too addict to the the cigaret. Their drug is most important than their neighbours, childrens or friends' health.

When I was growing up, I mostly watched television for the news and movies.  I no longer have television since I moved from my parents' house. Thus, I listened to the radio more often in order to stay current on the news, and when I heard something surprising, I made further researches on the web. Nowadays, absolutely all my media material (news, musics, films, TV shows) go through the Internet. Concerning my personal readings, I am maybe a little bit old-fashioned, but I prefer read a real book with real words printed on real pages. The e-book is a nice device (I tried out once), and I agree that it is very practical. It is light, compact, and, to a certain extent, less fragile than a book whose pages can deteriorate or get dirty. But, despite all of these advantages, nothing can replace the special feeling of opening a book for the first time, the smell of the paper (especially when the book is brand new), and, above all, the typical little sound of a page that turns.

text: I always wanted take up white-water rafting. I love the water sports. When I was young I begun sport with canoeing in calm waters, little by little I wanted more thrills, so I took up white-water kayaking. Next I practiced this sport in gorgeous place, with huge rivers, sometimes even the ocean. I really loved this sport. But one day, I saw people who did white-water rafting. It seemed to be awesome, and also very scared. I didn't understand why, but I felt afraid of this sport whereas I always practiced extremes sports, like hang gliding, snowboarding, windsurfing or also skydiving. One day I decided to face my fears of white-water rafting, so I enjoyed down the river. To finish, I want to tell that I'll never regret to face my fears, and I plan to face others fears. And I recommend to everyone to face your fears.

In my country, France, manners and etiquette can vary quite significantly from a region to an other region . It is as well very much depending on the social class you are refering to.  For example placing carefully your guests around the dining table is a critical exercise for a typical dinner in the upper class while it has less significance in other contexts . We have very precise rules if needed to place a bishop or a MP etc....These rules are useful to avoid any mistake which can hurt some people... and damage your reputation ! After all,  knowing these rules allows to concentrate on the most important thing which is the quality of the conversation associated to the quality of the food (not to mention wines which could be a subject by itself !). To achieve this you have to make your guests feeling comfortable But there is one sentence which is definitely prohibited and which I have never heard in France and it is to start the meal by saying 'bon appetit' !

Equality between men and women? Not yet in African countries. At home, at work, in the society there are still differences between both genders. At home, women who dont work are still responsible for domestic work, whereas men,even if they dont work, dont do those works. Even when women work they do some of the housework. Men, as before, provide the subsistence for the family and are chiefs of families. This structure is usually the same at work where men are often directors of companies.  In the society, people hold onto traditional values. Men are supposed to protect women, to rule the society, whereas women cater family needs and educate the society throughout the education of children. However women are becoming more independent, and are claiming more responsibilities in the society. The best illustration of this trend is the president of Liberia: A woman. Things are slowly moving

I experienced some changes in my lifestyle recently. As I mentioned in a precedent written work, I have finished my studies last year (in June). Thus, my position about money has radically changed. My parents don't help me anymore for my rent, and my grant is now over. I am in my 'job-hunting phase' since early August. I sent a lot of curriculum vitae for many many job applications, but of course none of them was followed by an interview. I only got form emails of rejection ('thank you for your interest [bla bla bla] not enough experience for us' final point), or even no response at all. It's really annoying and stressful (I need money to live). Thankfully I keep in touch with my friends. It let me forget just for few hours the toughness of reality. They are lucky and happy, in couple, and each of them has a job and a secure future (at least for three years). Sometimes it makes me a little sad (you know, that feeling of being the fifth wheel). On the other hand this allows me to keep hope. Perhaps I shouldn't had done such long studies. But now, what's done is done.%%
