Ive been working as a financial broker for about 10 years. I enjoy my job which is challenging and exciting. Prior to that, I didnt really have any professional goals. After graduating in economics, I decided to gap a year in order to think about my professional carreer. When my father fell gravely ill, I had to abandon this plan. My father owned a clothing store and couldnt give up his activity. So, he asked me if I could run the shop until he recovered. Although I didnt dream of running a shop, I was forced to accept. This experience which lasted a year, was very difficult but also useful. Not only I had to manage the shop but had also to coach the employees. I learnt to be responsible. Following this job (when my father cured), I went back to school tow years and became a financial broker. Although my job is very interesting, it is also very stressful so that today, I plan to work in a different field. Why not buying land in order to lease plots of land to private  individuals? They could grow their own vegetables.

text: I really enjoy rock-climbing but I took up late. I was fifteen. I think that iit's more difficult to start a sport like this at this age. Maybe because we are more open on the danger. However, even the first time, it was enjoyable to learn how to belay someone or myself. Contrary to what people think, the most important in this sport is not the strength but it's the way of climbing : choose the good grip, made the good choice... The mental is very important and at the beginning, that's the thing which was the most difficult for me. At first, I was scared of the height and of the possibilty to fall down. But when you reach the top, what a satisfaction!

A lot of people I know devote a large part of their budget to savings. They prepare a monthly budget that they try to follow. It's important to prepare the retirement. If they don't own their house, or if they have not yet finished to repay the mortgage, the roof represents the most important part of expenses because real estate is expensive. After, living cost has risen : food, insurance, ... are the second highest rank. If it remains money, it can be spent for travels and leisures. If not, there are only two solutions : either trying to get higher wages, or doing efforts to reduce some expenses as much as possible.

I'm an IT engineer. First I had followed biology studies. These studies passionate me but the trades didn't interest me because I thought the research environment was too competitive for me. I was not ambitious enough. So when I saw that there was many jobs around computers, I decided to change my orientation and I passed a competition for a short but intensive computer training. Afterward I worked in Hutchinson for 6 months as a programmer. Then, for complete my computer training I follow a training of production management. Afterward, I had the opportunity to work for a training center which delivered a training in many disciplines : computers, general matters, management, etc. This center delivered training for very various people : young people for initial training, unemployed for reconversion or also for companies for internal training. I worked for this center for four years. It was an exciting job but my ceaseless moving accross the country exhausted me. So I applied to a large insurance company where I have still been working.

Have roles for males and females changed over the year? In the past, women had no choice. She had any leading roles in their country or in the world. She just stay at home, take care their husband, cooked and cleaned the house. During the first and the second war, the role of woman  growth and change the usually mind. Lot of males were in war and the industry need people. In this period of crisis, lot of women worked in the industry and manufacturing to maintain and support the country while the men are fighting overseas. Currently, the roles of males are  the same than the past but the roles of females over the years have consideradly changed for more leading and responsability although there are between man and woman still difference. In fact, woman for the same job don't earn as man.

In France, the 20th century was an improvement according to the women place in the society. Before the Second World War, the role of women was to stay at home, to take care of childrens and to educate them. But during the war, women invested in factories to replace men. The war as been a rotative for women. In fact, the place according to women has increase. They obtain the right to vote, to right to work without the agreement of their husband... Gradually, they have a most important place in the society and mainly in the world of work where we can see more and more women as CEO or with an important place. However, there is still not equality between men and women. For exemple, the women's wage is often lower than the men's one. And theyis still inequality in politic where the place of women is still not important even if they represent the majority of the population. But we can says that the french society has made huge progress during the last century.

The new law in France for last summer says that it's prohibited to smoke in public places like bars, restaurants, bus stations, community transports etc... It's became as a felony to smoke where it's prohibited and the sentence could be a big fine or even 5 years to prison. My point of view is that I'm in favor of this new law. Healthly, this law protects people who  don't smoke and for addicts, they have specific places for it. But something annoys me very much: when I go take the metro at the station I always see people who smokes their cigarette. And it's the same case at all public places where it's prohibited. I think the police is not strict wich people who violate this rule. The police officers should do more rounds in public places and I'm sure they should get people who smokes in. I've a lot of friends who smoke and when I invit them at home, I impose them don't to smoke in my appartment. That's my rule. If they want to violate the gorvernment's rule that's their problem, but my rule in my appartment they have to observe it.%%

In all Europe, improvements have been made in the subject of gender equally for 50 years. After the voting right, the abortion right was obtained in 1980. That was an important social advance as the victory was won side by side with men and not against them.  However, we have to continue to seek the total equality for example about wages.  By the time,  the gender concerns are very important within specific politic regims which try to lower the women rights level. In those countries women can't have education or health care as effective as men are used to enjoying. We must endorse them.

I grew up at the time of the books and the newspapers. It was also the beginning of the television. In the mid '50s, my grandfather bought the first TV set of our little town. It was a huge varnished wooden box with a tiny round-cornered screen. There were only two channels available, but passing from one to the other was a big deal. You had to change the direction of the antenna thanks to a motor on the pole, controlled from a device behind the TV set. Far from current screens, yes ? My parents did not buy a television as long as my brother and I were students. My father enjoyed his daily paper for the news and the cross-words . We would read much, at that time. I was proud of my collection of books. I must say that today I still have a lot of books on my bookshelves. An encyclopedia in 12 volumes, for instance, and many atlas. They are just decorating now and taking the dust. An encyclopedia, a geographic map cannot be updated, while your computer gives you the latest information on demand. Nevertheless, I keep reading novels and fictions on traditional paper books. I have not yet tried e-readers...

Therefore, I advise that I am within my rights to object to being refused overtime pay, and I could sue my employers for damages. So, I hope the mistake  will be soon repared. Waiting for your answer, Yours sincerely.

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: I remember when I started my professional life, I worked in a team managed by a person who were gifted with exceptional technical skills and great charisma. He was responsible for 15 engineers. I was full of respect for all he had accomplished for my career. I also admired his commitment to the company. The mutual trust that he created between us was a major source of motivation for me. He often communicated the reasons for his decisions to everyone involved. In his opinion, if people didn't understand why a decision was taken, they were less likely to care about the change and helped the team. For a long time, I wondered how this person became leader. My questions remained without answers. Fortunately, since last year, I have worked with a company that leadership is a part of its areas of expertise. To summarize, the most important things that I have learned are a successful leader: 1) Needs empathy to understand how others feel. 2) Must help people to communicate freely. 3) Focuses on efficiency and a more effective way of working. 4) He is turned towards the future. He can't be a throwback. 5) Earns trust and persuades others.%%

Shouldn't women get same roles than men in France? There are plenty of debates about this, because it's a real problem and a taboo subject until 2012. Indeed, after having been elected Franois Hollande created a ministry of women's rights in order to reduce differences and inequalities between males and females. In 2010, men earned 25 percent more than women, but this percentage could increase according to social categories. However, thanks to new laws, the roles are going to change. For example, the paternity leave is becoming longer in order to incite fathers to use that more often like Denmark or Sweden. Gender equality is important, we mustn't be locked in an old model where women doesn't work and is dependent. What a shame! In France there are so few women who manage companies. In spite of the way is long and difficult, the mentalities are changing. Who knows? Perhaps in 20 or 30 years we'll have a female president. That's my dream.

Why gender equality is important in France? The gender equality exists for a long time in the French constitution. However, it is still a hot topic in my country. In the current government, there is a Ministry of the women rights. During the 20s, the role of women in France changed dramatically. Two events were mainly responsible: World War II and "May 68". Before World War II, women's role in French society was clearly defined: Their place was in the home, raising a family as their husbands went out to work.  During the war, the woman replaced the man in factories and agriculture fields. The events of "May 68" upset traditional values by the legalization of the pillule and the abortion, what gives to certain women the feeling to be more independent. At present, the debate concerns to the parity between men and women in the political and economic power.

text: When I was younger, I wouldn't strike you as an extreme sports type. I was shy, puny and I didn't have nerves of steel. People used to make fun of me. The differences between men and women have been reduced over the 20 past years in France. But is there still a difference ? In 2013, men dedicate more time to housework, and some of them take the parental child-rearing leave, but women still make 80% of the total housework, and they are 97% to take the parental child-rearing leave. In another hand, despite the new law presented by Najat Vallaud-Belkacem this year in order to get more equality in the genders roles in the society, we can still notice that there isn't any woman at the following functions : President of France, President of the National Assembly, nor President of the Upper House. Now a day, many women want to be financially independent, or family need to have both of the parents working in order to assume their social position, as one salary is not sufficient anymore. Does that mean that we completely lost the traditional model ? That would be a shame !

Is there a real gender equality in France ? Today, most of the women work, but are they get paid as a men ? No, French women are paid 30% less, than men. Is interesting to understand what could justify this difference. There's two items who justify it : the pregnancy and the life expectancy. Women can be pregnant, and then stop to work for few month, and still get paid. On the other hand, statistics show that women live more longer than men, so they are longer retired. Furthermore, in a company, it's not really usual to have a woman as a CEO, perhaps the possible pregnancy explain it either. Isn't it difficult to have his CEO away for her maternity ? The gender equality in a company, as in the french society is still a challenge.

Women are more affected by partial-time jobs with difficult hours (early in the morning or late in the night) which complicate the family life.  Due to their low level job, their average is lower and this point cumulated with the difficult hours is hard to manage for lonely women with kids. In the family, this is still the women that take care of children when a problem occurs (disease, school strike, etc.). The man is always too busy to have a day off, so the women has no other choice that managing this trouble. However, most of man knows that equality is requiring, but traditionnal values are still there !

I started my professional career in 1997 as an IT analyst. Now, Im a project manager in a small company that I founded with some friends in 2006. Our activity consists in supporting different companies in their human resources organization. At that time, the economy turned around in my field. Before hand, I enrolled in a business college in Paris and decided to study to become general manager thanks to my experience in several companies in France and abroad. Once I had passed my degree, I started to look for business partners in ordrer to start my own business. What was my delight when I signed my first contract as associate manager. Overall, It was a fascinating experience to be my own boss. Fortunately, Im not just in it for the money because I have worked hard to make existing my company and give a true meaning to my work. However, I have not lost sight of the fact that any company must be profitable not to die. Now, That is a difficult time. The economic crisis is tough but I don't give up. If the HR business burst, I can always fall back on my IT experience.

Dear Janice, I'm writing to say how terribly sorry i am about selma's behavior last night.It was rude and insensitive of her to go on and on about money. I'm sure she didn't mean to offend you. Althought I'm not looking to give excuses, I did want to give the reasons why she put her foot in it. Once again, please accept my sincere apology. With best wishes. Samba

I used to say that Im someone who's pretty good to manage his finances. Obviously, I already have encountered a lack of money. Especially since I moved and get a new job. Living cost progressively increases, housing budget literally exploded, my insurance and my motorcycle's mortgage as well. I had to borrow some money to my mother, which wasn't easy as I consider myself as independent. I also had to change the way I managed my finances, and start from a scratch my new monthly budget. But, thanks to that, I could keep a roof over my head, and food on the table. Now, things are turning around, and I'm saving money.

Just a few years ago, our reading was limited to the printed page. Digital publishing changed all that. People read on tablets, e-readers, laptops, desktop computers, phones and even wearable computers. Instead of carrying around a heavy backpack of textbooks, students can do all of their schoolwork on a tablet. What if you don't understand something in your digital textbook? Just tap on a link on your gadget; a pop-up window immediately provides you with helpful information. Even better, a video might pop up to provide an explanation. As a bonus, electronic communications media allows you to change the font size to make things easier to read.

I propose some additional management strategies to prevent and solve business problems. Just a suggestion : we might use SWOT analysis, the management-strategy process where managers evaluate a project's strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats (SWOT). Then, we could develop a greater feeling of mutual trust between employees and managers. What I'm proposing is that we be willing to allow employees to take on greater responsibility; we should be willing to allow them to make more decisions. The more we can trust our employees to perform certain tasks without supervision, the more upper management can devote to expansion and profit strategies. Last but not least, I think communication is the key element of our success!

text: A the beginning of the meeting, the General Manager announced the closure of the hotel for renovation work in the coming year. The Director of Operations agreed as the establishment really needed renovation, particularly rooms. The Financial Director thought that it was an excellent idea and asked the General Manager to organise a renovation work budget meeting as soon as possible. The Human Resources Manager also really liked this decision. However, he pointed out that this information was strictly confidential until the announcement to the Works Council. He really insisted on the fact that nothing had to leave this room to avoid any legal problems with the unions. Finally, they all agreed to work together on that item.

text: The last presentation I attended was really memorable. It dealed about the next steps of the recasting of the company's business model. The lecturer knew how to draw our attention to the main points of his plan of action with compelling titles and key points to summarize his ideas. I have to admire his tone and gesture very confident. Saying that, I would have liked his to put charts to emphasize each of his point especially when he was talking about the key figures of the company he was trying to explain how, for instance, the sales would increase to 10% from 2% a year with the strategy. Regarding the beginning I should have prepared the room and knew how the projector worked if I were him. I spent too much time to get ready, it was embarassing for him. Overall, the presentation was compelling and expressed really clearly our next move which is reassuring.

In my country we have many differents performances. Usually they are in the street for the parties in summer. In the coast is pretty popular playing with bulls. The people run in front they in the streets and in the beach. Sometimes the bulls have fire in their horns, also they can dropped to the sea. I think this tradition of playing with bulls is awful. They are animals, and they suffer. There are also other popular traditions like the "cabezudos" or "castellers". The first one is the people who disguises with old clothes and placed in their heads very large masks. They dance and parade in the streets. The "castellers" are a castle with people. That is very popular the exist also competitions of "castellers", the highest, which lasts longer. Is really interesting and good because that is as un sport.

text: What makes a good leadership is a combination of several qualities such as trustworthy, understandable, patient. The key element to master to possess a good leadership is adaptation. I have been learning English for two years now. It's somewhat more difficult than I expected at the start. I have a lot of difficulties to speak fluently and I have some difficulties to remember the usual vocabulary, no matter how hard I work. Maybe, I'm getting too old unless I don't use the right way. I intend to know usual words for many current situations. So, everyday, I listen English speakers on Englishtown, BBC learning website or VOA. I write down sentences or phrases I have heard with the different meanings of a word. I read every morning the phrases I noted the day before and I read an english book every evening. Currently, I read a very funny book of british humor. I have bought some novels of Patricia Highsmith as well. Furthermore, I do like doing easy English crosswords. So, I hope I'll be able to cope with this language.%%

I wonder if there are manners and etiquette to respect nowadays. I think, it might depend on your social class or the one of the persons who organise the dinner party. I belong to a family of industry workers and little farmers and my parents always receive people from the same class. There is no real etiquette to follow, no special attention to have toward a person regarding her job or the money she has. But, there is a natural and mutual respect, because nobody has an easy life and everyone do his best. Words that are used are normal ones and, in these occasions, there is a tendancy to try and avoid slang language and too familiar words. Depending on the presence of grandparents or children, technical language or adults word are less used, I mean internet or sms language and sexual humor. A natural politeness guide our behavior without exageration or hyprocrisy.

I have never thought about office politics in my job or at school, because it is not my practice. I try to do my job the most effectively possible, doing my better in every situation and I believe that thanks to that, I could progress in my career and my life. Maybe I'm too naive because I know certain people try to influence the hierarchy but it's not my way. I never go on parties or things like that where are the decision-makers because I don't like that and I don't feel at my place. Perhaps one day I'll play the game if I see I don't advance as I want. In the meantime I do my work and try to not annoy or disappoint my bosses. I'm confident of myself and I know my manager see how I am important in the company.

text: The meeting today was about the delay in the construction of the tower, due to the loss of the container with the generator. The client strongly recommended that we air freight a new one instead of shipping it. I said that would cost a fortune and asked the client if he was ready to support the extra-costs. He responded that we should report the incident to your Insurance Company, and move ahead. However the insurers will pay for the replacement of the generator, but not for air freighting it. I finally got him to accept the principle of  a modification in the time schedule, so that the engine would be incorporated in the works later.

Gender equality ? It is still a big issue apparently. The choice for the "accurate partner" in the private life is obviously easier now, with more freedom (at least in our occidental societies). topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: My country is member of the CEMAC: Central African Economic and monetary community. It is an international organization which counts six countries: Cameroon, Centrafrique, Congo, Gabon, Equatorial Guinea and Tchad. The CEMAC has many goals: create a very clos union between his member, delete all problems which can disturb the trade between his countries and create a real fair trade. However since my country is member of the CEMAC, we have not notice any change in our trade with the others countries. Even the agreements that we have signed are not respected. For example we have signed an agreement which give us the right to freely go to another CEMAC country without the need of any visa. The agreement is just for the paper because until today, if we want to go in a CEMAC country we still need to have a visa. Finally I think that the most important thing is not to be member of a trade agreement, but to be able to respect all agreements of this organization.

However since my country is member of the CEMAC, we have not notice any change in our trade with the others countries. Even the agreements that we have signed are not respected. For example we have signed an agreement which give us the right to freely go to another CEMAC country without the need of any visa. The agreement is just for the paper because until today, if we want to go in a CEMAC country we still need to have a visa. Finally I think that the most important thing is not to be member of a trade agreement, but to be able to respect all agreements of this organization.

Solar power To my mind, solar power would be the most effective form of natural power to meet our city's energy needs. The city of Lyon has been through 1582 hours of sunshine during the year of 2013. We need to take advantage of that natural power in order to reduce our consumption in fossil fuels and thus to reduce our monthly bills on electricity. The cost of installing solar panels would cost around 120 000$ and we need to include high maintenance and installation cost. However we can ask subisidies to the government if we decided to implement solar panels in the region. It could be a huge opportunity for us for multiple reasons. Firstly, it will enhance our image and reputation around the country and everywhere in Europe. Secondly, we could launch a new trend in France and thus, improving the quality of life in our country. To conclude I strongly believe that solar panels are the next thing for our beloved city and I hope that we'll take the best choice.

CSR is a very controversial issue which relates to many political, economic and ethical discussions. On the one hand, some people believe that CSR is a way to make the private companies look good and helpful. At best, it would be a PR trick, a publicity stunt or a marketing device. At worse, it would be a way of demotivating workers to fight for their rights and to engage in social struggle. It's the point of view of left wing politicians. On the other hand, most people are convinced that companies have a real responsibility to the community they belong to. Companies aren't only committed to their shareholders but also to all the stakeholders with which they have to deal: workforce, clients, local authorities, people living in the neighborhood, state. Doing good for everybody, including the disadvantaged people, is the core value of the free market economy which have always been related to philanthropy. American capitalists, who set up powerful foundations, are perfect example. In fact CSR is a new word for an old concept: Charity. The time has gone when the wealthiest simply donate directly to people in need.  Time has come when legal people do what natural people did.%% Thats what CSR make possible nowadays.

To answer this question, I would say in the first place : yes, I do believe that important companies have a big role to play in their public image and in the general behavior of consumers. Such those companies are carrying an important image and they look like examples or even references to thousands of people. For instance, let's take Apple Inc.. Steve Jobs changed the life of millions of people by developing a new vision of computer softwares with Mac. And then people starting to think differently about the Internet and new technologies. They now see it as a place where everything must be simple to use and intuitive. But Apple has also an image to keep : they must avoid such scandals as those with the Foxconn company, which is in charge of iPhone building. To finish, I would say that big companies have an important part to play. They must carry a good image of businness, and not the idea that their belong only to their shareolders. For instance, in france, the government is encouraging people to buy French products and not only products which come from abroad. CSR is a matter for all of us.

Montpellier is situated, in the South of France a few miles away from the Mediterranean coast. But the tides are not very strong, so the tidal energy is not the right renewable power to choose for this place. Alternatively, the region of Montpellier is crossed by a stong river, called "Le Lez", that might be a great potential for the local population, as well as for the environment. So, a dam could be built accross The Lez. And since Montpellier population is increasing steadily , this investment would not cost a lot, because 450000 inhabitants can easily raise $75000 project costs, as part of their local taxes. In addition, that there would be no fuel costs for this project, and the maintenance cost will be very low. Consequently, Montpellier population would use a renewable energy, for a lower cost than the energy they are using now. If I were the mayor of Montpellier, I would do my best to save my city from pollution, and thus preserve the environment. In my opinion, using hydroelectric energy in Montpellier would be a big step towards building an environmentally friendly society. It seems as simple as that, but politicians don't really practice what preach.%%

It not obvious to have the answer about this question: do companies have a responsibility to society? In fact I think there is no good answer, there is just many different point of views. For me it depends of how the companys activities impact the overall society. Actually every companys activities don't have the same impact on the society. Some have a very bad impact on the society like some big industries. They really heart the atmosphere and mess up the city. These kind of companies have a great responsibility to society. I think they should be obliged to give back to the society by contributing to the improvement of the environment, by helping students or by helping schools with computers and others staff. However others companys activities have any impact on the society. These companies have not a big responsibility to society, but they could act positively in society and have some good advertisements. We call it a win-win situation because everyone win, the company and the society.

I think that corporate social responsibility is an ethic way to live properly in this world. Many people don't have good chance during their life. Not only corporate, but private people have to think how to donate when they have enough to live. I have no share, only some money on ethic accounts. I think I have some responsibility to society. And I would like that companies share the same idea. But it is not the most of them. I will create a new society in two months. It will work around primary care in my country. I have choose the model of a cooperative to avoid shareholders and to give responsibility to each of the member. If this cooperative would win money, the benefit will be shared between the members. I hope that I will be able to convince the members to set up a CSR department. Not only to donate money, but also, to give time.

Success is often associated with financial and social success. The image that comes to my mind when I think of success is a hard-working and busy businessman, who is very rich and respected by people. I imagine a man who runs behind schedule but who has the time to show his wonderful and elegant woman during fashionable evenings. Businessmen are respected because they embody the power. On the contrary, a spiritual person like Soeur Emmanuelle, who owned nothing, was admirable because she was herself and made us aware of the essential things. I consider that Soeur Emmanuelle succeeded her personal life because she remained faithful to herself and to her deepest aspirations. She found the meaning of her life by believing in God and by helping the most needy. However, you can find some similarities between these two examples. The businessman and the spiritual person both have a goal and do their best to reach it. They both have qualities and skills that lead them to success like intelligence, a great involvement in what they do, an extraordinary strength of character and perseverance. Despite these similarities, I cant help thinking that the businessman is just gifted like thousands of businessmen whereas Soeur Emmanuelle is an exceptional human being.%%

Every day you can observe that companies are more worried about competitiveness than about respect for their employees or environment. Lately, there was a big fire in Bangladesh in which quite a lot of European and American firms were involved. How many workers, mostly women, died in this accident? How many CEO felt directly or indirectly responsible?  Dont you remember, too, the oil spills in Brittany, Alaska and Gulf of Mexico? The only law seems to be competitiveness. Market shares, shareholders dictate their rules and corporate social responsibility seems to be no more than a wishful thinking. Reality being what it is, would it be responsible to give up? There are so many powerful and gigantic international groups dominating not only economy and finance but also news and our minds that the temptation is to get desperate. Are we allowed to give up? Did Muhamad Yunus, Nobel Peace Prize, founder of the Grameen Bank give up?  In one of the poorest countries in the world, he invented new economical and successful concepts. In our own country, France, the social economy won today a true victory :Les Atelires who were supposed to be defeated, two days ago, were given enough money, from private donators, to save their jobs.%% What a symbol to celebrate the day of the women tomorrow!

The peak of the wind have exceeded 378km/h devastating everything on his way in The Philippines. A tropical depression going too far of pouring rains is announced on the area of Tuesday morning threatening to get worse the situation.  Haiyan has been declined moving in the West of Vietnam. The assistance have a lot of problems to reach the towns and villages in the centre of Philippines blasted and  overwhelmed by the typhoon Haiyan.  The survivors have no water, neither food nor medication and the operations of the help still difficult because the roads, airports and bridge have been destroyed by the storm or are recovered by debris. 40 tons of energetic cookies will be sent by planes to feed 120 000 people plus emergency equipments of telecommunications. One month ago, a terror attack has been place in Russia in the city of Volgograd. The death toll rose to 16 people dead. Just before the starting of the winter Olympic Games in Sotchi this week, there has been mounting critics about the organization about this controversial Olympic Games. First of all, there are a lot of critics about the cost of these games, around 50 billions dollars, which is five times larger than the organization of the Vancouver Olympic Games. Besides some experts believe that there has been a lot of corruption in the different contracts deliver by the president Poutine. Second of all, some westerns countries are worried about the treatment of gay people in Russia. Indeed, a new bill has passed recently, and gay people are not allowed to kiss each other on public squares. That is why presidents such as Barack Obama or the French president ,Francois Hollande would not attend the inaugural ceremony.

Green business is important to me. To be truly green, a business should consider using renewable energy, such as solar or wind power, or an office with a green roof. Businesses that sell green products have to conserve natural resources as much as possible by using organic, recycled and biodegradable materials in their products and packaging. I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I will pay a lot more for certain items. It's not always easy to find truly green products, and I don't want to use gasoline by driving to buy green products. However, I am concerned about the food I eat, so I sacrifice other luxuries in my life to make sure I'm eating healthy food. Of course, you have to take a company at its word that the product is truly green, which is why I buy from companies I know a lot about.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Good evening and welcome everyone. I'm Francis. As we all know, we are here to celebrate Hugh and Samuella's wedding. I'd like to offer a toast to them. To start, I'd like to tell you that there love story is a youthful romance. While they still were students they already shared a common interest which is a passion for song and music. To pursue their higher education, they often had to be far from one another for long periods of time. But never has time or distance altered their relationship. Their patience and desire to well organize such an event is a good indication of their respect for marriage. Hugh and Samuella, I believe that your couple will be a source of inspiration for family, friends and colleagues. I wish you to have as many children as you want and to be successful in your assignments for the generation we're living in. Could we please all stand up and raise our glasses to Hugh and Samuella!

During the latest 30 years of the XXth. century, many famous landmarks were built in Paris: Centre Georges Pompidou, Montparnasse Tower, Louvre Pyramid, and Bastille Opera. Some of them were very controversial. But one thing is certain, they all will left a long lasting footprint in the urban landscape. In my opinion, the Centre Georges Pompidou is the most obvious failure. Its groundbreaking architecture and its garish aggressive colors are unnecessarily provocative and upsetting. In opposite, the Louvre Pyramid is certainly an unquestionable achievement. This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

This magnificent glass and steel structure in the center courtyard stands out against the ancient architecture of the Louvre palace. It has become the main entrance of the place. Each visitor can experience how this museum is modern and living and no more as asleep as it was before Pyramid's construction. To my taste, it's a masterpiece. Unfortunately, nothing that deserves to be mentioned has been built for ten years. The architectural policy is static since the mid 90's The Seine banks have been refurbished and cleared of cars. An important part of them are now a pedestrian area. But that's an improvement, not an ambitious urban policy. Without doubt, Paris needs a challenging architectural vision to be able to compete with other world renowned metropolises.%%

Well, I gave a look at the list of propositions we had from some companies to choose a kind of renewable energy for our city. I choose wind power. The reason why I choosed that particular kind of energy is for several reasons : First, we here live by the sea, and the city is build alongside the shores, and we have wind all days. Second, the impact of the installation on the environment is quite low, so we could think about put turbines in many different places without have the fear of destructing the ecosystem if we do that. But I know, the cost is non-negligible (80000$), but I think if we do this, in less than ten years we should be able to reduce our cost of electricity by twice and save up more money to build new ecological facilities in town. It's a bet, but I'm sure everybody here is willing to do this if this is the better way we find to reduce the footprint of our city and habitants.

Although companies play an important role in developing economy, they do have a responsibility to society. For example, if a company owns a plant near a residential area, it has to comply with environmental protection standards. So that the area will remain clean and the population will stay healthy. It is not wise to think that company's only responsibility is to its shareholders. That is not win to win situation, because the main concern in this case is making money. So the company focuses on the most effective strategy to make money, even if that harms the environment and does not respect human rights. Instead of re-investing the whole company's turnover, money should be used in providing employees with good working conditions. Many companies in my country have a good sense of CSR. They save energy by installing solar panels on their plants' roofs, so they use it to run their machinery, and sell some to power supply companies. The saved money in energy is therefore used to reward the company's employees, for their hard work, and thus motivate them.

Although France is known as the country of human rights, French law is quite strict towards hardened criminals; for example, individuals detaining  weapons without any convincible purpose, and with no legal authorization from the local authorities, they will be arrested and emprisoned. Since the 11th of September 2001, France has been taking the same measures as many others countries, as part of global war on terror. Security forces have been using the most efficient tools to track terrorists; any suspects should be arrested for investigation. If there are enough evidence against them, they will be convicted and jailed. As for murderers, they are sentenced to life emprisonment, because the death penalty was abolished in France, in 1981. However, shoplifters and purse-snatchers are apprehended, but they may be released a few hours later, if the victims do not wish to take them to court. Unlike the security forces, French law is too lenient towards juvenile delinquency and petty crimes. So young offenders are generally recidivist, and are likely to become hardened criminals after many years of experience. Therefore, a solution should be found to reduce recidivism, and thus combat criminality at source.

It seems to me that successful people have many points in common. First, they live their lives in a positive way. They never stand too long on negative points and always think about what should be the next step to succeed. Whatever happens to them, they are ready to bounce back. In addition to that, they are persistant, passionate and ready to adapt themselves (or their ideas) to the market's needs. Of course, they also have a strong need to be their own boss and need to do things in their own way. However, sometimes, you can meet people who have successful people's traits and habits but are considered by other people as "loosers". Yet, when you talk to them, you are surprised to see they still believe in themselves, feeling that one day their turn will come up and they will be successful. To do so, they will need the only thing you have to get to be successful : the idea ! Without it, they will continue to be a successful person only in their own fantasy world.

text: Dear Jos, The visit to Portugal with CDMR is now clearly set. We will arrived on Monday evening in Lisbonne, in order to be ready to go Tuesday morning. First, Cristina will bring us to Artebel for technical visit of the plant. After lunch, we will go to Avelar with Eduardo. It would be a short and efficient visit of the expanded clay plant's devices. Hence we could concentrate our time to discuss on technical issue and prepare the launch of CDMR's new production during March. I bet this visit would have a positive effect on our futur collaboration and would be very profitable. Afterward, I will keep you updated on follow up. Best regards Nicolas

I live in the country where no buldings have been built. Only small houses and farms... But my village is not so far from a big city : Lyon in France. Lyon is the second city in France after Paris. A quarter is famous in Lyon : the Part-Dieu. It is famous because it is considered as the second business place in France. There is a lot of buildings and a few skyscrapers. The best wellknowed was called the Credit Lyonnais tour, then the "Tour de la Part Dieu" . The habitants call it : the pencil, because top is a pyramid. It is a red and circular building. It is 165 meter high. The seven highest floors are occupied by an hotel. The second skyscraper in this city is the tour Oxygen. I like the architecture of this other building. It is only 115 meter high, but 80% of the surface is made with glass. It is really beautiful. Even if I prefer the country...

I think my town is a masterpiece of the 14th century's architecture. In fact, I live in Avignon, France, where you can found some amazing buildings. First, the Pope's Palace, a huge castle inside the town, where popes used to live in the XIVth century. It is a very touristic  and a wonderful place that you can visit. On the top of the palace, there is a gold statue of virgin Marie, which shows the story of my town and which is particularly beautiful. The front of the monument is impressive and the park just above the palace, on a hill, is as wonderful. It's a relaxing place where I love to go with my friends or family. In Avignon, there are also a lot of small streets, really old and welcoming and some other parks with trees and benches. The library is an old church which has been renovated so we can feel secure and relax there. In fact, you don't need to love ancient architecture to love Avignon, because you can't think this town's architecture is not great, that's why a lot of people come here quite often!

At the beginning, Paris (capitale of France) was a small village. Since the middle age, most French kings wanted to make their mark on this town which has never been destroyed. Paris houses then an impressive number of historical and famous monuments which are worth visiting. What also characterises Paris are rows of buildings along tree-lined avenues.Those five-story buildings are well known for their maintained facades and their balconies on the fifth floor. In the 1830's, Rambuteau conceived this architecture with wide boulevards and large avenues in order to have the main areas cleared and to make public transport easier. Afterwards, the plan was developed by Haussmann. In addition to those typical neighbourhoods of Paris, no building better symbolises the capital of France than the Eiffel Tower. Indeed, you couldn't possibly visit Paris without seeing the Eiffel Tower which was built for the 1889 International Exhibition. The tower which was the highest structure in the world from 1889 until 1930 rises 300 meters. You can see its top from all over Paris.

Corporate social responsibility most commonly abbreviate to CSR seems to be the buzzword in management recently. A large number of companies are starting to dedicate a lot of ressources to adress the issue of CSR, such as l'Oreal which is one the pionner companies as regards to this divisive issue. However, some people believe that the only responsibility of a company is to its shareholders because they are bringing the capital to the firm.Today, it is too difficult for companies to just focus on their shareholders due to differents factors. Firstly, the negligence of corporate social responsibiliy can have a negative impact on the company's reputation.Hence it could have an impact on the future turnover of the firm.Furthermore, customers nowadays especially in modern countries seem to take a lot of attention to the company's image. This is why corporations need to deal seriously with problems such as environment, low-pay salaries or diversity.

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: In my country communication is more or less indirect. We do'nt do too much gesture when we are speaking. But what we French do, is hugging people we meet ; not at work but in the street. We also hug our friends friends also when we see them for the first time. Living at the German border, I must say , for them it's not a good behavior hugging. At work I have much contact to foreign people who have differents forms of body language. For example the Italiens are the ones which speak with theirs hands; they can't talk to you without them. For them it's also common to call me with my first name and to put a hand on my shoulder as if they know me since a long time. I like them because they are sympatic, even if I am not appreciating physical contact. I prefer their behavior to the North German's who are too cold for me. You have to worm information out of them. This can sometimes lead to misunderstandings, thinking being curious. To sumarize body language can be positif but it make you unconfortable in some situations.

In my opinion, wind power would be the most effective form of natural power to meet our city's energy needs. As you know, our city is located by the sea. We have three hundred and sixty-five windy days a year. Wind power creates no waste and does not use any fossil fuels or other kinds of finite energy. It is low-cost and highly profitable. The cost of installing wind turbines would come to only 80,000 dollars, and the maintenance costs would be low. It would be better than power, as it creates less carbon, and it would be more efficient than using a "Natural living machine" because the wind turbines would need fewer people to look after them, and therefore would be more cost-effective. All in all, as far as I'm concerned, wind power would be the most environmentally friendly and economically viable way to produce renewable energy for our city.

But they aren't a symbol of Paris and France as the scyscraper in the USA. France is know too for the beautifuls castles as "le Chateau de la Loire" or the Louvre (which is now an art museum ) build in the Renaissance time. The buildings of Shanghai are simply breathtaking in their size and diversity. The city has an impressive number of modern skyscrapers  more than a thousand, according to some counts  that are in sharp contrast to several styles of architecture from different time periods. The Bund offers the perfect tour of sophisticated European buildings, many constructed in the mid-1800s to early 1900s in various gorgeous styles, including Renaissance, Baroque and neo-classical. The area is attractively situated along the Huangpu River. Look across the Huangpu for one of the city's most intriguing architectural contrasts: Pudong, with buildings that look like they belong in a science-fiction movie. Here, you'll immediately notice Shanghai's most famous building, the Oriental Pearl Tower. It's definitely not the world's most beautiful building, but it is one of the tallest (at 468 meters) and most unusual. The spire and antennas reach an impressive 118 meters high. Near the Pearl is the Shanghai World Financial Center, which, with 101 floors, is China's tallest building.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: When i see my high school friend, we often discuss about our differents schools or universities which we have choose after the high school. I have some friends in Universities and others who are in preparatory class to an great school exam. The biggest difference i believe is the autonomy of the studdents. In Universities, students can do everythings they want, they can miss a lecture without being noticed. In contrary, in preparatory class, you are followed by your teacher and you are not allowed to miss one hour without justification. The number of student is different to, in university, lectures  can take place with one undred students, whereas in preparatory class you are by group of 30. The charge of work depend more about the subject o the sudies than about the kind of etablishment. Even if it is easier to do a party when you are in university.

In one hand, there are petty crimes, purse snatching and shoplifting, often occurred by young people, those lead to juvenile detention. In the other hand there are violent crimes: hardened criminals, murderer, terrorist which can not be considered the same way and often lead to incarceration. The juvenile detention gives higher chance to young people for rehabilitation than incarceration, provided that young people can get a new or different education in these special centers. The main goal of incarceration is to protect society from violent criminals activities and second to prepare criminals to their rehabilitation. But the chances of success are smaller as violent criminals are not committed to renew their education, consequently hardened criminals might be considered beyond hope. As far as I am concerned, crime could be considered differently in my country: even if the differentiation between petty crimes and violent crimes is clearly made in the court, but unless there is a lack of juvenile detention centers, young people can be incarcerate as well as hardened criminals. The consequences are disaster as recidivism gets higher and juvenile criminals get hardened.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: The WTO is an international organization which goals are to supervise and liberalize global trade. It has replaced the GATT that was found after the Second World War in order to facilitate free trade, development and peace between countries using business way. It involves nowadays around 160 countries all over the world including the most powerful economies, e.g. the USA, the EU or China. It's based on some simple principles that are the framework of the international trade law. Every country is required to apply the rule of the most favored nation to each other and the national treatment policy. Simply put, that means that a WTO member has to set the most favorable tariffs under which trade is allowed for a particular type of goods to other WTO member. Another important rule is the national treatment, i.e. the obligation to ensure that imported products are treated as good as locally manufactured products. WTO has succeed to enforce some important tariff's agreement on goods and investment, capitals and services. An agreement on intellectual property and a dispute settlement procedure added a plus to that and makes the WTO more effective. However, the WTO remains a controversial structure.%% Some argued that it is responsible for the hard global competition which can threaten the less competitive countries.

In France there are differents kinds of infraction to the law: the less important is the violation, after you have the offense which is a little more important than the violation. And the most serious is the crime. You can go in jail or pay a penalty when you are 18 years old,but for serious infraction, childrens can be placed in juvenile detention. Violations are often due to the drives laws and the punishment is always a fine ( under 3000 euros) and a penalty on your driving license. Offences are treated differently according to the gravity of the infraction. The punishment can range from the payment of an important fine to a detention in a prison. To finnish, the most serious infraction, the crime have some differents aspects : the crime against the person, against goods and against the Frenchs' state. They have differents penality,but you have always to pay an important sum of money and go in detention for in general a long time. But in France, the death penalty was abolished.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: There're many different opinions about EU.There're still those who are skeptical about yhe whole idea,and especially about the Eurozone-particularly after financial problems with Greec debts. They argue that there is just too much diversity involved,that there are just too many inequalities in the union.They argue that ,e.g.the economic potential of Germany is not comparable with Greec potential.They say that each country is unique ,i.e. each has its own cultural and economic heritage,and it will lead to failure in the end. On the other hand ,supporters of the EU argue yhat there is strength in numbers.In other words a single ,large market is much more powerful than many individual smaller markets.They also argue that the adoption of the euro in the Eurozone allowed all the member countries to more easily do business with one another.An added plus that the single currency allowed the Eurozone to play hardball with countries ,e.g. the US and China.

Paris is very well known for its variety of architecture. The haussmannian architecture is the most spread throughout the city and represents 60% of the buildings. The city owes a major deal of its beauty to Geoges Haussmann. One of the most touristic city in the world, the places that are the most attractive are buildings or monuments such as: The Eiffel tower, The Arc de Triomphe, The Pyramind du Louvre, The Georges Pompidou center. Among all of them, The best of the best is the Eiffel tower which is the most daring architecture with its 324 meters height. Beside this there is the business area of The Defense where are situated not only modern buildings but also the tallest in number. Each building differs from one another in shape and style but everything is well suited to give a splendid sight to the overall. At last, an important place is also given to parcs. That's why everyone can take pleasure to have a walk in the tuileries or the Luxembourg gardens.

France, is a country well-known for it's wonderful and mostly old-fashioned architecture. The towns of France make you travel to another time. There are a lot of Renaissance, gothic and Baroque architecture , particularly in the historical buildings such as St Peter's Basilica, Notre Dame or several castles throughout France. Paris, it's first city, is the perfect exemple of the melding of architectural styles, there are a lot of classical and antique buildings where people still live. However there are also a lot of modern and postmodern architecture, strangely enough it's not the youngest buildings but they really convey a feeling of innovation and modernism. The Louvre with it's glass and metal pyramid is really unusual and astonishing, people still cross the world to see it. Finally, there is the Eiffel Tower, the tallest building in France with, 324m, entirely made of wrought iron and it's individual look. It's a really touristy building that I don't personally find beautiful during the day but at night, with it's thousands of lights it illuminates beautifully the streets of Paris.%%

Okay, I will tell you a story of a man who confirm that zombies do exist ! it tooks place in 1960's in the native village of my mother. One day, a farmer has died of his injuries after falling off a cliff, he was 45 years old. And naturally, we took her body to the cemetery and was then buried. Three days later, when her daughter and wife were having lunch, he opened the door of the house with eyes wide open and dust everywhere on clothing. His wife dies directly after a heart attack and his daughter became mad ! this man has won everything. In fact, at the time, there were no doctors in the village, so we decide to bury someone if we can not hear his heart beat anymore. But the truth is that the man had a severe bradycardia, that was all ! Anyway, the end of this story is dramatic, but admit that it's so crazy !

I live in Arcachon, a small shoreline city, in the South West of France. Although the population is multiplied by seven or eight during summer because its one of the most famous touristic areas in my country, the breathtaking beauty of the coastline has been preserved, and there is only one building, that couldnt be built nowadays... Its only a forteen-story building, but nobody puts up with it, and we nickname it the verruca, because it spoils the landscape to the extent that you cant fail to see it all around the Bassin dArcachon. Here, there are mainly houses, and in the center of the town, the recent buildings have four stories at the most. Fortunately, we are far from the skycrapers of Shangai! I know that, in Paris, there are some high towers but there are neither as abundant, nor as tall than in Asia or in the United States, because France is no longer a great nation! There are beautiful buildings in Paris, but they were built in the eighteenth or nineteenth centuries, at the time when France was a powerful country. I think that the modern skycrapers are equivalent to the castles in the past. Therefore there are many castles in France, but few skycrapers, and conversely in Asia and in the United States.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: Having graduated from a university in my country and then having pursued my higher education at university of Montreal in Canada, I found out many similarities and differences between both countries' educational systems. First of all, tuition fees in my country are not as high as those required in Canada. In addition, we had received a certain amount of money, on a monthly basis, from our government to take care of all expenses as students whereas most of Canadian students needed to work in summer in order to face academic costs. Unlike in my country, it was possible for students in Canada to develop more friendly relationships with professors. During my first year in Montreal, I had been several times shocked to hear my classmates calling our teachers by their first name. In Canada studies were very practical. How to apply what was we learned in real life was the focus. In the contrary, what counted the most in my country was to be knowledgeable about a broad range of topics. Finally, what universities in my country share in common with their counterparts in Canada is this great and noble duty to pass on, as faithfully as possible, knowledge from one generation to another.%%

Taking care of Environment is more and more a responsibility that companies tend to assume. Initially, managers were thinking that caring about environment would affect the profits by using budget for unprofitable matters. Generally speaking, thats true: it is more profitable to invest in a tool to produce more and faster than you do with the old one. Your immediate costs are decreasing and until you sell all your production, your profitability is increasing. This is a perfect equation! But what if the new tool is careless for environment and pollutes?  Does the neighborhood will accept it without reaction? For sure no, the notoriety of your company will be affected as well as your brands. Consequently, your sales may decrease, you may fail in your new tools payback and the perfect equation will not work anymore. Even if this example is a caricature, in real professionnal life if you take care of Environment and if you use it for your communication then it could create a double reward: better production ratio, and improvement of your notoriety in the society. This is why Environment is a part of Corporate Social Responsibility's policy for many companies nowadays.

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: We will operate the disaster recovery plan mainly in three steps. First of all, we will restore all the data from the backup site. Secondly, well start all the applications and finally we will ask the users to perform all the tests to make sure that the data restored are consistent.  The data restoration is a process that will take twelve hours since there are four servers to be restored and the average restoration time per server is three hours.  The starting of all applications, we'll take at least one hour and a half. This duration also takes into account the tuning of applications components and the technical tests to make sure that all parts are working properly and interacting with each other.  The users tests will include authentication, basic and complex requests execution and then results analysis.  The success of such an exercise will depend on how well we will prepare it and our capacity to quickly handle any problem not foreseen.

For me Jessica is maybe more easy-going but also generous and sensitive.  Together, I think they form a complementary couple and I think their future will be great.  For celebrate the next happiness future, I want to everybody wake up and share this toast for them.

I live in Paris. In this city, there is no very high building but a lot of unusual architecture. The most famous is certainly the Eiffel Tower, symbol of Paris in the world. This tower was constructed by Gustave Eiffel for the Universal Exposition in 1889, in order to symbolize the capacity of the french industry. What's funny is that at the beginning, this monument was not loved by the French and Parisians, and it should have dismantle just after the exposition. During its life, the Eiffel tower was painting in several colors, nowadays it is brown. The Eiffel Tower measure 324 meters and is composed by 3 floors. The first two can be visited by the tourists, with a restaurant in the first floor. On the other hand, the third floor is only use for antennas in order to diffuse TV and radio over the town.

Small start-up rise everywhere in the world and succeed on markets that big companies would like to lead.  But the behavior of these small start up is often to be bought and soon or later to be integrated. These acquisitions give social responsibilities to big companies regards to the process of people integration. Although the skill of integrated people is high and lead the start-up to be at the highest level on the market, it's easy to understand that some practical issues need to be solved: twice support services, twice CEO, twice IT manager etc.... As a consequence, this integration leads to a rapid reduction of human resources and gives to the buyer responsibilities of people selection and replacement. But what would be the final image of the company after this process? The buyer may help or at least gives a free pass to people in their way to find an alternative activity inside or outside the company. The buyer should consider past and future of excluded people . One important reason is not to create your future worst competitor . Another important reason is to prevent bad advertising that degrades the commercial image of owned brands. Give respect to people that are no longer necessary in the organization is surely a corporate social responsibility with payback.%%

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Hello Michael, I'd like to update you on Neptune's project I'm currently working on. According to our progress, we will achieve the Feb, 5th deadline. Nevertheless, regarding our budget, we will exceed the plan by 10%. Primary cause is our lack of skilled people. I took decision to take temporary workers. It was crucial to hit the dealine. I reflected on the financial situation and came to the conclusion that we should meet our client. This customer added some new requirements which have not been budgeted at the beginning of the project. By having a face to face meeting with them, we will better explain overall situation and ask our respective team leaders from each department such as Engineering, Quality, Sourcing to get involved in that final negotiation. I've already asked them to get prepared and come with a power-point presentation that should be concise and effective to better convice our interlocutors.

Green business differ from standard business in that it offers to customers a product with more respect to environmental concerns and it gives a kind of guaranty that you do not support a disturbing activity. I will choose vegatables of a local producer at the expense of an imported product, provided that the local producer is environmentally friendly. Naturally, local businesses are reducing transport and so the carbon footprint unlike imported products. As the local producer is most probably living in the same area as I do, I have just to go out of my way a little bit to buy his product. In additition, we should consider that tons of vegatables are imported from foreign countries, while arable land are neglected or abandoned, and while people are desperately unemployed. Local business can be considered as natural Green business, in that CO2 footprint is reduced and employment is preserved, to the extent that it is respectfull to environmental concerns.

CSR is a divisive issue. Some people denounce the hypocrisy and the cynicism of corporate social responsibility the sole aim of which is to make money. Indeed, numerous surveys have emphasized that consumers tend to support companies which are involved in a social cause. Although those activities dont provide an immediate financial benefit to the companies, we know how much positively they affect their image. It is often said that it is just a marketing device. Of course, its true but should we expect the companies to be philanthropist? In so far as its a win-win situation, why should we blame CSR? Let me give you examples of Kellogg which plans to provide more than half a billion breakfasts by 2016 or of Sparkly Cola which is going to help underprivileged high school students by donating $200,000 in scholarships. Whether its a PR trick or not, the fact is it really helps needy people. Although companies are free to choose their own policy, they have duties toward their employees who must be respected and fairly paid. Besides, companies which pollute the environment have to clean it up.

If you visit the south of France, it will be worth to have a stop at Montpellier. You will be intrigued by the architectural contrast that has been elaborated since the city's establishment, in the 10th century. The historical city center, built in early middle age, is a real labyrinth. You can be lost in the narrow streets which tell you the story of the city, through their beige stone facade houses, that nestle a number of well-maintained private properties, dating back to the 10th Century. You can also find 19th-century Haussmannian style buildings, standing around the main city square "place de la comdie". The most impressive building at the Comedy square is the theater, its facade is exactly similar to the one at Opera Garnier in Paris. Antigone is the modern part of Montpellier. Built in the 1990's, it confirmed the architectural contrast of the city. The most dominant building in that area is "Languedoc Roussillon region's hall", a daring glass Arch, standing on the Lez riverside, it is 50 meter high, the tallest building in the city center. In Montpellier you can have the opportunity to go through three different eras in less than a day!%%

I will describe my previous manager who hired me in my previous job. Thanks to her, I was able to move to London. She is very smart and clever and most of all she has a gift for discussing with people and understanding situations. She smiles all the time. Maybe what I admire above is the fact that she is successful in her professional life but also in her private: she is married to a clever guy who has a very high position in his company, she has two daughters who seem to be wonderful. She doesn't seem to have one failure in her life. I don't know her personaly but it's clearly with whom I want to continue to work innthe future. Some people are born lucky, ithers not. Just difficult to explain why it's like this... I hope that in the future I will look apart like her! Thanks

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Charles, After our weekly meeting, I contacted Serge and evocate this topics tomorrow. I noted that i have to prepare an expatriation package for our new Deputy Managing Director in kualla Lumpur. This is the first time we relocate an employee there, so i need additional information from our local HR manager before sending you a first proposal. This year salary review will start in February,  the board validated the budget en of december, i think that we will be in time for applying salary increase as from March 1st. Regarding appraisal review, the process will start lator on; the process is delayed because of IT data base failure.

I think the question of CSR is very simple and it's suprising that it could be a divising issue. On the one hand, companies are taking all non sustainable ressources on earth for obtaining big profits. What more natural they pay back for that, in giving a better life to society? On the other hand, when companies argue they have nothing to do with society's problems because it's it'the government's job, they are not logical with themselves. If we sould follow their rules, governement would be without public interest! The market is only the one to rule everything. No need goverment and all its regulation! I think all these speeches about morality in companies are funny and really naive. Howether, I recognize CSR is pragmatic, like all the anglo-saxon culture: "A well paid worker is a good worker". Good! Good for them! Good for the company! But I think we forget the real meaning of life, the respect of the planet we have to give back to our children. Not a question about companies but a fundamental question about real human rigths.

In the south of France, their isn't any skyscraper. I am living in Nice where all the buildings are not small. However, you will find a lot of interesting kind of architectures. The most famous part of Nice is the Old City who was one century ago part of Italy. In this area, you'll find old buildings. You'll also find a lot of churches. In an other part of Nice, you can visit the Russian Church who is the most unusual architecture I ever saw. A lot of Russian bought houses and settled down in the French Riviera. The weather is warmest, the people are more friendly and although they wanted to live in a free country. They built the Russian Church who is really colorful. Outside, she has a lot of colors such red, yellow, green, silver and white. She is a masterpiece of architecture. I'd like to say that I don't really like these race for the sky and I feel oppressed when I'm standing near of a tall building like in New York City. I enjoy to live in a less modern area where people have "their foot on the ground".

I'm a firm believer in sustainable living. I believe that instead of relying on fossil fuels, we should make more use of renewable energy, that is wind and water power, the energy of the sun. Provided that you have strong winds,wind power is quite effective. Building a small wind farm will provide our whole village with electricity. It's a cheaper way of generating energy. Why don't we built our houses with traditional energy-saving techniques. The way I look at it, we can do more. If each individual does their part, we can move mountains! In my opinion, we have to be active ourselves

A lot of businesses exist simply to make money. There are others who seriously which to provide a needed service to a community or to the world. Each of these businesses has a corporate responsibility to the public, its shareholders and the world it trades in. Years ago, a company corporate responsibility was dictated by its government. Today, however, corporate responsibility has to take into account the world that we live in on a much wider scale. The public has become much more globally aware, and there are a number of groups that monitor corporations closely. These groups have the conditions of the world in mind. They think about the social issues of the world, such as labor laws and the exploitation of workers. They are also concerned with environmental issues, such as the rainforests disappearing. Large global companies know that people are watching them and that any wrongdoing will not go unnoticed. Personnally, I think companies must have a social conscience, treat employees fairly and try to do the best for their shareholders while trying to be socially responsible. Corporate responsibility has a huge impact on the local community, but also on the world. Bad and good corporate responsibility has effects that reach from the worker in the third world country to the air that we breathe.%%

There are definitely both pros and cons associated with superstores, but I think we have to be realistic about the circumstances of some communities. I understand the argument that superstores create primarily low-wage jobs and provide few if any social or health benefits to those same workers. I also understand that these giant stores could possibly bankrupt smaller stores that are unable to compete with them. I think just about everyone knows this to be true. On the other hand, if a community is already in economic trouble, with few jobs available and people struggling, saying 'no' to a superstore doesn't seem to make any sense at all. Isn't a low-wage job better than no job at all? And, if a community is struggling, wouldn't the lower prices that a superstore can offer help those most in need? I want to reiterate that I understand that superstores are not the answer for every community. Different communities have different circumstances and different needs. All I'm saying is that, under certain circumstances, a superstore might do more good than harm for a struggling community.
