I would like to talk about the nicest buildings you can find in France. I personaly like very much the city of Paris. That's amazing how many interresting buildings we can find here. There are not so impressing by  their respective height but rather to their styles. By going to the center of Paris, where are located the biggest and well-known shopping centers of " Printemps" , " les Galeries Lafayette", you will see here what we call "haussmanienne architecture" . It's a specific style. I do not know with accuracy when it was created but I guess it was in the 1800's century. Now you may want to see more modern buildings. This time you will go the a famous place called " La Defense ". All biggest french and foreign headquarters of business companies are located on a small place as built on a total suface of 25 square kilometers, not more. They are not so impressive by their height compared to the ones we can find in Schanghai for instance. But they are all ultra-modern and regrouped arond the " Arche de la Defense" . This realization has been built by the old French president Franois Mitterand before ending his political carreer.%%

Companies have responsabilities. They offer products to a number of demands, but the demand expects to receive inexchange of its money, a good product or service. That is when the goverment reacts, when the trade ain't fair.  I have example, like mc donalds, which killed a young boy with one of its burgers. They had to pay the price of his death. But again, i have questions because if mc donalds burgers could kill, why are mc donalds restaurents still open. It's not like it happened once or twice. And shareholders must pay to, for giving money to allow those poisoned burgers to be prepar and sell. But how can you juge the juge? And the way meats are provided oh my lord, you give mc donalds to a starving asian or afrikan they would die. Same for coca cola, but what can we do. CSR would be a great idea if the criminal wasn't the cop.

It's not really easy to pinpoint success since everyone can succeed in some things in his life and fail in some other ones. Most of the successful persons we usually admire have been successful in their public life but they did not always met the same success in their private life. as for example, Mahatma Gandhi or Martin Luther King Jr are considered as two of the best leaders of the XXth century ; they have changed the world by showing how to live in peace all together and by helping people to become better persons. However, most of their biographers consider that in their private life they both were far to be perfect, caring or faithful husbands. Similarly, so many popular actors, singers, politicians or businessmen have failed or never succeeded to be truly happy in their private life. Marilyn Monroe, the most beautiful woman in the world committed suicide and Howard Hughes, one of the main shareholders of TWA, spent the last years of his life alone suffering from mental problems... The line between success and failure is often really thin.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I'm European, more precisely French. European Union existed before I was born so I've known it my whole life. For example, I know that I can travel to United Kingdom, Spain, Germay and almost each European country freely, i.e. I don't need Visa, Embassy authorization or anything else, I am free to move to each of these country just because I want, like any another place in France. As a consequence, I get used with this idea that in addition of the fact to be French, I am European, and Europe is, to an extent, my home. We have a unique currency with an important number of countries, which is something enjoyable to travel. I mean, I use to go to Spain for my summer holidays, and I know that I don't need a particular ID paper, change currencies before to travel or anything else. Moreover, since this summer, fines received with our cars in Spain and Germany cross the border too, and have to be paid to the Spanish and German governments.

text: Dear all, I am writing to you to keep you informed about the Diamond project. I have a very good news to give you, the software "Diamond" is finally installed and ready to use. I would like to thank you all for your commitment. First of all, we have to schedule an agenda for the training on the software as soon as possible. Furthermore, we have to book a special room which everyone could look at the projector and make suggestions. I propose to gather some special group belonging in each department for optimizing the time-training. In addition, we have to collect some information to enter the data in the computer as example. I will organize a brainstorming soon, as all ideas are welcome! Please let me know your availabilities. If you have any questions or concerns regarding this project, please let me know. Best regards.

Located on the equator, Dienga is a small city of about 1200 inhabitants. The city council has decided to invest into renewable energy source for its needs in electricity. This decision had been motivated by three main reasons.  First of all, due to its distance from the power grid, it would be costly in terms of time and money to extend power lines to Dienga. Secondly, because of its geographical position, there is a lot of sun over the city almost all year around. The installation of Solar panels takes only a couple of weeks and requires the services of two specialists whose responsibility is to get the proper panels and install them on all people's roofs. Finally, the whole project that amount up to $120 000 will be financed by the local community whose main activity is sugar cane production. For Dienga's people, Solar power is not only environmentally friendly but also is the most cost-effective source of energy.

In 25 years, fossile fuels exploitation will most likely decrease as it makes no doubt they run out and new unlimited and ecological ressources are quickly bound to gain market share. In any case, if we want to continue living on the Earth, people will be obliged to reduce their footprint carbon and so industries will develop a lot of eco-friendly and efficient transportations and buildings to get with the program. Furthermore, at work, things will drastically change due to the evolution of the artificial intelligence and the unlimited growth of Internet all over the world, especially in the developing countries. Actually, telecommute is in the running to become the preffered option for most office workers. Indeed, that will allow companies to reduce their expenses like electricity, heating, rents, water, food. Employees will stay at home to work and will be paid according to the time they effectively work which means the time they stay connected, no matter if they work at midnight or at noon. All the meetings will stand remote, all the services will be interconnected via Internet, phone and videoconference to stay in the loop. Companies will be available to run at top speed, 24 hours a day, without paying extras for the overtime.%% Losing his time in the traffic jam or in the public transportation has an uncertain future!

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: After World War II, European countries sought to create a union by agreements in different sectors, in order to consolidate peace. Belgium, the Netherlands and Luxemburg created "BENELUX". Basically a customs agreement, signed in 1944, BENELUX now refers to the geographic, economic and cultural union of the three countries. In 1951, The BENELUX countries joined France, Italy and Western Germany, to establish the European Coal and Steel Community, whose the main aim was to monitor steel and coal trade, by one joint authority, in order to avoid another war, since weapons are made of steel. This union was enhanced, when a common market was created in 1957, by the Treaty of Rome, then was renamed "European Economic Community" or "EEC". Many other European countries joined the union, so that no tariffs would be imposed on their trade within the EEC area. In addition to the common market, many European countries decided to create a political union, i.e. common political institutions, and free movement of citizens within the Union's Area. The European Union (EU) was then created, in 1993 by the Maastricht Treaty. The EU countries are now trading with a single currency "The Euro" that took effect in 2002.%%

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Welcome to all friends and relatives who are here this evening to celebrate the wedding of Charlotte and Henri. As most of you probably know, I'm the best man of Henri and Im very delighted to offer a toast to the great couple they form. To start, Id like to tell you how Charlotte and Henry met because it makes it easier to understand on what their love is based. They were both at a summer camp for disabled people and didn't know each other before. They had decided to spend a part of their vacation time helping others. Once, they were taking care together of someone in his wheelchair. This person was truly depressed. Charlotte and Henry did their best to comfort and reassured him. At this time, they discovered how deeply they shared the same warmth and purposes of life. This camp was the first milestone of their love affairs. Love of neighbor and common faith are the core value of their amazing couple. Now that they have got married, Im sure, they will remain faithful to these values which are a strong promise that their love lasts a lifetime. Let's toast to the happiness and future of this graceful and marvelous new family.%% To Charlotte and Henry.

There is a big number of big-box stores which have been created since a few decades. For instance, on my island, namely Corsica, there are four big-box stores just for Bastia, a small city on the North of the Island. I think such stores have both positive and negative effects on communities. Concerning the pros, such stores can provide a wide range of products at a price more interesting than those proposed by a little supermarket. Therefore people can afford more things and have more choice. They can buy a lot of things more quickly. Nowadays, the big-box stores also propose the delivery of the products directly at home for free. So, it's really convenient when you are busy. However, at the same time, big-box stores shows cons. First of all, local family-owned businesses often go to bust and go bankrupt because they can't keep up with the competition of the big-box stores. Their prices are too attractive. Secondly, the workers of the big stores get very low wages and they don't benefit from social and health advantages. Nevertheless with the economic crisis I keep thinking that it's always a good thing to have a big-box store around the corner.%% I would like to point out that it allows community to create new jobs and revitalize the economy even though such model of store presents some drawbacks previously mentioned.

There is a big number of big-box stores which have been created since a few decades. For instance, on my island, namely Corsica, there are four big-box stores just for Bastia, a small city on the North of the Island. I think such stores have both positive and negative effects on communities. Concerning the pros, such stores can provide a wide range of products at a price more interesting than those proposed by a little supermarket. Therefore people can afford more things and have more choice. They can buy a lot of things more quickly. Nowadays, the big-box stores also propose the delivery of the products directly at home for free. So, it's really convenient when you are busy. However, at the same time, big-box stores shows cons. In my country, France, what I know about crime might be a widespread prejudice regarding the three partners: delinquents, police, Justice. - Delinquents are younger and younger. As the law does not plan the arrest of children, young offenders use their brothers to commit petty crimes such as purse snapping. Small brothers, a movie by Jacques Doillon, showed it a few years ago. Moreover, delinquents are armed. Arms dealing started during the Bosnian war in 1991 and weapons circulate almost freely in our suburbs. - The police complain about the fact that when they arrest a well-known culprit the justice releases him immediately. They prefer the old time when they fought against criminality: godfathers had laws and were less unpredictable than young individualistic men. Being a policeman is nowadays a very dangerous job. Some districts in our suburbs are in state of civil war with victims in both sides. - The justice tries to find a compromise between protecting the society and not encouraging recidivism. Judges know that jails are a breeding ground for terrorism. They also know that French prisons are incriminated by Amnesty International for their state: they are too small, overcrowded, too dilapidated. Moreover, you find in jail some people who are mentally ill and need medical care.%% (cf Dr Vronique Vasseur)

As far as I remember, I have always lived in cities in which at least one big-box store was located. It is not really a recent evolution of the commercial industry, but it is true that they likely led to the decrease in the number of mom-and-pop shops in the dowtowns. Obviously, people are now used to purchasing their food and cosmetic in the shops of these big retailers, because these companies have succeeded to cut down on the prices when they negociate a large amount of products, with their suppliers. Furthermore, all you need is at the same place and you do not face any problems to park your car for example. To sum up, you gain time and money. However, there are not only pros when a new big-box store sets up in a commercial area. Generally, the market share of the mom-and-pop shops decrease because the craftmen usually apply less attractive prices than the biggest food retailers. In consequence, the social life in the downtown grows poorer, even if some small shops like backeries or drugstores continue to exist. Moreover, the quality of products in the big-box stores is never as good as the one in the craftmen's shops.%% Indeed, you can sometimes hear about food which was not appropriate to the consumption in the big-box stores, because they pushed the prices so lower that the quality would not follow.

text: In France, after high school, you have different ways to continue your studies. Coming from a scientist education, I will describe the two main types of scholarship you can follow after having passed your high school diploma : University and Engineering's school. University Every student is allowed to join a French university, there is no test to take, it is granted for all students if they want to pursue this kind of study. Tuitions are close to zero, it means you will have to pay less than 300 Euros a year. It is really focusing only on study, it means no extracurricular activities, no students trips, no party and so on. Engineering's school There is generally an acceptance test to take and only higher marks students are allowed to register on. Tuitions could be close to zero in certain type of schools and conversely not free in other schools, around 5,000 Euros a year. There are a lot of extracurricular activities, a lot of organized trip and, of course, a lot of party.

Dear Elizabeth, We have decided to go for a flashy ad compaign for Century watches, with a trendy slogan that would sell the brand in no time. Since its establishement, Century brand has been known by many fashion designers and models around the world. In the meantime, many other brands have recently started to spread on the maket , and are adopting a more efficient strategy to conquer the world. That's the reason why we have chosen the slogan "Your glamour, your Century" with the ad featuring a glamorous Marylin Monroe's look alike, wearing a white gown, and surrounded by fans. We can see the star smiling, and facing the audience. This ad compaign will show to the people, that Century watches are still here, and the brand's quality still meets their expectations, more than any other brand, in terms of glamour and reliability. We will give the audience the feeling that, they already have decided to buy a century Watch, by insisting on using "your" in the slogan. The Slogan " Your glamour, your Century" is concise and straight to the point. Please let me know, if I missed any important dtail to emphesize on. Kind Regards, Yacine, Marketing Manager

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: MERCOSUR stands for "Mercado commun del Sur" in Spanish which means Southern Common Market. This is an agreement that was signed in 1991 by five countries: Brazil,Uruguay, Argentina,Paraguay and Venezuela. In 2012 there has been a new member, the state of Bolivia. Some countries have associate member status such as Colombia, Ecuador,Chile... The main objective of MERCOSUR is to promote free trade and a fluid movement of goods, people and currency. However there has been a lot of critics about the effectiveness of the trade agreement in the last years. Some politicians from Argentina or Brazil argue about the real impact about the trade agreement on their respective economies. Indeed, some countries do not respect all the agreements signed and would rather pay fines instead of eliminate completely the import tariffs due to political pressure. The Venezuela is a country that illustrates perfectly the mounting critics about some policymakers about the effectiveness of MERCOSUR.

I believe that in 25 year's time, the technology we already have will become more and more advanced, adding additional functionality to things that we already have, like cellphones. For example, rather than having to show your gym card to use the facilities, retinal scanning is likely to become more common, even with portable devices. Mobile technology is already in the running to replace PCs and desktop computers, and I think more and more people will soon be on board with it. Ideas like GPS autopilot and artificial intelligence are still likely to be in development at that time, although I'm sure many advantages will have been made. The future of the gas-powered car is still uncertain in my mind, since hybrid and small electric vehicules are increasing in popularity even now. Other types of eco-friendly and safety-related technology are bound to increase, especially with the development of things like wearable computing devices. I don't know about you, but I'm really looking forward to a tech-savvy future!

text: My son was at a national  university seven years ago. he often tell me about the differences between university in France and in Germany. As he said, one of the big diffences was the way they learned; In France for example they were much more studens then in Germany. They had more courses with professors, more theory, more difficulties to follow the courses, and no chance to change majors.What in my opinion is a nonsense because if you do'nt feel good in a majors you must have the opportunity to change easily. Another thing, was theu had more contct to theirs professors in Germany, they respected them but on the other side they went with them out for a evening or also for a dinner. In Germany, the studies were more based on practical learning, with some month working in companies. One amazinf thingwas , the companies went to university to hire the students which had alsolutely no problems to find a good job after their studies. My son for example was hired at university by a big company where he is working since he was been gradutated. And top of all, this Autum, he was invited by the university to a big party with some politics for the 30 years of this university.%% A very good experience !

In my opinion green business is definitely one new way of doing business. That's a new type of business that impacts us in our daily life. It is both a new tool from marketing point of view and an intelligent way how to preserve all natural ressources. But I personaly consider that people are too much manipulated. When watching TV, we are given so many information regarding new products, new brands that, in the end, we are almost obliged to buy some of those green products. Some people even if they can't afford buying them will sacrifice some other things in their life in order to be a member of I call " the green community". Marketing concept has created new needs to people. On the other hand I need to admit that, yes, natural ressources are geeting scarce. It's a just a fact we can not ignore it at all. We all know that the worlwide population is drastically increasing. The earth will have to do more to feed that population but we all know that our planete is already under capacity. In that sense, the green industry is doing its part by providing some solutions such as recycling old products, reducing any kind of waste.%% One good example is the recycling of old clothes into new materials to create new products.

I worked at Honeywell Technologies for several years. I learned a lot from that company. For instance I'd like to point out the fact that Honeywell is deeply involved in CSR. Along the years Honeywell demonstrated in many cases that it contributes to help poor people, support education programs of young students or also is able to create a specific fundation in charge to help people who have been impacted by severe climatic conditions such as hurricane, tsunami, earthquakes....I realized that Honeywell is a well-known company by its products and its quality of service but also because it is considered as a company costantly engaged in many volonteer works programs. For sure it is an excellent way to developp this company's image and keep in close touch with a diversified community all over the world. To give a clear and significant example of what Honeywell is able to do, its board decided several years ago to offer possibility to children of its employees to join Honeywell's academy. That program dedicated to young students from the age of 16 to 19, allows them to discover new scientific or engineering programs. We can think it is a win-win situation as those students with their excellent technical background will have later possibility to apply to some vacant positions at Honeywell.%% Serving local community is also a way how to better communicate on the company and creating futur talents of Honeywell.

text: Mr. Smith, I'm writing to update you on our progress. I'm sure you will be glad to know that we have tested the samples and determined that the quality was excellent. Although, we identified an issue with some of the packaging materials, we immediately fixed it. Then, the marketing department sent the samples to sever key partners for further testing. Once we receive the feedback, we will develop several marketing campaigns for your review. We plan to mail the materials to you on July. Alternatively, you could visit our headquarters after July to discuss them. In either case, it's critical that we receive your comments by July 22. I can arrange a meeting to touch base the week of August 8th. Could you let me know whether that works for you ? Best regards, T. Bobbeline

I believe companies have a moral responsibility to give back to society. Let me give you an exemple. Sparkly Cola Soft Drink Co. announced today that it will partner with local schools across the state to provide scholarships for underprivileged high school students to attend college. The Sparkly Cola Scholarship Program will award 20 scholarships in the amount of $10,000 each, starting next year. Scholarships will be determined by local school boards, based on need and academic achievement. The people of New York have made Sparkly Cola one of their favorite soft drinks, and they just felt like it was time for them to give back to communities across the great state of New York," Sparkly CEO Sandy Parks said. "Our donation of $200,000 is a lot of money, but nothing is too good for our kids. And we hope to do even more in the future."

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: A trade agreement always involves at least two countries. It is a set of rules these countries agree to comply with in order to ease import and export of goods. These goods could be raw materials, refined products or manufactured products. To facilitate trade exchange between countries, taxes on imports must be lowered and custom administrative procedures be simplified to reduce delays. But free market is still a subversive issue, critics think that it doesn't help the local industry to develop and it could even be detrimental for small producers.  Sometimes countries have no other choices than to import certain products because of their scarcity especially staples. In addition, importing them gives to the consumer a great variety of products and local producers become more innovative to deliver the best they can on the market. In this way the economy is boosted. The problem is that some countries have difficulties to stick by the agreement when a trade transaction is not profitable for them. That's why it is important that an institution or a group of person be responsible for overseeing the application of the terms of the agreement.

text: I am living in the eurozone and I must say, I consider it ,as a good thing. Living at the german border, we have now the same currency, its easy to compare the prices of the goods. In the 90 s when I went to go shopping in Germany, I had to go to the bank and change money, now we have the same. we can travel easier, we have no more frontiers; More and more the two countries are working together like the police for example. Both states are comparing their livestyles and they are coming closer. Its also easier to study in other European countries. They sign a agreement, and can promote free trade, no more customs. Making deals inside of Europe is very simple and brings also people together.The members of Europe belive that theirs closer economic cooperation will make them stronger face to the rest of the world.

In the 90 s when I went to go shopping in Germany, I had to go to the bank and change money, now we have the same. we can travel easier, we have no more frontiers; More and more the two countries are working together like the police for example. Both states are comparing their livestyles and they are coming closer. Its also easier to study in other European countries. They sign a agreement, and can promote free trade, no more customs. Making deals inside of Europe is very simple and brings also people together.The members of Europe belive that theirs closer economic cooperation will make them stronger face to the rest of the world.

Dear Teacher, You will find below an exerpt of my thought. We are living in a crisis period. Nowadays,  CSR would be a luxury attitude for most of companies. In my opinion, CSR would be: - Taking care of the environment accross a reduction of the polution. - Thinking about the sustainable development. - Improving the quality of life (and of work) of the employes. - Discussing about the work-life balance of their employes. - Helping the society by giving money to charity. - Giving scholarship to attend students to college. - Developing useful projects for the undeveloped countries. - Getting assistance of unemployed and homelessness people. In my country, I am French, it's difficult to find a CSR company. In fact, we have a strong state which take care of the society needs. I'm leaving in a catholic country and by contrast, it's different from the US or UK countries. We have a relatively good quality of life but a high level of taxes. It's the reason why most of the French companies don't agree with CSR. However, weave doing good would be an opportunity to improve their reputation. For example, an employe fired can backfire to his employer. The ecologic organisations can degrade the image of these companies.%% In the future, I will try to leave my job without a negative attitude, to buy time to think about my comportment. Best regards

Dear Ms Hamilton, I looked carefully into the three images and slogans and my final choice is the image picturing two golfers with the slogan: Golf and Century.The perfect team. It was not an easy decision but let's me explain it. Century watches is a luxury brand, and what's better than golf to demonstrate your premium image. Indeed,the fact to be associated to a sport like golf could bring to the company potential customers and raise awareness to the public. Concerning the ad campaign we have foreseen to carry out several tv spot, radio spot and also advertisement in magazines which target customer are men with means over 35 years old. We intent to target all the different ads to your target customer in order to be the most efficient possible. If you have any questions or remarks, feel free to ask. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Kind regards. Elanzize Abdallah

Well first is I start by elaborate the meaning of green business, is a company who is thinking about ecology, it incorporates principles of sustainability, the business is going to create more green products to reduce demand of not green, it is greener than original business. Green business is company or association who is going to think ecology and respect of our environment to conserve it longer. My opinion about this kind of business is simple, every one have to keep chose to believe in what they want and in their own ideas, this kind of company starts to become much more popular, because of a lot of reasons for example "Greenpeace" this corporation give thinking to people and have a big impact, or if I want to go further I can talk about Woodstock 1969 who got a big impact in United States of America with Hippies movement and counterculture lifestyle. For my part i will use some green products because it is good if everyone start to think about ecology but still use others, eat and live green stay kind of "prehistoric" method.

My uncle has always been a reference to me. When he was student, he was poor but hard working. He became now a lecturer at the university, and he has a good salary. The keys of his success are hard working and resilience. He set a target and believed in himself then it paid off. Although many difficulties he goes through during his daily life, he remains positive and optimistic. When he has a problem, he think of its solution instead of its reason. My uncle is always one step ahead in term of making crucial decisions. If there is a difficult situation within our family for instance, he finds a solution straight away as if he has predicted the problem before. My uncle is helpful and generous, but he knows who deserve his help and who does not. He is intelligent and fair. If I could choose a father, It would be him. I wish I could succeed in my life as my uncle did. My hard working and my optimism might be a good start, so I will keep it up until I will reach my goal!

The city of Lyon, formely known as Lugdunum during the ancient rome houses a lot of famous buildings or square to visit. The most famous square is the Place Bellecour which is the largest clear square of Europe with around 62 000 square meters. In the middle of the "Place Bellecour" there is a famous sculpture which is a horse mounted by the king Louis XIV. With regards to buildings, the tallest one is the" Tour du Credit Lyonnais" with rised to 165 meters and has 42 floors. The building is known for his shape of a pencil. In France, this building is the 10th highest skycrapers, the nine other tallest skycrapers or all located in Paris. I cannot finish this article without talking about the Mettalic tower of Fourviere which was built between 1892 and 1894 and has a striking ressemblance with the Eiffel Tower. Nowadays the tower serves only as a television relay tower.

CSR is a difficult question to deal with when you are running a business. For a long time, it was considered as a PR trick: companies gave back money to charities in order to have a good image on their consumers, it was also called "greenwashing" especially for companies who's businesses were involved in environment issues. The best example to me on that point is Total : an oil industry which causes a lot of damage all around the world because of their activity, such as the boat full of oil which broke up among the britain's coast, causing thousands of animals' deaths and a big impact on the local environment. It's now a part of the past: according to me, big companies have no choice now to take their responsabilities: new jobs were created in order to promote their actions and try to improve benefits among this issues.

text: Dear Mr. Jhonn, I'm writing you this email to the problem result of one piece, i order new pieces which must arrive soon and we will finnish the project. Now the good news, we've recieve the propeller and the seller made us a good price which allow to save a lot of money. I have find some potential buyer, i put the list on your desk, they want a meeting with you, you have just to say when and where. Have a good week end.

Dear Elizabeth, After a thourough study we have selected the following ad campaign for Century watches. I'm pretty sure you will love it. First of all many people match Century watches with a certain concept of luxury. That's the reason why we tend to choose the picture representing a man playing to Golf, cause as you know Golf used to be considered as a gentlemen sport. But we would like to enlarge our public target by reaching out also women. In this way we have had an idea that will capture your attention : we wish to have, on the same ad, a professional woman Golf player associated to a man celebrity representing a lot of class, like George Clooney for instance. With the two of them, we could reach out a really large public, from almost all men catagories to an important number of women, doing sports or having a glamour side. Please let me know if this idea has fully caught your attention. Kind regards

Today in the world, we have a serious environment problem. Because of the pollution we have some animals species in danger of extinction or climatic changements which are sometimes dangerous. It is the reason why, in my opinion green business is really important because i believe that it can be one of the best solution to save the earth. The main aspect, in my mind, is energy. We have to use more sustainable energies as solar and wind power and less non renewables energies as fuel or gaz. For example, i have on my roof somes solar panels to produce electricity. A second aspect of the green business really important to me is the consumtion. We need to use more organics feeds avoid to waste. In our life of everyday, we can use recyclable things as paper or towels. We can also sort our trash to permit recyclage. But we need to be carreful, some products aren't really green and we have to informe ourselves.

In my country petty crimes don't send people in jail. If you are caught by police for shoplifting for instance you will just receive a blame, but if you do it several times you will be presented to a judge who will decide whether or not you have to go in prison. However, there is almost no chance that you go in jail. For medium crimes like purse snatching, mug or any kind of such crimes, you will be presented to a judge and probably condamned but due to the fact that prisons are full, you will certainly have to wear an electronic tag. For big crimes, like rape or murder, there will be no escape for you, you will go in jail. If you are just a suspect and before your trial, you will go in custody till the beginning of your process, because you are considered as dangerous for the society.

In my city as I live in the small village, the only renewable energy that would be beneficial will be the solar power and wind power. We don't have an ocean or a strong river in my area, so it will not be possible to use hydroelectricity or tidal energy, even biomass can't be conceivable. Here, some homes have panels solar on their roofs and I have to say that this installation is really costly.  For promoting them to the future owners, it was said that they will save money if they purchase it. The first assumption of this operation was to obtain a tax reduction and the second was to sell the electricity provided by this concept to the government. In fact, the reality is that this investment is too costly and the return on investment is not profitable, because the government of course, dropped the cost of electricity in return. Concerning the wind power I heard that it is not safe for the health, independently the fact of the nuisance of the loudly sound. I think that everyone should take care of our environment by recyclable each thing possible, and try to save energy each time we can do it, because the future does not appear as happy days nowadays.%%

I am living in Marseille which is one of the oldest city in FRANCE. In Marseille we do not have skycraper, but small old building, most of them have less than 5 flours. The city is a succession of small  villages whirh their own characters The tallest building in MARSEILLE is situated by the see, it is the CMA tower owned by the bigest french maritime company. This building is not built to be used for living, it's a business place. More than 2500 emplyees work at the CMa tower. Since the last 3 years a new district is Under construction and will replace ancient factories and ware houses located around the commercial Harbour. Tall building emerge but nothing to do with Shanghai. Even if the city is cosmopolite and open to various culture it remains a regional metropole Economic turn down and unemployment limit the development of business and commercial centers.

In my country, the maxmum punishment for a criminal is to be sentenced for life empriosonment. All criminals even those who have done petty crimes have to be judged. Because of the increasibng numbers of crimes, Poeple involved have sometimes to wait for months before being judged. Another major issue, is life conditions in jaIL. Actually the number of places available in jail is not sufficient in comparison with the number of criminals supposed to be emprisonned. The result is that prisons are overloaded. Where prinsoners should be 2 per rooms, they have to share their cell with 3 or 4 others guys. this creates por conditons of Hygiene and difficulties for the staff working for security. A report was recently sent to rthe governnement in order to force it to take appropriate measure to ensure that kife conditions in jail are respecting human ritghs the Ministry of justice proposed to replace enprisonnement by other s maesures for petty crimes. Theses measure should emphazise rehabilitation.

In French cities, the buildings are a bit older than in other countries such as China or Duba. Firts, if you go to Paris, you will see a lot of old-fashion buildings downtown. They reflect the previous architectural French styles, such as Baroque or Renaissance. Buildings are not tall, and they are a lot of windows in each appartment. Then, if you go to the museums, you could have an idea about what France looks like in the past. The Louvre is a formal royal palace, and the Muse d'Orsay a former trainstation. Each of them have been refurbished to create a place where famous artists and exhibitions are host. I'd say that the most common line of the Parisians buildings are their windows, and their old-fashion look. Museums or houses are sometimes very similar, and it makes Paris as one of the most beautiful city in the world. A place where modern and old architecture can live together.

Dear Elizabeth, We have selected a ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice. Many people think that Century is a classic ans well-know brand, cause it has featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true that customers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's why we have managed to be one of the top worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity in the campaign. However, we decided that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement.  For this reason, we decided the ad would feature a man playing golf with a message not directed to stars ans athletes but directed to each and every man that will read and look at our ad. "Golf ans Century, The perfect team". Everybody should understand. I look forward to hearing from you. Best regards...

This topic is very interesting because I do trust that success is linked with passion and vision.  The movie "Jobs" is a good reminder about successful people ! In my mind, I do admire Justin Timberlake. He owes his success to his talent but also to his passion. Singer and Dancer he is today an Actor, a Model, a Business Man, a Charity Union's President... On the other hand I do admire my mother because she believes in my brother and I and boost us in any of our projects life to get success. My younger brother has been studying design for 6 years and he is currently in India to perform his skills and knowledge. I am sure that his passion and curiosity could make him different to be a success person too. To sum up, the success persons I admired are from different background but as one things in common : passion !

Ideally, success would mean a complete achievement of a professional and/or personal project. It could bring notoriety, financial benefits, or 'something' special that everyone could admire. As an example, Steve Job is a model of success and everyone knows his story, his ability to lead a company to a planetary commercial success. Consequently, everyone knows his failures, his good and bad habits. In Steeve Job's cases, his success is so focused on his person and his mind than his life seems to miss some equilibrium. In reality, success is probably more complex. Taking example about a colleague of mine, Joaquim C. working as a marketing manager, it is possible to achieve a great carrier inside a big company and to stay quiet anonymous regards to the world. The traits of his success can be summarized as a balance between several success : individual development, recognition of his peers, ability to lead projects successfully through crisis, commitments of his colleagues to his goals, familly balance and healthy body. Unlike Steve Job, Joaquim as met success but anonymously. Therefore, success is really a matter about what you expect in your life and what you intend to do.

First of all, i would like to pinpoint what success is. To my mind, it's when you reach hapiness helping the other to do the same. Moreover, to insure hapiness for a long time, we should find a good balance in the most important parts of our life (family, social, business). Once this enlightened, a successful person for me has first a vision (target) wich make him move in a constant way; secondly, failure isn't so much a problem for him and what helps to be resilient, persistent, is to juge a failure as an experience and surely not as a bad feeling of yourself. As well as you project yourself in the futur, you have to enjoy the ride as she said and to be present to the present: it means, to be aware of what happen now and therefore let your intuition work. If i summariz in one sentence, i'd like to say that if you listen and respect what you feel and think good inside yourself, you will do a very good job for you and for the others, in your job, family and social life.

Green businesses do their part to conserve natural resources as much as possible. They may operate their machinery using renewable power  such as solar, wind or hydro  reducing the dependence on fossil fuels. Some environmentally conscious businesses install green roofs to positively affect energy consumption and provide a clean, vibrant break area for employees. Green businesses use organic, recycled, reusable or biodegradable materials as part of their business processes, such as furnishing the office or making copies; or to manufacture products, including food, paper products or packaging. People are increasingly attracted to the concept of sustainability. As a result, many go out of their way to support green business and buy green products. There are down sides to this trend, one of which is that some businesses claim to be green or sell green products even though their practices and materials have no environmentally friendly qualities. In doing so, they sacrifice ethics for profit. In fact, some people have become so disgusted by 'greenwashing' efforts that they are put off by the entire green industry.

I saw on internet this morning a dreadful news. The story is about a baby who is between 15-23 months and was discovered in the boot of the car. She was found in her excrement, naked, underfed,  dehydrated, ready to die and of course wasn't able to speak any word. The facts reported from the media were that the woman who was in trouble with her car's indicators came to a garage to repair it. The guy heard some strange groaning coming from the back of the car and took the advantage to turn mother's attention for opening it.  The mother and father were arrested and the woman said to the police officer that she had hidden this poor creature since her birth. The drunk husband pretended not knowing anything about the pregnancy of her wife! This horror happened in my country few days ago. How could it be! Here we are! Not only it is at the page of horrors, but where the family, neighbor, husband were? I still don't understand and realize how in our century,  people could be selfish at this point and still focus on their navel.  Should we not ask ourselves the question how to go on?

When its about talking about renewable energy, my country seems always to be absent. Indeed our politicians think that we have bigger problems to deal with, like economic crisis. In my opinion all, if we make the good choice, renewable energy could be a trigger to our economy. I know that the first solution we have in mind for renewable energy is solar energy. Although it has a lot of advantages, he has one of the worse inconvenience: a high installation costs. So if we want to install solar energy, we shall invest a lot of money. For a country like ours it is a high brake. As far as Im concerned, I think we should choose a renewable energy like biomass. In fact biomass is relatively low cost and it just requires forest residue. Like you know we have a lot of forest. The government should just use his power to collect and reuse all forest residue in order to generate biomass.

Who doesn't want to be successful? What does success mean to you ? I think its best to start by defining success. Are you thinking about money or ability or friendship or education or teaching or family. If you have an idea for a business, if you are really passionate about it, if you still believe its the right thing to do, just do it! Business is simple, which isnt saying its easy. It could be an amazing success but also a failure. In a way, success and failure are inextricably linked. The biggest difficulty would be to you to know when you have to pivot or to adapt. On the other hand, opening your own business could be a life-enhancing experience to you. However, we are always speaking about business. There are many different types of success and many different ways which lead to success. What manner of success are you looking for exactly and with what kind of qualities ? Are you creative, innovative, incredibly passionate, persistent, are you able to bounce back from adversity. I think that the harder individual works, the most theyre successful and rewarded.

I live near Paris, the nicest and the most beautiful town over the world. Beautiful because of the size of buildings and avenues. Tipically, buildings are six or seven-story high and were built in the mid 19th century. We have a curious tower designed by Mister Gustave Eiffel and built from steel in 1887.That became the most important symbol of Paris in the world. This old-fashioned Eiffel Tower is about 320 meters high with antennas. Nobody's living in. We just find a famous restaurant on the top and a lovely place to admire the beautiful townscape and to dream about a fabulous date with a really nice young woman. Moreover, we can see the Tour Montparnasse built from steel and glass. Its a dark glass building for business and housing situated near the main rail station inside of Paris. The tallest buildings are built in the area called La Defense outside of Paris. Most of them are business buildings for all kind of companies. Many modern towers were designed over there for a huge amount of business workers. This area makes you feel like youve been kidnapped by a strange community of people, over worked and over stressed, moving around a large esplanade like an antique meeting.%%

Dear Mister X, Please find herewith the advertising campaign's description, we have chosen for our Century brand. The advertising agency had presented three projects which were interesting. The ad campaign "your glamour, your Century" seems to be the most relevant for the following reasons. A watch is much more than a simple device to read the time. Its chiefly a status symbol and it's has to do with self-image. Because Century is a luxury brand, our market targets are wealthy people or these one who wants to look like wealthy. Therefore our campaign has to make our future customer dream. Featuring a well-known star, drinking Champagne, surrounded by reporters, it allows our target audience to project itself into an attractive life. The message is easy to understand: If you buy a Century watch you will be similar to beautiful people. Celebrity endorsement is much more important than the quality of the watch which is self-evident. We have to be aware that we are dream makers. So it's not necessary to focus on the product but only on the image which is connected with. That is why we believe that the slogan "Your glamour, your Century" and the picture supporting it will be the most efficient.%% Best regards

Sun and wind power We can change our lifestyle in saving more power. What a lot of my neigbours did the last five years,  is investing in sun power panels on theirs roofs to warm they water and the rest of power , they sell it to EDF the energy company in our country. My city is situated on the hills so we can use to produce wind energy. Every year, our city istalls new wind turbines round the city. We have about 200 days of wind a year, so it's very effectiv. it's save,  environementally friendly and economic too. At the begining a lot of people were not seeing it positively but now, more and more are accepting it. Finally the turbines are not so lound people was thinking and they don't disturb the deers who are living in our forest. I know even two families which installed theirs oun turbines to produce all theirs electricity for theirs houses and for the farms which are near theirs houses. Next thing we are supposed to work on, is the electric automotive industry. We are on the begining, our postcars, clean companies local radio, TV  and other public station are already using it.%%

In this article I will talk about Barack Obama a person that I really admired for many reasons. First of all, he is really charismatic. I am always been amazed by his presence on stage. Everybody seems to listen to him carefully. Even during the different debates with Romney or McCain I always felt that he has some kind of innate talent. Secondly, I admire his career path, I have the chance to read one of his autobiography  and I was completely passionated about his life.There is a lot elements I did not know about him, like the fact he liked to party a lot when he was young but decided to stop at a certain time and dedicate his life to school to be a better student. Due to the fact that I am still a student, this really help me to motivate myself and set goals that I will never have thinked before. Finally I believe that being a good person with your family and friends can really help you in your business career.

Buildings in France are an amazing blend of modern and traditional architecture. For example in Paris, you can admire the Louvre Palace which was built in the 12th century.The main building and its two wings are made of light yellow stones. They are surrounding a strange glass design reminding of a greenhouse without tomatoes : the Louvre Pyramid. Even if you are modern design friendly, you can't prevent from avoiding walking past it. In the heart of the city, when going for a walk, you can go past the big Hausmann buildings. Even if those rough apartment houses belongs to a luxurious era, they are lacking of refinement. In the same way, the skyscrapers in La Defense area are not very attractive. Considering the crowding of buildings,  the place I prefer in Paris is certainly the Trocadero Esplanade, a large area without any buildings, only some gardens and a few fountains. A place where you can stroll and rest from the crushing presence of the overwhelming buildings.

Hydroelectricity To answer the issue of nonsutainables energies ued in our city. I believe that hydroelectricity is the best way to participate in the world movement to save our planete against polution and to reduce the use of fossil fuel. Hydroelectricity is produced through use of the gravitational force of falling or flowing water. And with that, we can provide "green" electricity to all the houses in the city. To install that, we need a strong river with a dam built across It. I have imediatly think about the river which flow near the village. We have all what need hydroelectricity, why don't use it ? Hydroelectricity no required fuel costs and relatively low building and maintenance costs. With my research, i deduce that it will cost the city $75,000 ( one of the cheaper sustanable energy). To finnish, i believe that hydroelectricity can be a way to give a good image to the city ( an "eco-city"). Iam really concerned about saving the planete and you should be to!

Protecting environment has become, nowadays, a major issue. So, green businesses should take part in this challenge, in order to protect the environment, as well as to guarantee green and environmentally friendly products. First of all, they should use renewable energy, and reduce using energy, generated from fossil fuels. Why not installing solar panels, on the buildings' roofs of their sites? That would generate renewable energy for their internal use. In addition, businesses' offices should reuse, for many purposes, printed paper sheets, which still have blank sides, so that more paper will be saved, and less waste will be thrown away. In terms of production, in order to sell their products, businesses should use the most credible guarantees, to clear up consumers' doubts, on the green origin of their products. As a consumer, I do not really go out of my way to buy green products. I occasionally buy reusable dishes, such as stainless steel plates. I also buy biodegradable products, like paper towels. I do that just for good sense. As for food, for instance, I prefer organic chicken to massively farmed chicken. That is just a matter of taste. But for all the green activists beware of green-washing!%%

Dear Teacher, I would like to have the pleasure to say that the successful person I admire is myself, unfortunately it's wrong. Besides it's difficult to find at work a person that I admire exept perhaps my CTO. The most important trait that I admire is entrepreneurship. It rather difficult for me to understand how a single person can create his own business and later being the boss of his own company. Moreover I admire all the researcher and their capability to invent something that nobody in the world has already discovered. In addition, the people who take care of the others as a volunteer is the kind of person I would like to be. However I am too busy to spend time as a volunteer. In my point of view, the most successful story is the adventure of Bill Gates. He was a great inventor, entrepreneur and businessman. He was the best in his job and had a great idea with the creation of "windows". Plus, he was capable to have his operating system mandatory for the all the computers all over the world. In fact, he had an incredible sense of business. Best regards

To my mind green business represents all differents actions implemented by companies in order to be environmentally friendly. For instance, a corparation can have a program to save energy inside their buildings or to avoid waste of papers and furnitures. I think green business is taking a greater part in our life because of the problem of climate change and the increasing number of inhabitants of Earth. Indeed, statitics stated that the number of inhabitants in 2040 will amount to around 9 billion people. So we will have to use the same natural ressources with a soaring number of inhabitants. That is why it is crucial that environmental issues become a top subject in all countries and not only in the most developed one. However, paradoxically I do not go out of my way to buy green products. The main reason is that they are more expensive that regular products and I am still a student. Besides I have some doubt about some companies which pretend to sell environmentally friendly products.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: In my country we can notice two types of Universities: private and public. These two types have the same main goal: to educate students. Except their goals we can notice only another one similarity, they use sometimes the same teachers. These two points are all they have in common. In general the number of students in public universities is bigger than the one in private university, therefore we have a very wide campus in public universities. Unfortunately public universities have not enough resources to manage all these students. Beside the education in public universities is very tough, to succeed in these universities the students have to do a lot of work. By comparison, private universities give a better education due to his little number of students and to his bigger resources. In fact private universities are more expensive and they have good material to help students to succeed. We can find in it many libraries, many computer rooms and teachers are always availables.

I think the most successful people prioritize their lives to reach their goals,spend their times on what's important for achieving their dreams.But often they are in a dilemma.I speak about the overfocus that can have negative effects.Overemphasis solely on one important thing can lead to you cause detrimental impact on other things,like family,relationships,a social life and hobbies.It's like a coin with two sides....I think that significant trait of successful people is to be passionate and believe in yourself,be optimistic-because often failure goes with success.One of the most important thing is understand and analyze failure.It's important to be ready to pivot ,be ready to adapt to particular ,changing situation.Specially for tough business world is important to have the right idea to take on the world and go up against your competitor.Good business all comes down also to good recruitment,to reliable team.

I believe companies have a responsability to society, but like eveybody has. I mean, I cant stand the concept of irresponsibily, and Im deeply convinced that, whoever you are, whatever you do, unless you live alone in a remote island, you have a responsability to society. To my mind, the only difference lies in the spread of the aftermath. Therefore, the companies responsability is higher because they are more powerful, and all what they do impacts in a vaster medius. In one hand, companies cant dismiss their shareholders, to the extent that their money fuels their investments. Without them, they would belly up quickly and many employees would be laid off. Nevertheless, they must not consider only the shareholders interest. Too often, you hear about companies which shut down a factory in a country where workconditions are respectful of human dignity, in order to recreate it in another one, where children underfed will work at no cost, and in unsafe conditions. Thats outrageous. In the same way, it occurs that companies harm the environment deliberately to increase their profits. Thats outrageous as well. Therefore consumers must be vigilant and aware of their own responsability. Its up to everyone  not only to companies  that our world doesnt become a dog eat dog world.%%

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Hello, As we spoke this morning, I made the few things you asked me for. It won't be long before I receive the first feedbacks from  our colleagues about what they would like to prepare for our boss's departure. God, I think you too can't even imagine how it will be withouth him in the company. He's here for years and he taught us everything. Anyway, I think it will be some kind of party at work, then we might invite Karl at the restaurant with us all and his wife. Carrie told me last thime I saw her he doesn't even imagine we could prepare such a thing for him. I can't wait,  We keep in touch,  Bye

I live in France. There are not a lot of tall buildings in my country. It is a more traditional architecture with litlle buildings. In Paris, the capital, there is a district called "La Dfense". It is a recent district with some high buildings especially for multinational companies. However there is an unsual monument that is a well-known model of architecture: "La Tour Eiffel". The Eiffel Tower was built in the 1880's for the Universal Exposition. It was an awful construction, very ingenious and bold for this period of time. Fortunately, the Eiffel Tower has not been dismembered and it became the symbol of Paris and France. French people and tourists are happy to see it ans to have the possibility to visit it. Although there are a lot of taller buildings, the Eiffel Tower is special and with the lights that enlighten it at night, it is just magic, as a lighthouse.

Sustainable development is a very innovative idea: it appeared in 1987 when it became obvious that there would be no more petroleum left in 2040 for children born in 2000, and no more natural gas to celebrate their retirement. Green business became an excellent business for some lobbies. Lets have a look on wind power for example.  Its main quality is that it is renewable. In the name of this superiority to fossil fuels, we are supposed to forget a few drawbacks. Is it consistent? No. Is it storable? No. Is it pleasant to live near a wind turbine?  No. Does it embellish our landscape? No. How many birds are killed thanks to these windmills? Last but not least: how much does it cost really? In France, it is very interesting to get its own windmill because EDF  is compelled to buy your electricity a good price and who pays the bill? Certainly not the wind companies which are the great winners of the operation! When I hear the nice people in their boat talking about paper towels, I am not surprised, just a little disappointed. The green  new religion has its rituals. Make small gestures and you will be saved and dont think too much on global problems of energy.%%

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: I choose again to discuss the similarities and differencies between the educational system in my homeland (Morocco) and Japan. If we just compare the infrastructures and the organization established in primary schools in Japan and those in Morocco, we will be shoked. Schoolchildren in Japan studies in electronic e-book and everyone of them have tablet so it can be easy for them to transport their school fournitures everyday. They also have electronic educative games that helps them to stimulate memory and curiosity to learn. In Morocco, each student have to transport on his back daily around 14 kg of books, and once he get to schools, he must search for a place to sit down among the large numbre of students (first challenge) and then listen to the teacher. Also, he has access to very few extracurricular activities which is not the case with his Japanese counterpart. This last, has also time to visit museum, do school trip to visit other countries and cultures. Comparing the incomparable make me sometimes sad, but this is the hard reality.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Hello everybody. As everybody knows, we are here today to celebrate the wedding of Guillaume and Julia. I would like to take a few minutes of your time to make a toast to this wonderful couple. Guillaume and I are long time friends and as some of you knows, we have always considered each other as a brother. I would especially thanks Guillaume and Julia's parents for this wonderful ceremony. I remember a time when we arrived at the airport at the end of our holidays, waiting for check-in when the hostess explained us that our number of flight was the good one but the plane took off the day before. Both of us were flat broke and we started a 2,000 Kms Hitch-hiking to go back to our home. Started a wonderful and surprising 2 days trip on the road. That's why I like him, a character balanced between spontaneity and fun. Always keep a good mood, whatever things are. Guillaume and Julia, I'm glad to be here, even proud, and I hope you the best for the future. To Guillaume and Julia !

The most of the part of successful persons should have specific traits and habits in the business world. On the one hand, they must be ambitious. So it means that they want to get a highly position in their job without losing their mind. On the other hand, they must possess qualities which could allow them to reach their goals. They're very proactive. They try to anticipate problems before they happen. No matter what happens, they never gives up. They are very persistent when faced with adversity. They have a great ability to find pragmatic solutions to problems. So they should be clever about their relationships with their subordinates. They have to use and to show them that they imagine the best ideas to develop the functioning of the company without asserting them. Like that, they could get success with others. The business world is tough. They have to be resilient ans innovative thinkers.

In France, the juvenile justice system  is based on the fact that young delinquents need assistance. In addition, their level of responsibility in a crime is mitigated due to age. In order to take these facts into consideration, a special juridiction was established since 1945 for young people under 18, where all cases ranging from petty crimes like shoplifting and purse-snatching, to violent crime like rape and murder are processed. Most of sentences in this juridiction  are education oriented especially for minor under 13, but minor between 13 and 18 could in special cases face criminal punishment. Though rehabilitation has been the main objective of the french juvenile justice, recent policies have shown a trend towards the toughening of sentences : A lot of money have been invested by the national government to build more juvenile detention center to allow more incarcerations. Plus, there has been suggestions from  lawmakers to extend detention times. A trend fuelled by an increased rate of juvenile crimes and the public fear generated by the sensationalisation of these events by the media. In the mean time, this trend is meeting a strong reaction of people thinking that these changes are a regressive move towards methods of handling social problems belonging to the past.%%

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: To start with this writing exercise , I would like to underline that I belong both to the French culture and to the Corsican one. Even though Corsica is a part of the French territory, it should be noted that the culture and the ways how people communicate between each other, are a little bit different from the French culture. In general people use both direct and indirect communication. It depends mainly on the context and the relationship between the persons. For instance, at work we use more the indirect speech to ensure friendly contact and polite relationships whereas at home we use more often the direct communication especially when brothers and sisters are speaking together. No need to soften the requests or to avoid conflict, we can be directly honest without hurting the listener. Concerning the body language and the gestures that are common in my culture, we can mentioned that the thumbs-up is really appreciated to encourage or congratulate someone. However the use or rising the third finger should not be used because it is seen as a really bad insult against someone both in French and in Corsica. The V-sign is also a positive sign but tapping your head is frowned up because it may have two contradictory meaning (positive and negative).%% Finally body language and gestures are more important in Corsica. You should keep a soft eye contact and a kind tone and everything would be perfect!

Wind power In my opinion, wind power would be the most effective form of natural power to meet our city's energy needs. As you know, our city is locared by the sea. We have three hundred and sixty-five windy days a year. Wind power creates no waste and doesn't use any fossil fuels or other kinds of finite energy. It is low-cost and highly profitable. The cost of installing wind turbines would come to only 80,000 dollars, and the maintenance costs would be low. It would be better than solar power, as it creates less carbon, and it would be more efficient than using a 'natural living machine' because the wind turbines would need fewer people to look after them, and therefore would be more cost-effective. All in all, as far as I'm concerned, wind power would be the most environmentally friendly and economically viable way to produce renewable energy for our city.

There are many kinds of success and I chose to pay tribute to a young student that I know well, since shes my daughter! Indeed, last year, she began to study medicine and prepared for the competitive exam, which is very selective in France. She hadnt a tremendous ability, but shes a shining example, which reveals significance of willpower to succeed. Driven by a huge yearning, her paramount quality was to be persistent. Nothing could deflect her from her objective, neither tiredness, nor failures. When she was disappointed, she always bounced back from it and never gave up. She knew that all was up to her. To reach her objective, she sacrificed all the common pleasures of her age without complaining. The second key of her achievement was her ability to pinpoint an efficient work method and never change it. Besides, without being cocksure, she always kept confidence in herself and thought she was able to be part of the successful candidates. Thanks to these qualities, she overcame a tough obstacle, but she knows its only a stage, and many others await them. Plus, she must not forget that she hasnt but to succeed in professional life to succeed in her life.%%

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Mr. X I have just received the complete file regarding the bid that you have done to the city of Bulka.I'm going to review it as quickly as possible. I'll give you by e-mail a complete legal advice next Wednesday, at the latest. I'll try to explain why they rejected your proposal from the perspective of the public procurement law. Meanwhile you must be aware that the legal framework has changed in France for the extra E-C companies since 2004. They have to justify that they comply with the social and environmental European regulation. Once, prices and quality of the tender were the main selection criterias. It's no longer the case. In order to be able to give you a pertinent advice, please send me before Friday your social report and any additional information which could bring to light that your company is involved in a socially responsible approach. Its critical that I receive all these pieces of information by the end of the week. If necessary, I can set up an appointment in our offices the second week of January to discuss the point. Could you let us know whether that's convenient for you?%% I truly appreciate your business and remain at your entire disposal for any question. Best regards

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: I have a friend who's attenting a university in the United States. We often discuss the similarities and differences between university life in the US and in our country. One of the big differences is flexibility. My friend tells me it's fairly common to change your major in the US. This is not the case in my country. Unlike the US system, it is very difficult to change your major. Being able to easily change majors makes sense to me, as a person's interests might change as well. Another thing my friend tells me that's different is the level of formality. Even though professors are respected, they can also be your friend. Students in some classes actually socialize with their professors. By comparison, I don't think I would ever go to a party or restaurant with one of my teachers. Just so you don't think everything is different, my friend tells me that students in America are pretty much like students in our country. They want to get a good education so they can get a good job and have a happy life, but they also want to have fun. That's something I think we can all agree on.%%

If I speek about green business, for me it's the sale of products which are environment friendly. It also can be products which are produce in our area or country, not at the other end of the world. To be green I think the industrustry has to think about the packaging which often is too much for me. For example when I do my shopping , I always use a bag which I can be reutilised. I need my car to go to work every day but I try not to take it if not necessary. I also have a little garden where I plant  my own vegetables. I buy always tissurs which are recyclable. In my neighborhood many people have solar panel on their roofs; I taught to myself if I have to change my heating I'll also buy a green one which runs with air power and I'll put some solar panels on my roof too. I mean if a company built a new building, they also have to take care to utilise green material which are safer for our planet. My point is if everyone kate a little more care of what I buys and hopely what he puts in the rubish we can go ahead.%%

Big-box stores are becoming more and more present in our cities. I think most people enjoys the fact that there are more big-box stores. Indeed it is way more convenient to shop there given that there is everything you want to buy. From a business perspective, the rising numbers of big-box stores has definetely a negative impact on small companies. This phenomenon could affect the life of a large number of people . Another dilemma caused by the increasing numbers of big-box stores is the environnement. Indeed, as all big-box stores are located at the center of the town, people who do not have cars will have difficulty to have access to these stores. In that sense, that is why small business are appreciated in small community because it is way more convenient to take the car and drive during 30 to 40 minutes. However, some huge companies which own these big stores are creating small stores in order to respond to these customers who do not want to shop in these huge stores.

A lot of people would like to be successful but if so much people are writing about this topic, it is clearly because no one has found a recipe! It is true that one can observe some traits in a majority of successful people: be self-confident, motivated, creative and innovative... But it is not so simple. According to me, success doesn't only depends on you. The support of your family and your friends to believe in your capacities and to help you to realize your dreams is important. Then, if you are born in a wealthy or a successful family, it could be easier too. In this case, your family brings you the money you need to begin your project or can make you meet the people you need. Another point could be destiny and chance. Sometimes, you have the good idea at the good time or by chance you meet exactly the people you were needed to meet! This last point seems very important because in a lot of interviews of successful people they are talking about 'chance'... so, are you born on a lucky star?

In my faraway country you can find plenty of strange buildings, traditional and modern, pink, red and yellow skyscrapers with funny windows, and huge main entrances.  My country, located in the planet X2.0, is famous for housing the tallest skyscraper of the Universe. This skyscraper called as the MONSTER is more than 10 000 of height from the sidewalk  without taking into account flagpole, functional structures as the antenna that reaches more than 30 meters. The Monster's architectural height is so fascinating that people are coming all around the Universe to visit it. From the sidewalk you can't even see the top of the building, and you've have that strange feeling that the all building is moving slowly from one side to another.  You can find also more traditional buildings, smaller but more welcoming and warming making of vibrant british red bricks. Since humanity decided to shift towards more renewable energy and eco-frendly infrastructures, in my country you'll find buildings covered by  solar panel, with organic garden on the roof. Nowadays some of the buildings are self-governing using photosynthesis energy. Well to conclude in my word, every buildings deserve to be visited.

Have you ever heard the most colossal blunder I made? While doing my military service as a navy commissioned officer, I was attending an official dinner offered by the French Consul General in Bruges. We were in an extremely good mood, champagne was excellent and I didn't pay attention to the different people who were introduce to myself. They were ten people at my table. I knew most of them. Officers of different NATOs Countries with their wives were there. A young woman who I had never met before was also at our table. We were talking about a wide range of issues. The conversation went across the different kinds of naval weapons which were used by our warships. At the point, I said laughing and smiling: 'We French, are very happy what happened during the Falkland war. Our Exocet anti-ship missiles have proven that the French one were the best when they destroyed the HMS Sheffield". Suddenly, a leaden silence fell over the table. I didnt know that the woman was the widow of a British officer who was killed in action when the Sheffield was hit by Argentinian exocet missile. I was devastated. Unintentionally I had been so deeply offensive and nasty.%%
