I live in France. Buildings in France can be really different, and you will see : - farms, everywhere in the countrysides, - private houses, in small towns or in the countrysides (and sometimes in the middle of nowhere), - buildings, with 3 or 4 floors, around city centers - towers, which have more than 10 floors and are usually grouped in a same area.  Of course, as France is a country with a lot of History, you will be able to see many of historic buildings in town centers. These buildings are classified by the department of Historic Monuments and cannot be modified or destroyed without their agreement.  What is the tallest or unusual architecture in France ? The Eiffel Tower in Paris of course !  As you certainly know the Eiffel Tower was built by Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 Exposition Universelle, which was to celebrate the 100th year anniversary of the French Revolution. Each year, almost 7 million of visitors come to see it, especially for its panoramic view of Paris from its top (really magic at night !) The Eiffel Tower is 324 metres tall (with its antennas) for a total weight of 10 tonnes.

It is difficult to pinpoint what are the typical traits leading to success. I could be more concrete by taking an example of a real success story. For instance, we could talk about my friend Nathalie, who recently opened a caf in our home city. That of course required courage to face the bankers and convince them to let her take a loan in their institution, but also passion to be able to work overtime every day of every week, in order to make it profitable. I think realizing this type of plan also require proactivity, because her project was very well planned. This goes ahead with the fact to be innovative because the initial idea, the concept, had to be brilliant. Moreover, creativity has to be highlighted, because she managed to set up a nice place with very little financial means and a lot of ressourcefulness. Let's hope it will be profitable !

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Josh Just to give an update about my strategic plan. I have already finished the three main parts: firstly define vision, mission and core values, secondly set up global firms objectives and thirdly collect the principal indicators. As the staff doesnt like change, I had some difficulties, furthermore I didnt have enough resources. However I have finished these parts in the delay. Now I am ready to continue. I just need your involvement. In fact to fully complete the task, you have to be involved during all the process. Your presence will give more confidence to the staff and could trigger the success of this process. If you agree with me, I'll schedule two meetings for the next week between you and the staff. By the way, find attached the agenda of this meeting. If you have some adjustments or comment about that, I will be pleased to give you some responses. Best regards Stephane

In my opinion, corporate social responsability is an important concept. Indeed, I do believe that companies have a strong impact on its peoples' lives and on society thus a responsability. On the one hand, a job is an important part of a peolple's life. It is a part of his identity, his public identity, his role in the society. I think most people are really involved in their company, it seems logical that companies should be involved in their people's welfare too. An employee who is taken care of will give 100% of his capacity to the company. The workforce efficiency can be really enhanced this way. CSR also concerns environmental responsability. I think companies could play a big role in climate change slowdown, if they wanted to, or ought to. Maybe some changes in legislation would enable waste reduction, hazardous chemicals ban, energy savings, and a lot more. One the other hand, the first goal of a company is profit and economical profitability. Shareholders will always pressurize to increase their rate. Finally, I still think it would not be tragic for big companies and their shareolders to reduce their profit by 0.01% to spend the money on CSR.%%

Let me tell you a little story about Patrick. Patrick is a good worker. He likes to be at work early in the morning, before everyone so he can do a lot work without interruption. To achieve this goal, he always arrive at the office at 07h00 AM. Then he can work alone for at least one hour. Everything was fine until one day. This day the government has decided to do some work in the main road between his house and his office. Since this day the traffic in this road became very difficult. In fact the work on the road caused a lot of traffic jam. Patrick was now unable to be at work at time. To fix this situation, he decided to quit his house earlier, at 06h00 AM. He though that he will have a chance to be on time. But even at 6h00, there was already traffic jam. After one week of trying, he finally decided to quit at 05h00 am. For the first time there was not any traffic jam, he was very happy and excited to finally have his own moment at the office. Arriving at the office this first day, he just noticed he had forgotten his office's keys.%%

Corporate social responsability is one of the main issue related to company's governance in the XXI century. Nowadays people start to be aware of the environmental and social impact companies' actions have on the local community. In this context, CSR became a top priority for firms that wanted or had to improve their image. Beyond the branding issue, the very meaning of CSR is not just about bordering your impact on the society or improving work conditions but being truly concerned with the environment within the one your company is developping. Therefore, according to CSR definition, company's responsability goes far beyond its shareholders. Looking at the exemple of Veolia, one of the biggest worlwide water reclycling company, one could argue that CSR deals with the overall production channel, thus tacking into account not only your own impact on society but as well your suppliers' impacts. Indeed Veolia has invested in improoving working conditions of its suppliers and has also supporting water reclycing systeme in restricted aera. Beyond the positive impact of clean water free acces from a social and environmental point of view, the idea was to develop local economy using local workforce and local suppliers.

Rennes is a city built in middle Age. Today, still some roads are paved. Down town, buildings are not very tall, the average is 3-4 stages. Two tours of an old castle delimite the old area. We could say Rennes is a city which has not decided whether it should stay small or big. For instance there are no big avenues nor skyscrapers. But the differentes period of construction are presents. Unfortunately a fire happened in the 15th century so very little remains from before since all construction were in wood that time. Then we have some monuments from classical period, with carved stones on the faces. In the last thirty years, buildings have developped a little every where in the perifery of the city. One of them built in the 70's is famous today for its bold architecture conveying balconies as eggs.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Hi Everybody, First of all, I would like to thank you for coming here and celebrating the union of marriage of this totally wonderful young couple! My name is Franck and I am one of the best friend of the groom. Obviously, you should think I am going to toast Bertrand, and you are completely right! Indeed, we know each other since for such a long time from now on, that I could share many very funny stories about him...But I prefer to keep the strangest secret...After all he does not exactly married yet! However, I sometimes remember the time we spent on the benchs of the High School. Of course, he used to cheat on me during the exams and was probably the first to avoid working his houseworks. But, he knew how to put a very great atmosphere, and used to be the first to suggest a lot of activities such as sport, entertainment or trips! What I can say to the bride, is that she made an absolutely amazing choice, for sure. I am used to being proud of him, he is loyal, dynamic, smart and devoted to the people he likes. To conclude, I wish the best to both of them and I would like people here to raise their glass to the bride and the groom!%%

In my country like others we have many type of crimes. We have petty crime which include stealing, murders and many others. Today, our government has decided to open a very big war against politic corruption. In fact corruption in my country is has very badly affected our economy and our life. Perhaps it is the main cause of our economic crises. Two years ago our president has created a special team to fight this corruption. The punishment depend on the importance of the crime. Sometimes, the criminal is obliged to reimburse all the amount in cause, and goes every time in jail for at least 5 years. Today we have a lot of politicians who are now in jail because of corruption. The population hope that this war is fair and not only conduct for personal or political reasons. For the first time we can see some politicians being afraid to show up their illegal fortune, and maybe doing more professionally their job.

What is a successful person? I can't make a complete and global definition. It depends of to many things.Some people believe that success and money are linked, while others consider that a successful person is a person who can help the others, everyone have his definition of success. But i am going to give you few aspects which are to me, the most important to be a successful person. In my mind, a successful person have inevitably to be motivated and passionated, because it is really hard to succeed in general and it necessite sacrifice. In the way of success you have to fail few times and learn obout your failure. A person who is not motivated, determinated or passionated will give up rapidly by discouragement. A successful person have to be competitive to because they are often other person who want the same thing than you and unfortunatly everybody can't succeed, it leads to an hard human competition. To me, a successful person is a person who follow and realize his dreams. And who is happy in his life.

One day a man said : "most of people think that  success is a finality, but in fact, it's the beginning of a new life". Personnaly, I like this way of thinking. Indeed, the success is something that every entrepreneur looks for. But if we think about it, when the success is here, it's thanks to an idea, and also a business model, and many other things and the lifes of the entrepreneur and all the employees are linked to the success of the company. And it's exactly here that all begin : an incredible ride where competition is the master word and a crazy race to increase profits and keep  market shares! I like participating to conferences or watching videos of entrepreneur all around the world and knowing their vision of the world and business. In my opinion, there is five main traits to succeed and keep on the top : be open minded, be tenace, be a good communicant, be a visionnary person,  be honnest in everything you do. This last trait is particularly critical because it affects the others. Indeed,  you are honnest with your staff, your clients, your investors, people will feel it and they'll give you back the fruits of your efforts and, in the same time, they'll insure you sustainable success.%%

When I left Bordeaux, I was twenty-one and very happy to say good-bye to this town that I hated.  My feelings were more addressed to my family than to the town itself. Now I feel rather like making a declaration of love to my beloved town! What happened? It is not only because it was listed on the UNESCO World Heritage a few years ago. There is a huge change in the way Bordeaux has taken advantage of its architecture. When I was young the town was not only dark but black; the river was brown and sad, you were prevented from seeing it because of the docks. Now, from the Garonne banks, recently pedestrian friendly, you can enjoy the wonderful sight of the Place de la Bourse as well as the long embankment faade a way to enter the 18th century, the glorious age of Bordeaux. The architecture of this century is sober: all monuments are on the same height (3 floors), same style, same soft stone. It is so beautiful to discover the Grand Theatre and the different avenues around that you can forgive, if possible, the origin of such a wealth: slavery and, later, wine.%%

Youll never believe this, but the most incredible thing happened today! I was sitting at my desk thinking and staring out the window. Then suddenly I noticed someone on top of the building next to mine. It was a man trying to ride his unicycle across the narrow edge of the skyscraper. I screamed and called my colleagues over. It was so alarming, we didnt know what to do. I mean, the stunt was great but terribly dangerous. And everything happened really fast. What he did was, he got on the unicycle and rode it for a few meters. Then he lost his balance. He landed on the roof, but his unicycle fell all the way to the ground. The guy really lucked out. Nobody on the ground was hurt by the unicycle. A big bowl of pudding that Puddilicious was using for marketing was broken by the unicycle, though. That made a huge mess in the street. A lot of money will be needed to repair the damage. It was the stunt mans fault, so I guess hes in a lot of trouble.

For our city, we have to choose between five kinds of renewable energy: solar power, hydroelectricity, tidal energy, wind power and biomass. Our town is located in the countryside in the east of France and hasn't got any powerful river. That means that we can't use tidal energy because the seaside is far away. The same goes for hydroelectricity because of the lack of useful river, for solar power because of the insufficient sunshine. Wind power and biomass only left. Wind power could be an environmentally friendly and cost-effective solution due to the windy weather. But wind power farms require huge facilities which may injure the beauty of surrounding nature. So, as far as I'm concerned, I would recommend to develop biomass power. Our city is surrounded by large forests and crop farms. They could produce a lot of biomass with a good carbon footprint owing to the fact that it could be made on site. Required investment are relatively low. In addition to that, this way of using renewable energy would provide new openings to our farmer who are suffering from the reform of the common agricultural policy. In these circumstances, biomass would probably be the most relevant solution, both environmentally and economically.%%

In France, crime rates have constantly increased for the last fifty years. But it must be distinguished between different kinds of violation of the law. The number of bloody murders remains pretty constant. In opposite rape's cases are growing steadily, probably due to the fact that victims are less reluctant to complain. But what is the most worrying is the inflation of petty crimes, like purse snatching, shoplifting or voluntary violence. Most of these acts are committed by young people who often become hardened criminals. This raises the question whether our penal policy is appropriate and how effective it is. In fact, it doesn't work. They are more and more criminals who are apprehended and convicted for breaking the law. But the number of people thrown in jail doesn't increase proportionately. Actually, that means that many sentences are not served. This situation is really peculiar and dangerous because it triggers a feeling of impunity which encourages offenders to keep doing their felon activities. So people start being upset. They become angry against political authorities. And when a population feels neglected and abandoned, extremist and populist parties are growing. Without doubt, ensuring that sentences are served, is currently, one of the most important political issue in France.%%

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: To all I.T department employees, Yesterday at 5:15 p.m, a virus partially damaged our company's server. But the situation is under control, and our internal data are saved in a safe hard drive. I expected a few hours to repair the system, but further updating is still required. Should anyone have an approaching project deadline, and need to use any saved figures, please call me on 2422, or come to my office today, from 4 p.m to 9 p.m. I am working overtime this evening,in order to resolve the problem as soon as possible. I apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. Kind Regards, Yacine, I.T Department Maintenance Manager

Honestly, I don't admire anybody so it's a difficult topic to me. But if I don't admire anyone, I can tell that i'm certainly envious of certain. In fact, I think that a lot of people who reached the success have a social predisposition to succeed in their life because they are almost always the sames profiles: whites, with parents who have a good job like doctor or executive manager, so that can have a good education. They integrate the best schools and build a tough culture. With all of this, it's easier to have a successful life and to be radiant enough to be a "perfect" person. Of course it exist persons who achieve a great career whereas they didn't have as chance in the beginning, but let's admit that they are not a lot. They are those who have a very tough willpower and that nothing or no one can deviate them of theirs objectives. Finally, if you don't have enough money you have to have a very strong willpower.

Green energy is one of the major issues of this decade, or even of this century. In fact, with the world population growing at such a high pace, energy needs are increasing exponentially. We're now trying to replace nuclear and coil power by wind and solar power to get to a more sustainable world. That's what we call "green business". My opinion about green energy is that it is necessary to start using them massively, in order to preserve the Earth for the future generations of humans and animals that will receive the legacy we will give them. However, hypocritical measures are taken by governments and companies to give the impression that they are acting in that way, while they are doing only the very requiered things. I'm not really going out of my way to buy green products, because they're often too expensive, but I try to act every day in order to reduce waste. For instance I reuse plastic bottles, I buy products in biggest quantities to avoid getting many little packages, and I recycle paper and glass.

My Uncle build his empire by nothing... At the beginning he worked in a little company which provided elctricity in africa, he were an executive and managed operators. But one day he decided to create his own company as he had save enough and set up in a new market he didn't know : The Petrolium one. Many people tried to take him down, family, friends, competitors etc and bank didn't want to give him a loan. He didn't give up he still looked for a loan when finally he found investitors who accepted to  follow him on his travel. They took many money on the table for this. Nowadays the company is very successful. It is one of the best company in african petrolium market and has gained 40% market part since its creation. Those events made my uncle stronger, the most admirable thing is his tenacity, he didn't give up, he always believed in himself convinced he will be successful one day. He's an example for me and should be for most of people.

I do believe companies have a responsablity to society. Unfortunately, more of them only think about profits for their shareholders. However, when I think about the companies I worked for over the last 15 years, I would say that things are changing. Many years ago, it was accepted that a company think about profits without thinking about the community or the environment. Today, because of the crisis and internet, they have to change, either they do not want to, They have to communicate about what they do for the planet, for the community or for their employees all around the world. For example, the last company I worked for did a lot to improve the company's image. In 2010 they started to recycle papers and plastics and gave instructions to save energy. They also gave a customized mug to each employee for their coffee or tea time, etc... As you see that could be a nice story. But, as people felt it was done only for the company's image, they did not subscribe on it and things went worst. That 's why I do believe that if a company wants to do things only for its image, with no aim or real concern, the result will never be positive.%%

Paris is a town steeped in history.The city is well-known for its marvelously preserved historic architecture.Paris is a most tourist-visited city  in the world. In my opinion ,the cause of this success is maintain of balance between modern and historic arcitecture.For example ,the modern very busy district La Defense is relatively separate of historic center. At the same time there is a system of modern and effective public transport.The Parisian museums The Louvre and Orsay are well-known collections ,it houses as ancient as contemporary sculpture and painting.Paris is a very green city ,there are a lot of picturesque gardens and beautiful parcs here.The most famous is Jardin de Tuileries ,near the Louvre museum and Jardin de Luxembourg.I would like to recommend You to visit The Notre-Dame cathedral ,situated on Cit island.

At the same time there is a system of modern and effective public transport.The Parisian museums The Louvre and Orsay are well-known collections ,it houses as ancient as contemporary sculpture and painting.Paris is a very green city ,there are a lot of picturesque gardens and beautiful parcs here.The most famous is Jardin de Tuileries ,near the Louvre museum and Jardin de Luxembourg.I would like to recommend You to visit The Notre-Dame cathedral ,situated on Cit island.

I admire successful persons and, among them, the rare ones who use their qualities for their passion: a humanistic ideal. If I think about the Abb Pierre, Germaine Tillion or Robert Antelme, all of them have in common at least three main characteristics: intelligence, generosity and courage. One of these qualities wouldnt be sufficient on its own but, together, they create strong, remarkable and admirable personalities. Intelligence: I was a kid when the Abb Pierre called for people generosity in 1954. He did it on the occasion of a radio game. He appeared as someone very cultured and the whole country agreed to be woken up. Courage: Tillion and Antelme, both deported, described how they tried not only to survive but to live, keep their dignity and their faith on humanity. Generosity: The three of them liked to be close to people. They never used their celebrity for themselves. They were helpful, curious and interested in others.

Steve Jobs' main trait always seemed to me to be his proactivity. In fact, he always tried to be ahead of the competitors and to be the most innovative one on the market. Moreover, he was also an amazing speaker since his speeches always drew attention. By the way, he is often used as an epitomy in this field. One of his habits was preparation. For instance, he always dedicated a lot of time to working on his speeches in order to make them perfect on the D-day. Furthermore, it was common knowledge that he spent a lot of time working with his employees so he could listen to what they yearn for. This habit also let him strenghen Apple's creativity power for his was not the only one to think about new products. Eventually, he didn't let the failure lose hope after being sacked from his own firm - he stood up and continued to follow his dreams.

My native country is France, which is not specially famous for its breathtaking skyscrapers. One exception is of course the Eiffel Tower, the symbol of France and Paris. I am always surprised by the impact the Eiffel Tower seems to have on tourists. Its creative metal structure provoques shouts of joy and amazement. Another building I like in Paris is the Arab World Institute premises. At first sight, its squared shape gives an impression of rigidity and coldness, but then the beautiful patterns of the windows and the way the sunlight is reflecting on it is simply magic. The work of Le Corbusier enabled the creation of other famous French buildings. At the time, the creation process and realizing this work on residential buildings was very innovative. However, it remains residential large scale buildings, and I will always find it ugly. In France, the best known building are from Renaissance. These are not amazing skyscrapers racing to reach the top of the world. I think both are beautiful.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: The differences between universities and "Grandes Ecoles" in France is difficult to understand for strangers. When I was post graduating law in Berlin, I spent much time trying to explain to my schoolmates what it meant. Having studied at the Universities and at a "Grande Ecole", I should have been able to tell clearly about it. But some didn't catch it because it's a little bit strange concept that doesn't exist abroad. Similarly, universities and "Grandes Ecoles" are teaching places which provide means for graduating. The main difference is that everyone is allowed to go to university, regardless of his school curriculum. Conversely to go to a Grande Ecole, you have to pass a very demanding exam which a lot fail. So the best students go to Grandes Ecoles" But unlike university, after having been selected, courses are very easy and there is time left to have fun without studying too hard. This different ways have both their advantages and drawback. The main problem is that it influences the whole professional life. The best jobs are, in fact, reserved for "Grandes Ecoles". It's always surprising to hear a CEO, 60 years old, who is still proud to be a past pupil of a famous "Grande Ecole".%% With the exception of Ivy league and Oxbridge, its a French uniqueness.

I'd like to tell the story of a well-known man who was born in France. He was educated by a modest family who was relatively poor. 20 years later on, He started his career as salesman for a T.V company. Let me just say that after 20 years, this person became a politician and a businessman admired by lots of people in his country. He learned a lot along his career. Thanks to his very big drive, he always set -up to himself strong targets in order to exceed his limits. It's a good example showing how a man who was not born in a powerful family could have been transformed himeself into a very important person, being able to influence others (the government in particular). But in the opposite and to be complete in my story, I also have to mention that he knew ' black days ". As politician, he was not accepted by his counterparts as they knew he was not born in a well-known family. There were some doubts regarding the origin of his fortun, his success. It explains why some politicians were jalous about him regarding his successful story. I will end by saying that he finally had to go to prison, lost a big portion of his fortune.%% He finally decided to stop his political career. How to say...coin has always two sides...

I want to tell you about the the famous monnument of Paris : the Eiffel Tower !. Now Eiffel Tower is not in the running of the most tallest tower but may be the most famous over the world. Green business is important to me, but more of that, I would say ethic business is. That means I do not want a green product if it has been made by a kid in a poor country. For this reason I try to buy less but better, and I always ask myself "do you really need this product ?" If the answer is "yes" then I try to find a green product. However, as I do believe we cannot take comany's word for it, I always choose products certified by independant organization. And if I have to go out of my way and pay more for it, I do it. Moreover, since my first child's birth, 4 years ago, I started to buy our food from a local organization. That means that every week we have our vegetables, fruits, meat and cheese directly from local producers. Of course, as they are small, they do not have any "international green label" but as they do not have anything to hide, you can visit their farm and talk directly to them. Doing that, I can be sure the food we eat is healthy and environment friendly.

One the one hand, I think that it is a good thing to have superstores near your home, because it's useful and helpful, but in other hand  Internet and the superstores kill the small shops. Furthermore, in my area I can prepare my shopping by internet and I have just to pick it up in my car, what is definitely a time saving.  Nowadays, in the megastores they tend to phase out some jobs and replace it by machines what I am definitely against. A lot of people nowadays buy everything by internet because we can compare the price, they offer good services, good guarantee for the product  but after? What if you have a trouble with your purchase? How will you do to return it? The cost of the transportation still expensive, and it can be difficult to package it, if you had throw away the packaging. We have to make a balance between superstores and small shops because it is important to preserve the job  of people, and it is important to speak face to face, if you need some information and advices. Even if you have a huge description of the product by internet, you can't touch it and you can be duped by it.%%

I think you also need to have a great idea or some kind of vision that noboby else has. When I think of successful people, I think of steve jobs. People say he could lose his temper and didn't always lusten to others, he hab fantastic ideas. That's what mettered mote than anything else. topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: I'd like to talk about the implementation of euro in France. As many european countries, when our politicians started to talk about such a topic, many people were really skeptical about presented expected benefits. The resistance in France, but I should say in most of european countries was stronger and stronger and there were lots of opponents in favor of keep using our local currency. There is a good reason, easy to explain and understand. Currency is part of our heritage ; adopting a new currency would mean lose our traditions. On the other hand we were explained all the benefits we could get from using euro, such as buying some goods in another country and having the possibility to easily compare prices between them. I should admit that, I was also a bit reluctant to use Euro, I now think it's finally very convenient. As there are always pros and cons in any sensible choice, I have to add that unfortunately Euro's implementation had also a negative effect as all prices started to immediately raise up. The implementation process should have been better controled by politicians. Due to that non expected inflation consumers have seen their buying power drastically reduced.%% The combination of the slowdown in our economy caused by the global economic crisis and that inflation explain the current trend to less consume and more invest our money in our respective banks.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: The acronym NAFTA stands for the North American Free Trade Agreement. It is a trade agreement between the North American countries of Mexico, the United States and Canada. Following long negotiations that started in 1986, NAFTA was finally signed 1992 by the leaders of the three nations. It took effect in 1994.The goal of NAFTA is free trade among the three countries. This includes removing trade barriers  for example, import tariffs or quotas. Supporters of the agreement say that it stimulates trade among the countries and builds closer relationships. Opponents say that it hurts domestic industries and only benefits the big corporations. Generally, the US has a goods deficit with its NAFTA partners; that is, it imports more goods from them than it exports.  On the other hand, the US generally has a services surplus with its two neighbors, i.e. it exports more services to them than it imports. As in any trade agreement, there are winners and losers. Unlike the EU and Eurozone agreements, NAFTA does not allow the free flow of people across borders. The three member countries also did not adopt a single currency. It is more of a simple trade agreement.

text: Hi Micheal, I come back to you about the final report on the project 'Heatlhy childs" in Brasil. I met Sam this morning and the deadline change from april 6th to march 28th. I already did the first part (presentation of the overall project and its objectives...) and I'm currently working on the achievements (with statistique table...). But, Sam would like us to presente also the financial aspect of our local partners' development. To do so, you need to get in touch with Laura Martinez, our focal point in the NGO "XX", she can gives you relevant data. I know the timing is short and I'm here to help you if you have any concerns or problems. Anyway I think it would be great to schedule a meeting next week. Let me know when you're free. Have a nice day Micheal and see you soon!

The architecture of Paris has a really old story : it is a blend between old and modern architecture. Actually, the Paris urban area is the place where you can find the most important number of skyscrapers in Europe. They are located in three different areas : La Dfense, Italie circus and near from the Seine river. La Dfense is located in the western Paris area and gather the most important part of the French skyscrapers, it is so called the business district, the place where the main and most powerful companies have their offices. Italie circus is a residential district in the South of Paris, less than half a mile away from where I live. The third most important place is located along the Seine river, it is a blend between business and residential buildings. On top of that, there is of course the Eiffel tower, the symbol of France in general and Paris in particular.

It is really difficult to pinpoint success in general terms, because it's really subjective. I think it's easier to relate to somebody's success because one's achievements are not necessarily what we pursue in life. So how could we define it ? For me, being successful means being happy and proud of what you do, of course I can't deny that success will always remain inextricably linked with money and fame but I strongly believe that there's more to it. Christine Caine is a successful person that I personally admire, she founded an association which battle against human trafficking. (A21) She started from the bottom, failed a few times but she always had this project in her heart and she never gave up. She often holds conferences to help people build their confidence and to encourage them to trust their own abilities. I really like the fact that working is not a burden for her, she has always been passionate about people and she managed to make a career out of that. This would be my definition of success, being happy, proud of what you do and manage to make a living out of what you enjoy the most.%%

I have a friend who is always successful. She is a former colleague of mine. She seems to reach every goals she had define. I admire her because she is a self-made woman. Her parents where not wealthy nor influent. However she worked hard and joined a famous high school. Then, after being graduated, she was hired by an international audit firm where I met her. I don't know exactly what traits make her so successful but the main reasons I can pinpoint are the following : she is a great listener and has exceptionnal abilities to adapt to every type of individuals. In addition, she has a strong work ethic that endows her with respect. Furthermore, she is very resilient and is always able to bounce back from adversity. For example, when it appeared clearly that she would not be promoted in our company she resigned to create her own audit firm. But from my point of view, what is the most important is that she is incredibly passionate about her work.

text: Dear Santa Claus, I'm writing to update you on our progress regarding your different requests. First, from now on, as time is an important part of your business, we decided to forward children's letters to you every day, instead of every week. In addition of that, one person will help children in writting to you. Of course we will also send an answer from you to each of them. We really hope that will help you to be on time for your distribution of presents. Should you have any additional request, please contact me directly. Best Regards, Sylvie Virtu Mail Box Manager

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: I think the communication in France can be both explicit and implicit but it is generally done in an indirect way, as in most of the latin countries. Actually, in France, communication implies understanding without words, that means the receiver/listener is responsible for effective communication. Furthermore, the french language tends towards an elaborate style, so the communication can be hard to understand for someone who would not be a french native speaker. We do not use different intonations when we speak with a friend or an unknown person. That's the reason why, the only way to be polite is to use the "vous" form, instead of the "tu" form, which is more familiar. That means a foreign visitor, who is used to speaking using the pronoun "you", could make mistakes if he does not pay attention to this simple rule : "never use 'tu' if the listener is not one of your close relations". However, it could also be surprising for a french child to hear someone speaking to him using "vous" instead of "tu". To sum up, french language is not very simple to master because it has so many styles, rules and conjugations. Finally, it is probably why we like complaining constantly.%% However, if you rather use spoken communication with lots of body language, you will be understood.

Nowadays, crime in France becomes a tough problem, such as in many democratic countries in the world. Here, unless the government opens new prisons and recruits new staff, we can't deal with all the people incarcerated. Their solution actually is to let people with sentences lower than 5 years -even recidivist ones- in conditioned liberty. But the problem remains the same : we haven't got enough people to keep an eye on every criminal. To my mind, cutting down on the scurity budget is a really poor idea, even for political reasons. Maybe they should also think about forcing prisoners to build new prisons, with individual cells because... there is this second issue : I assume that most of the prisoners aren't hardened criminal and yet, they are often considered all the same. A few years ago, I took my parents to a fast food for the first time. I remember this time like it was yesterday. Indeed, it has been one of the most hilarious moment in my life, especially when my father ordered his food! The waiter had pronounced the usual sentences such as "small, medium or big" about the size of the drink when my father answered "a normal one please"...Then the waiter asked if my father would like some potatoes or french fries. Obviously, for him there was no difference between a potatoe and a french frie so he responded "whatever you want"...Finally, the waiter finished by asking if we would like to eat-in or take-out. Of course, my father did not answer as expected by saying "it is for now". I am not sure that the waiter found this dialogue very funny, given that my father did not joke, he was serious. Actually, I guess the waiter had a good sense of humour to remain calm. Clearly, my parents did not used to eat in fast food and I think they never went to a restaurant like that from this time. But it's still a good moment of hilarity, each time we remind this scene!%%

Although green business is important to me, it would be a lie to say that it is a concept thats is dear to my heart. To be considered green, a business should be using organic and biodegradable materials and renewable energy such as solar, wind or water. These are the cornerstones of sustainability. However, to be truly green, a company should embrace social welfare, (cf. Corporate social responsibility) because humanity itself is a part of the world. It is not enough to treat the planet well, they need to treat people rightly as well. Unfortunately, I am all too aware of the increase of "greenwashing" in our society, which is one of the reason why I wouldn't go out of my way to buy organic products.  We can't take companies at their words anymore. Moreover, I also regret the fact that the products that come from well-know and respectable green businesses are so expensive. As a student, I, more often than not, can't afford them. Nevertheless, when I can, it doesn't bother me to pay a little bit more because there is always a good value for money.

The goal of green business is to encourage "green" practices among businesses. I mean, the first aim is to encourage people to be more environmentally friendly in order to recude and minimize impact of Human Being on nature. Green business is a concept developed to make people more responsible regarding to environment. These last years, within European union, a kind of ECO label has been developed. It aims is to avoid companies that pretend to sell ECO products where as they are not doing as much effort as necessary to consider themselves to be like that or, worst, by doing misleading publicity. To be honest, I don't go out of my way to buy green products but I try to respect as much as possible environment by doing simple tasks: take shower and avoid bath, turn off the tap when I brush my teeth, sort by kind of categories the garbages and so on...

I think green is a very large field, and a green business is characterized by numerous factors it respects to a greater or lesser extent. One the one hand, there is the green product itself. One the other hand, the company energy and paper consumption, its behaviour towards its employees, the waste generation are other key factors, and there are many others. I appreciate when a company does a green step, even if it is a business move. The problem is that for big companies, we have to take their word for it, and I have serious reservations about their commitment. I think for a lot of them it is just marketing but nothing real is realized, and it puts me off buying their supposedly green products. I would like to buy more fresh products at the market, from local producers, that would be my way of being environmentally friendly. I don't think I would be able to change my habits and sacrifice comfort to use more green products, at least not now. I think there is more to do first.

One of the main reasons why Paris is considered one of the most beautiful cities in the world, is the continuity in its urban planning and architecture. I'm going to talk about two of its most strange and extravagant buildings. The Montparnasse Tower is a 210 meters office, one of the tallest skyscrapper in France. It is in the ideal location to discover all of Paris. It is the most central high point in the capital. Facing the Eiffel Tower, it is the best location for taking in the loveliest monuments of Paris. You can reach in it one of the fastest lifts in the world. You arrive at the 56th floor in less than 40 seconds. Situated at the heart of Paris, The Centre Pompidou reveals its unique, timeless and modern architecture. The outside of the building reflects the modernity that is held inside. The building's exterior is very bold: brightly painted pipes and ducts crisscross its transparence facade and an outdoor escalator flanks the building. To put it in a nutshell if you are into arts and architecture, you can't go wrong with Paris. It is one of a kind.

Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR), is an initiative to assess and urge the companies to take responsibility for their impact on the environnement and society. CSR is front and center in economical and social debates, however there are strong arguments on both sides of the issue. On the one hand, companies already answer to fixed regulations, they have to pay taxes and follow a certain code that varies according to the country. So why should they do more than that ? Shareholders insist on the fact that companies already fulfil their duties and taking care of society is not one of them. On the other hand, companies make business out of society (e.g. : the trends, the workforces) so it's logical that they should participate in it's welfare. Without people, there is no business so companies should definitely meet them halfway. For my own part, I believe that the government should incentivise and prompt companies to take part in this combat against dehumanisation and environnemental  deterioration. CSR really means "corporate citizenship", companies should feel the need to help despite the fact that business is all about profit.  This is not a business transaction it is our world and our society slowly dilapidating, and with the impact that companies currently have on society they can easily promote positive and environmental changes.%%

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Incoterms are made to clarify international trade; that is, it defines clearly what the supplier's responsabilities are all along the transport of the goods and when the responsability is tranfered to the buyer, e.g. at departure (EXW), after being loaded on board of a boat (FOB), or at delivery, duty paid (DDP). It also clarify what the supplier is supporting in the name of the buyer, e.g. insurance and transport cost. Into EU, the incoterms are also used but generally simplified to only 2 over the 11 availables. Symply put, the sales are Ex Works (EXW) or Delivery At Place (DAP). Therefore, during the negociation of a sale with a customer, rather than spending time in the terms of the trade, i.e which responsablities are you offering to assume; you can focus on the additionnal services you wish to offer and indeed invoice. Look at it this way, EU offer a large market, with easy terms of trade for a more profitable activity. Mind you, EU is not the world and you should keep on trading out of.

I recently moved from up country to a city named Tours, in France. The city has history, every house, building even churches have history. Nowadays, almost every tower or house is located on a classified area. You can't change your windows without informing the city because it must not keep its look. Old, kind of like Asterix's vilage, there's a castle not so far from my home. The Basilic is being renoved, it's as high as any buisness tower in Paris, but it look more charming because every tower, house, even sidewalks, the go together. They fit like human body parts. It good to preserv a city's history, but when it doesn't allow the city to evolv, to develop itself because of certain manners the city tries to keep going with. It takes too much time to have a projet examined, and there's to many borders not to cross. You can't change your window like that, you have to make demands, you can't instal solar panols like that, etc... Too many rules kill the law. Even if the city looks great, what about the people living in it.

i'm currently working for a global company which employes ten thousands peaople in 30 countries. We have to prepare ech year a report on CSR. Only one persn is dedicated to compile actions done for the hole company. This dedicated person is coordinating the gathering of information from diffrent departments as Human resources, HSE, business and so on. In my opinion CSR is just a question of communication, this is not the business of a company to take care of the environnement or to save the planet. this kind of responsibility should stay in the hands of states or international organisation as ONU or dedicated ong. Company should demonstrate that they respect the rules implemented by states and if they want to make more they should have the possibility to donate money or support projects lead by governments.. To oblige companies to fill an annual report on CSR is counter productif, the result is a comunication exercice.

The other day, a drama happened in the Philippines. A hurricane destroyed everything there, and more than ten thousand people died. Even more people could lose their life in the next days, as they lack of everything: water, food, medicine and shelter. Fortunately people from all over the world are giving money to local NGOs, which are trying to rescue survivors. This disaster shows the vulnerability of coastal areas. Indeed, they are particularly exposed to hurricanes and other environmental disasters. Since a few years, several similar disasters occured in the Pacific ocean. Some scientists say they are related to global warming. For the moment, it is difficult to confirm or invalidate this theory. Nevertheless, I think that governments should tackle this issue preventively. If global warming were to be confirmed, we would have to change our ways of living quickly. What is sure is that facing environmental disasters will probably be the main issue of the twenty-first century.

In my view, corporations have a social responsability. They should give back to the society. That means working to satisfy their employees, donating to the local community and contributing to the environment. Though shareholders are important a company cannot be without a society. Some companies work to their employees' satisfaction by offering incentives on top of the salary like stock options or vouchers, by providing well furnished offices and a good follow-up of their career plan. They also contribute by sponsoring sporting team in local communities hence helping to reveal talents that can reach high levels in well-known and significant competitions. Another way to help is to sell outdated computers, old mobile phones or used batteries to recycling companies and give the income to a charity. Which is a valuable contribution to improve people's living conditions and to preserve the environment. The end result of it is a win-win situation where CSR is an essential part of certain companies' development that want to build long term success. They get a good public image, loyal customers and a profitable positioning in the market.

Uno is a card game. The rules are, every player starts with seven cards. The rest of the cards are placed in a pile face down. Next to the pile a space should be designated for a discard pile. The top card should be placed in the discard pile, the first player tries to match the card in the discard pile. You have to match either the number or color, for instance if the discard pile has a red card that is an 5 you have to place either a red card or a card with an 5 on it. If the player has no matches they must draw a card. You must lose all your cards, first player to do it is the winner.

My son and I ate a new seafood restaurant called El Huaghinango. The place looked clean and beatiful. It had live music, it was really cool. I ordered shrimp salad and a dozen of fresh local oysters. Alex ordered a special seafood soup, and it came with a loaf of bread hot from the oven. He ordered french fries. The server gave us a fantastic, free classic dessert. I had a cappucino with fat-free milk. Everything was delicious and fresh, so we'll probably will go again.The service was really nice. We gave a twenty percent tip to the server.

Hugo and I recently ate at a new restaurant called La Nacional. I had called one week earlier to make a reservation, and the employee was very friendly, She explained to me that they are always full. She puts us down for a 2 p.m. reservation. When we arrived, the place looked just fine, nothing special. Once there the server gave us a fantastic, free shrimp consomm as appetizer. I ordered the sirloin cut well done with asparagus as side dish and spicy mashed beans as complement. Hugo ordered a roast octopus with tomato sauce and a side of baked potato. Both were unbelievable! As soon as we finished the main course, we both ordered their specialty the soft toffee. It was the classic dessert in town. The food was definitely not low fat, but the chef said that its mostly local and wild. Everything was delicious and unusual, but so expensive. So well probably go again.

I also told the seller how much I loved the car but he didn't accept the money that I offered so, I continued looking at other cars, but I didn't liked any of them so I tried to get more money, I asked my father to borrowed me $ 5,000 and he did it. I went back with the seller and I made a final offer of $ 85,000 and he accepted!! I was very glad and he thought it was a fair offer so, we both were happy .

Mexico is a beautiful country, one of the countries with most tourism ever. There's no such thing as a correct etiquette to hang out with a Mexican. However, here are some tips that I consider helpful: If somebody offers you food, do not refuse it, some people may consider it an insult. Never complain about the food taste, it is a big insult. Do not say anything bad about football, (soccer) people here are very passionate about the sport. Don't bring up any conversation regarding religion or politics, if the host brings the subject to the table, then you can talk about them.

I think, one of the most interesting and effective management strategy is to use the SWOT analysis technique, that is exactly what our business need. SWOT helps evaluation of internal and external strengths,weaknesses, opportunities and threats of a business. We need to have everything clear and in order if we want the SWOT analysis technique works well. We need to work more time on the team to identify the strengths in order to motivate the team and also identifying the weaknesses helps to be aware of the problems in the future. Knowing how the team works we can pay attention on how we can make movements and task assignments, we can learn how to motivate the team, make them stronger and develop mutual trust, so the team can get involve in the process, collaborating altogether to complete the SWOT analysis. Making changes to this technique could be a good idea to take advantage of all the features it offers.

Has gender equality improved in Mexico in recent years? It's interesting that many people believe that it has, but we can still see many gaps in work opportunities as well as in politics roles. Mexico has never had a woman as a President, even as governor of any state, woman in the senate only account for the 20%, which is really low compared to the actual gender population Mix (52% women and 48% men). There is a law that the Political Parties have to postulate at least 40% women, but most of them, if they won the election, are then replaced for Men. Still much to do in Mexico to make gender equality improves.

In Mexico manners are different according at geographic zone that are you living or visiting. In south region is a common practice offer a small gift on birthdays, gifts are related to gender. For example if you are visiting a woman, flowers are accepted, for a man wine will be ok,  in some places also small bucket of fruit. But in the north things are totally different, most common practice is a money on a birthday card is accepted. Visiting a friend house is formal to call  at least one day before, is not polite just arrive on the last minute. Other important rules are on a restaurant appointments. Is not polite to arrive late on any date, a lot of people think that arrive 15 or 20 minutes late looks good, but not. Other common practice on a restaurant is order a center dish for all the attendances, if you are thinking to do that just be sure that all people like the food that are you thinking, for example some people hate onions, others can't eat cheese.

text: I recently attended an interesting talk about why we can be sure about a coming better future. I want to highlight how confident the speaker was. It is worthy that he was well prepared. He choses viewpoint question to help people to reason at heart instead of just giving endless information. Also, he uses short and effective phrases, then, the information was well supported and easy to memorize. However, I think he could use some graphs or charts to impress the audience, maybe giving more statistics in order to motivate people to take action, improving our skills to help more people to build a better future. Overall, I am happy because of the useful teachings.

When I was younger I had two embarrasing moments. My first moment was when I were at school with my girlfriends talking about silly things and drinking soda. Suddenly the guy who likes me approches to my group but I didn't see him, so one of my friends said something really fun and I spit it out the soda in the face of the guy I liked. I felt so out of place and blushed myself. Oh my Gosh! My second awkward moment was when I were in a place called the Rodeo Santa Fe where you can dance country music. I were with my friends having fun and then the cheerleader made a contest where you have to drink a big glass of beer, turn around a coin in order to get you dizzy and run to the other side of the dance floor to win the competition. So I participate in the contest, drank my big glass of beer, turned it around the coin but whe I ran to the other side I didn't run straigth ahead and I fall over a table... it was the most awkward moment I've neen trough.

I am mexican, I live in a small town called Papantla in Veracruz, I have a good life, I am single and live by my self, I am an RPM (Radiation Protection Monitor) so I work in nuclear facilities, and as you think My work is hectic and demanding and the stress during my shift is not low, the pay is not so bad, so I could get my own house, car and have enough to have food on my table. However, sometimes I feel that my social life is not good at all. The thing is that I have got everything I need and more but some times I don't have enough time to enjoy it, as a result, I am getting a little lonely. I live nearby my parents but as I travel a lot I can''t see them so much, I just call them now and then. Well at least I meet a lot of people around the world, I'm happy, and I shouldn't complain.

text: I like watching snowboarding, especially when there are olympics games, but I would not practice, i think its extremely dangerous. The sport I love to practice it's rock climbing, I think it's exciting, climbing steep slopes and find that your hands are the only ones that separates you from a free fall it's like... i don't know how to explain it, i'm speechless, well of course the harness will be there if you have a slip and  the leg straps that are a full salvation. The first time I went climbing, i was sweating profusely, it was really a wall, but I could hear the thud of my heart beat, then I felt cramps in the fingers, I let go ... i felt like I fell from a great height, it was the most terrifying experience of my life, I felt very scared and didn't want to do it again, but I had to face that fear and a few months later, I returned, very scared, but eager to keep climbing, I love this sport.

Jusith Wight talks in her book, how people can  afford their career anxiety or career path confussion.  This book shows how eople tend to change their career path five to seven years in times throughout their lifetime. Encourages people to examine the position if it really will enhace our career path. So the book is showing how to determine which jobs corresponds to our personality. It is interesting how the author is showing this in two simple steps, I think it is more than that, but lets see how she thinks: -Learn about your personality. Consider the activities that really uplift you. -Learn what jobs are best suited to your personality and interests. Yo will need to be sure what is that would make you happy at work. The book is very motivational, but makes unrealistic claims. Is not saying how to make some exercises, probes, or participate in some activities that could help to afford our decision to choose the correct career.%%

As part of my paternity responsibility I'm thinking to change my mortgage to a one that fits better to our personal finances, right now we're not being affected by the market but  who knows if some day the bottom drop out of the market and the things become complicated to us, so we're being thrifty in our finances therefore we are changing our mortgage to a lower monthly payment. Also we need to expend less money in our daily expenses because they're too high and also our utilities bill are increasing every month, another expense it's our gasoline expense it's way too high because we have a six cylinder truck, so we will change it for a four cylinder model.

I don''t have a career path yet, but I can write about my fathers. He doesn''t study at college, he started to work in a bank after he finish high school with a background in administration. He started as a cashier and went ascending in the company until he has the assistant director position. Then the market fell and he was fired, those were hard times so he got into the trades industry . Currenty he owns a miscellany, prior to that he worked in a construction company in the human resources area, before that he worked as a salesperson for a pay TV company, a money exchange, a taxi driver for a long period. But first the opened a coffe shop and a glassware, those were bad bussines and he lost a lot of money. I''m happy to say that after all the bad times he finally is financially stable and I hope that he''ll subsequently  open a bigger store.

I am a student of John Brown University in Siloam Springs, Arkansas. Since I am studying two majors, my life is not easy. I manage a certain amount of stress, which can sometimes become bad for my life. However, I always try to control it so that it does not take over my life. I seldom think wether studying a double major has been a good or bad decision. This happens because once I realize that the hard work is worthy enough to go the extra mile, I feel good, and ready to do my best at school. Even though I try to balance all of my activities during the day due to my stressful life, I feel like my social life is not that good. I spend a great amount of time doing homework instead of hanging out with friends. This is one of the things I believe I have to change.

Executive Summary Introduction The company plans to build a strong market position in the town, due to the partners' industry experience and mild competitive climate in the area. The Company It is equally owned and managed by its two partners. it has extensive experience in sales, marketing, and management, and was vice president of marketing The company intends to hire two full-time pastry bakers and six part-time baristas to handle customer service and day to day operations. Products and Services We offer coffee and espresso products, all from high quality Columbian grown imported coffee beans. The Market The retail coffee industry in the U.S. has recently experienced rapid growth. The cool marine climate in southwest Washington stimulates consumption of hot beverages throughout the year. We wants to establish a large regular customer base, and will therefore concentrate its business and marketing on local residents, which will be the dominant target market. This will establish a healthy, consistent revenue base to ensure stability of the business. Financial Considerations We expects to raise $110,000 of its own capital, and to borrow $100,000 guaranteed

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: Last week I attended a meeting about the inventory that we have in a few days. To summarize, the presenter gave a presentation of the process we will follow step by step. And the big finish was a test to assess what we learned, with any problem I passed it! There were good and bad points. The presenter gave clear information and examples. The presentation contained visual aids that helped us understand. The exam at the end was pretty good because it helped us to increase knowledge. Something that I didn't like was the voice of the presenter, was low and powerless. That made the meeting very boring. I think we all understood the key points in spite of the details. Overall, the presentation was very helpful and well-prepared. A suggestion might be to change the presenter. Anyway, I learned a lot.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: The last presentation I attended was a real mess. The presenter didn't know anything about the topic he was supposed to expo. We all guessed he hadn't had .time to prepare the information.  At the beginning he never presented himself to the audience, so anybody knew who were they listening to. He  was too nervous and was sweathing as if he would have done  high impact excercise for hours. Later on the expositor spoke about the impact of a good presentation and the very important first impression it causes. However, there was something I enjoyed a lot: the public's behavior was preety respecful , and this situation allawed the presenter to feel more confident which made him to improve his performance. He started to speak up louder and more confident. Everyone enjoyed the experience and learned about how important is to be prepared for speaking in front of a group of smart people. Thank you.

topic: Writing a report on staff satisfaction
text: English has played a major role in my life. I started studying it when I was 12 years old at the elementary school. I learned the most English when I was at the University. Then I got my first job, and I didnt use the English there. After some years, I had some difficulties to talk with people who speak English fluently. In my current job the English is mandatory to get a better position; so Im studying hard to go up as soon as possible. I think that my constant efforts to learn English made a big difference to get my last promotion. In fact, this year Im going to take some training in the US. Even though the process has frequently been frustrating and painful, Im grateful I spend so much time learning English. It has changed my life and I guess that I began to get the rewards.

I graduate from college in psycology with a background in human resources. My experience had been development in financial companies, most of them banks; previously I had been working for a marketing company. Prior of that I was intern at a humanity organization where give attention to women who were domestic violence victims. That was a wonderful experience because I could help to many women to overcome their situation and at the end of therapy they felt more sure of themselves and more confident. Unfortunately my intern finished and the organization didn''t have financial resources to hiring me. So I started to looking job met the human resources world. Subsequently I got into to organizational development where I help to employees to grow in professional and personal plane. I love my work because I have the opportunity to help people too. However in the future, and when I have more time, I would like to be a voluntary at any humanity organization.

Two weeks ago I started in a new position in the company where I work. Beginning on my first day, Peter, one of my new subordinates began a series of troubling comments and actions including: 1) In our first meeting he stated that the loss of the previous person in my position (his old boss) was a big one for the company. He didn''t help me, rather he said that the two previous individuals hadn''t lasted long. 2) He took the seat at the head of the table at our first staff meeting before I arrived in the room. 3) He usually claims that he doesn''t understand what I am asking for or what I'm saying and he says that he is not going to do so. Unfortunately, however, he was apparently hired by our company''s founder Mr. Lopez who still owns a good portion of the company and is still very politically powerful. I heard that when he had a problem with his previous boss he went straight to Mr. Lopez, and my predecessor was fired soon after. I''m shocked that his acting in this way so early, but what I should do?

Mean and women gender difference is still today a hot topic in Mexico. For instance, men usually earn about 10% more than women are in equal executive roles in business. The house cleaning chores are usually performed about 70% by women; while at the same time men get more liberty. For instance men may get home later, they usually watch TV or uses computer while women take care of the kids. It''s true that women are gaining positions in politic positions, but still you can see that the main roles are taken by men, while women are not taken seriously for example for the Presidency of Mexico. Gender equality has improved in Mexico, but our "macho" tradition in movies and TV is still against these efforts.

At childhood I knew the marionettes or puppets that are moved it with threads at itinerant theaters on the villages and towns. This performing art is not unique of my country, because it was originated in Egypt, China,Greece and Rome, and was very popular. At the middle-age this performing art reached great diffusion at Italy, Spain, France, Portugal, England, Germany, Netherlands, Austria and Turkey. At many  countries of Europe there were various itinerant marionettes theaters, just as the English punchinello, named 'Punch'; Karagoz at Turkey; Petrushka at Russia; Hansworth at Germany. It knows two kinds of marionettes; one of them is moved it with threads and the other one is moved it with a hand, the last is known as Guiol Theater and has reached a high grade of perfection and it has been utilized at the opera and the ballet, and at concert-hall imitations, be highlighted the Italian Piccoli and French Waltons. At my coountry,Mexico, this is the performing art that I knew when I was a child and I remember with affection.

Is gender equality still a problem in Mexico? Sure it is. There aren't many women in leading roles in business and politics, And that's because Mexico is still very sexist. Of course there are a few leading women in the media and politics. However, you can say that the road they took, was more difficult than the road that most men go through. The good thing is that that tendency has been changing with the younger generations. Now, the number of women and men studying in universities is almost equal. Many young women are making a big effort to make a living by themselves. Things are changing slowly for good.

text: I think it is easy to be a leader, but what is difficult is to be a good leader. It is not all about commanding people. Leadership means to help others, understanding situations, comprehending people, sharing ideas, sharing hardship, organizing everything among others characteristics a leader has to have. Some people are born to be leaders and it is very easy for them to do it while others have to take courses and training to be leaders. In my particular case I think I was born to be just a follower of somebody else, but I am willing to take the time, training and get the skills necessary to become not only a leader but a very good one. I also believe that a leader has to be adaptable because people is always variable, some times it is hard to earn trust, confidence and respect. Patience is very helpful in those cases. It is not easy to become a leader as it is not to stay in that position.

In my country, Mexico, when attending a dinner party it is impolite to arrive too early or too late, but, curiously, arriving at the precisely hour is not as good as arriving a few minutes later. Regarding the attire, it should be appropriate depending on the occasion: for a casual dinner with friends, feel free to dress as you want, but, for a business party it is recommended that you follow the guidelines provided by your company. Should a guest bring along some other friends to the party? Never, unless you have asked in advance to the host and received the permission to do so. Nowadays it is common to meet with friends in their houses in a relaxed way and every guest take to the party some food to share, or even people order food from a restaurant and everyone contribute to pay the bill. In my case, I dont like cooking, so, what I do to please my guests is to order some special dishes they like, such as a lasagna or sushi and, for dessert, a mango pie or guava pie, the flavor of the last one is really out of this world!

In this all years that I live in Monterrey, I just haven't been invited a party where people's behavor, but I would like to chare some table manners that are considered very important at any moment. First of all you have to eat slowly, besides it is good for your healt, you must to do it, I mean you have to take care the way you eat and what spon you should use for each meal, food or what ever. It's very important to seat correctly, if you go for an informal party you have to take a gift, but it shoulb be too expensive. If you go with your girlfriend or boyfriend be careful, you'll be observed and probably you'll be graded in the way you react. good luck

In general, I think I have a really great life. I have a good job, that although it does come with certain amount of stress, it''s completely related to my studies, it''s well paid and is near from home. I''m also married with a great man that I know he loves me back. However, sometimes I wonder if my life would have been better if I would have made a master degree. Now I see that some friends from work earn more money than me, only because they have their master title, doing exactly the same job, and that situation makes me regret about it. But when I really think about it, if I would have made my master degree, maybe I wouldn''t be married with the man of my dreams, and that thought reassures me, and makes me think in how happy and thankful I am with my life now.

The cost of living is definitely  an ongoing issue. And now days the bottom dropped out of the global economy. This fact has strongly affected to my country, all the taxes have gone up and are continually going up. I think I am lucky to have a roof over my head and food on the table, unfortunately I'm going through a tough time right now, as I'm unemployed and I strapped for cash, I must ask my parents for a loan, but I don't be sure because I'm suppose to be independent, but this situation left me no option.However We need to be optimistic, I hope things turn around sooner.

I have a really good life, Im an accountant for a financial company, and my wife and I are owners of a comfortable house. I have certain amount of stress from work, although I do take summers off every year. The fact is weve got everything we need and more. We got married so young and by this time we have three wonderful children. We have work so hard to build a family and to reach all our dreams. As out children are becoming more independent, our social life is rather intense. As a result, work, family, social life, and so on, normally I get so tired by every weekend. In the end, I realized that I dont have any time to have any kind of crisis or thing in thinks like those. I know that life isnt perfect, and day by day Im learning to enjoy the good things and the difficult or bad things too. I dont have any regret about my entire life, and I really happy about it and it makes me feel fulfilled.

text: I've been hooked to technology since a log time. About 10 years ago I bought my  first Palm device. It was a black an white screen but it was really helpful for me. During that time I changed three different Palm devices, every new one was even better then the old one. I got to the point that I needed it for everything, I mean for my work and for personal things. Now I have a smart phone which is really great, but I can say I miss my old Palm device. Anyway, technology has been really useful for my job, my cell phone has turned into my personal secretary. When I'm away from my cell phone or my tablet everything go crazy because there's a lot of information that I need for my job on those devices. I don't usually go into social network but if I need to I can do it wherever I want. One big disadvantage about technology is that if you are away from it, seem like it's the end or the world.

I would like to talk about the Dance of Flyers in Spanish named Danza de los voladores or los voladores de Papantla. Dance of Flyers is one-of-a-kind dance around the world, which is an ancient Mexican or Mesoamerican ceremony that still performed currently. The ritual of Dance of Flyers is related to the fertility, but there is another version that said the ritual is for asking to god rainy because people wanted a severe drought finished. There are five performers and the ritual consists of dance, first, they have to climb of a 30 meter pole from which four of five performers launch themselves tied with ropes in their feet to descend little by little to the ground while descending they turn around the pole . The fifth performer remains on top of the pole, dancing and playing a flute and drum. You can see this genuine art performing at El tajn an archeological ruins located in Veracruz Mexican State, near to Papantla city.

In my opinion, all people we have an awkward situation in ours life. It's difficult to believe but rarely people can claim something easily. Normally, when we want to get an important thing we must to work pretty hard, then we learn that the best experiences in our life we have in awkward situations. Frequently, it can serve for lifelong. As many people, I've had difficulties situations. One of this was when my lovely son was born. Thanks God for give me my best gift!

Hi Claudia, good afternoon. Today I am going to talk about a performing art that is unique in Mexico, the Mariachi. The Mariachi is a mexican musician group, originary of Jalisco, especially of Guadalajara. It is very special the type of music, stronger and sentimental or happinest. There are diferent kind of instrument, for example: guittars, violins, trumps, and other. he singer has a stronger voice. The custom componens are jacket, pants and a big hat. The colors of the costum varies, and depends of the occasion. My favorite Mariachi is the Mariachi Vargas of Tecalitlan, this group exist since 1800's. The singer how I most like it is Vicente Fernandez, his voice is unique in the world, he partipated is many movies, but is not a good actor. I guess, invited you to Guadalaja for heard some of this Mariachi bands, and drink some diferents kinds of Tequila. Thank you.
