text: This graph shows the trends for the National and International Sales Figures for the Best Books Company. International Sales Figure: 2004-2005: Sales increased dramatically for this period up to 2.7 million, however in the second quarter of 2005 book sales fell sharply to about 1 million, remaining steady until 2007 where once again there was a significant decrease of about half a million but sales have been steady during 2008. National Sales Figure: 2004-2005: Book sales have increased gradually to 1 million.  2005-2006: Sales have been steady. 2006-2007: Sales increased dramatically to reach about 3 million and after that, there was a sharp decrease to 1.5 million followed by a slight decrease during 2008.

text: REPORT: GENERAL EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION AT HANS AND SONS TERMS OF REFERENCE. PROCEDURE: The topics included satisfaction with: 1 Employee basic paclage: including pay scale, number of vacation days, sick days and overtime. 2 Employee advanced package: pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. FINDINGS: 1 Employee basic package: -The employees were satisfied with pay scales, number of vacation days and sick days. 2 Employee advanced package: -Most employees were satisfied with the pension plan, health benefits and shares scheme. 3 Working environment: -Most employees were satisfied with office equipment. -Most complained that they didn't receive enough feedback. CONCLUSIONS: -Employees needs feeling more important at the company. -Need receive more appreciation of his achievements to feel comfortable at work. RECOMMENDATIONS:  -Remove some levels of management. -Introduce additional informal weekly feedback meetings. -Allow employees to become eligible for the advanced package after 18 months.

Really, woman''s place is in the home?, I don''t think so. Gender roles in Mxico are changing, for a lot of years our culture had been defined by Machismo, with the wrong thought that women should stay at home taking care of children and her housband, keeping all the house clean. And for that reason , woman did not have right to give her opinion. In 1953 women gained the right to vote and are becoming involved in political and social movements. Nowadays women prefer to work instead of tolerating a male who wants to repress her, women are becoming more independent and spending more time outside the home.

I'm a computer science engineer, I had been studying at the Juarez University for 4 years. Afterward, I had been looking for jobs according with my career without any success, prior to that I had been working as a computer operator keyboarder at Datamark when I got married, six months after I finished my career, one friend recommended myself to send a resume to the Tatung company and after 1 week, I got a job. As some people say, the office is the new or modern jungle, msurvival of the fittest. This is not literally like in the jungle when a lion hunt a little deer or something like that. Rather, there are many deceptive strategies, some people see them as tricky and other persons see them as neccesarily. So, judge yourself. My philosophy is, talking about of climb to the top, be yourself, because if you act like anybody else and you have success with that character, late or early you will tired of acting so try to be yourself is my best advice. I strongly believe in the law of Karma; based in cause and effect so if you are doing wrong things you probably will receive bad or wrong things and vice versa. So I'll give one lesson today: Most of the people don'treally want to be successful, they only want it a little, not completely. They prefer more go out and spend time in banalities as have better clothes, get the best car, have the most expensive house. Indeed, one day my dear father, rest in peace, tell me these wise words: Many individuals of the human race, intoxicated with vanity, have neglected the economy, and, by holding a brief moment its empty pretense of independence, have exhausted all their resources, spending it on frivolities.%%

topic: Helping a coworker deal with a phobia
text: If you ask me How technology gas affected my life? I think it has affected a lot, all days and almost all the time I use technology. When I wake up the first thing I do is to see an smartphone app that works with sleep measures, there you can see how good did you sleep. After that I take a shower while I listen music with bluetooth speakers into the bathroom. Then I turn on the TV to see the news while I'm having breakfast. I go to my office and there I usea a laptop to do my work, receive and send e-mails, and many things more. When I get out of the office I usually go to exercise, running or climbing while I listen music in my iPod, and at the same time I have an app to measure my heart beating and my physical condition.

Well, talking about awkward moments, I could say that having a relationship with my second ex-girlfriend was one. My relationship with her at first was a charming, sweet, adventurous, like a fairytale. Anyway, I spent with her one year and a half, a year that I fell in love so much and I fought for her in many awkward situations (taking her out of a party because she was pretty drunk, believing in her about her "special friends" and the most awkward was seeing her many times with her ex-boyfriend surprisingly). However, I think the most awkward moment but not EPIC was one day when I was at college and a friend of mine told me she saw her with her ex-boyfriend a day that she supposed to be traveling, anyway, we made a plan to find the truth about her lie. We were waiting for her outside of our classroom and my friend said, hey whats up?! I think I saw you last Friday on that street, then, she looked anxious and said, I was in Monterrey, she changed the subject so quickly and she ignored us leaving the hall with a friend. I felt bad and so awkward.%%

I graduated from college with a background in computer science. I started my intership at governmental organization. After finishing my intership I was hired in the same organization in a Human Resources position. What a fantastic job! Since I am working in Human Resources I realized that's what I should have studied from the beginning because I love my job. When I was working at goverment I didn't have any benefit, only my salary, so I decided to look for another job with benefits. I went to several interviews and finally a manufacturing company made me a job offer as a recruiter. After two years of working there, I got a very good job offer from a marketer company as recruiting coordinator. For the last three years I have hired all staff of the company and have been working in retention programs. I definitely love my job. I hope to have another job soon with more responsibilities.

I live in Mexico, during our last government we have received new changes in the law. For Instance there is a new law for all small business specially those who are not regulated to do the necessary arrangements in order to be on the list of regulated locals if they don't do that they are going to be penalized for the state with a very expensive fine. Also we have received a new law for telecommunications it will provide a better service regarding TV, internet and telephone. On this regard people are happy whit this new law that allows to everyone to have a cheaper prices in this services. Finally here in Mexico the 27* article of our constitution has been changed. People are really mad because of this because there is the idea that as a country we are going to lose our principal source of income that is, petroleum. I hope that all of this be a rumor because it will be a disaster for all our country if we loses the most productive thing that we have here in Mexico.

I have a really good lifestyle. I'm the principal private secretary of a hospital. I dont pay rent and have no kids. I still live with my mom. I have a certain amount of stress from work , I only take vacations 9 dias a year and, I think deserve more days. It really affects my social life because I dont have a full time work, my schedule is broken . I have to come in the morning four hours and then I go back to home and come again until night so, I dont have enough time to have a social life on the week. I really like my life, I have everything I thought. I bought a car, I have traveled and I can buy everything I want to, however, I would like to get a schedule change and get paid more. Life isn't free, if we want something we have to make an effort and work for it.

Well, I think in Mexico there are not so many differences between men and women, actually both assume the same charges in so many areas. The only important detail that I have noticed it's that usually men take the most relevant charges, what i mean?, I said for example, the president has been always a man, the higher positions inside the country also men. Nevertheless, I have also noticed that women are gaining terrain in some work areas that normally were for men, like gas stations(I had never seen one working in a GS), or some positions in the state, etc.  Okay, that's my perspective about gender differences in my country, I confess that I disown a little about this issue, but anyway, it was a good opportunity to reflect about it.

I remember a long time ago, I was living with my parents in Mexico, growing up with them. I was at second or third grade in school, I can not recall exactly. We, the students at the classroom were announced a performance was going to take place the next day. It was going to be a magician with a white talking rabbit. The time arrived and my mother had bought the ticket for me to enter and see the magic show. When I was in front of the show I could not believe I was looking at. I was like, Oh my god!. I really was so innocent, I was a young child. The magician was performing tricks of magic like, taking out doves from a hat, taking roses from I do not know where. I had never seen something like that in my life. I was really impressed and asking myself, Is it real? Is that possible?. Then, the magician also was a ventriloquy, you know, the man was speaking so that the voice seemed to come from a living rabbit. That was crazy and completely unbelievable for me at that age. How in the world a rabbit could listen to and answer questions?%% I thought. For me, the show was authentic, not only because the magician was an expert but costumes and choreography were fascinating, as well.

I've been in many awkward situations in all my life, but I will tell you the story what happened to me in a social meeting with my wife's colleagues. Well as far as I remember the meeting was supposed to be in a restaurant, but suddenly a close friend from my wife decided to change the place to our house, because the house was given to us since by then, so we didn't have any choice, then all her colleagues started to arrive on time and everybody brought different kind of meals and snacks. Everything was ok until I started to drink alcohol with no limits, and that's where I started to behave myself in an inappropriate way, and I made my wife to experience an awkward situation, I didn't realized that everybody was trying to avoid me, because maybe they didn't wanted to make my wife to feel more ashamed, I don't remember what happened later, but I feel ashamed writing this story right now, because in that time I didn't behaved in an appropriate manner. What I recommend is that when you're the host, you never have to overpass the alcohol limits or you'll be in a situation likely mine.%%

topic: Finding a home for a wealthy client
text: Four years I ago, I used to take up rock climbing.  I had a friend who is an expert on this sport, so he introduced me to that and took me to different places to practice and face my fear to heights. I remember how I felt when I crossed a defined height, and I used to feel a debilitating fear and my legs started shaking.  However, in that moment I thought that was the perfect opportunity to get over my fear of heights and tried to focus only in reach the top. I regret of stop doing that sport because besides of the rush that feels when you are on top, it is a very good strenght exercise and also mental because you need to be completely focused in your movements, therefore is a good chance to forget the problems and be in a kind of meditating moment.

Many scientific reasearch have proved that smoking is very harmful to people. Because it is hard for people to get rid of this unhealthy habit, some countries have passed laws which ban people from smoking in public places and office buildings. In my opion, this policy is very necessary and worthy. It is the matter of our health. Some people say that they feel relived and relaxed when smoking in that it can help them forget their daily problems. Yes, this is one of smokings advantage. However, if one keeps smoking over a long time, he can grow lots of types of diseases. He might lose his strength gradually though hardly can he aware of this. He is easily affected by other severe diseases. And it costs him lots of money for treatment. Above all, he loses his health which goes and never comes back. Health is obviously the most valuable treasure in our lives.

In Peru, a South American country, there is still a gap of inequality in gender roles. Society still expects to shape the behavior of males and females differently. Women are supposed to be more patient, more faithful, more invested in family and relationships. In the other hand, men are expected to be the main financial supporters of their homes. This difference is even bigger in rural areas where is common to observe more traditional values. However, in the cities the generalization of education makes possible for men and women to obtain a degree and gain independence through a job. Many women in Peru raise their kids alone, so they are the main financial source of income for their families. There is also more representation of women running the government of the country, although until now there hasn't been any woman president. There is still work to do for gender equality.

When I started attending college, I was not aware of office politics. Indeed, I thought at first that office politics only had to do with jobs ; however, the reality is that in college there are special interests by students everywhere. A clear example is a job I wanted in the management department of my college. I was the best candidate for it; by contrast, a friend of mine was a candidate as well, and once she got to know that I was about to be awarded with the job, she started to influence in the decision by getting to know the people who would decide who would get the job. Indeed, she got the job. From that moment to now , I've learnt that everyone is a participant in office politics, even if you are now aware of it.  So, what I started to do was to get to know my circle of influence, and try to listen to others, so they can listen to me, too.

To create a successful project an soli teamwork, I propose that compnies use the SWOT analysis technique. SWOT helps a business evaluate internal an external strengths, weaknesses, opportunities an threats. Let's say you carry out a SWOT analysis at the beginning of a project. The first thing is to make sure that project goals are clear. Then, I' say you shoul make a list of questions to satisfy each element of the SWOT analysis. You want to make sure all the major project tasks are covered and identify possible risks. My thought then woul be to take SWOT even further. Team members coul collaborate on a SWOT analysis of the team itself, making them a stronger team and developing mutual trust. For example, if the team sepnds time identifying their strengths, then the resulting information can actually motivate the team. And, as they indentify weaknesses, they become more aware of how to prevent future problems. Once you complete a SWOT analysis, I suggest that you review it frequently. Change the questions an answers as the market changes, and focus on taking advantage of your strengths and opportunities and reducing your weaknesses and threats.

One of the most representative performing art in my country is "Mariachi". The Mariachi is a world-renowned Mexican folk music that combined different instruments. Usually, Mariachi includes many musicians who played guitars, violins, trumpets and sometimes flutes. Another important element that distinguishes Mariachi is the use of charro outfit. The Mariachis wear a traditional suit black or white with gold-colored buttons in the side of legs and arms and pointy boots. Commonly, lyrics talk about love, disputes for a woman, and stories about Mexican history, for example: Mexican Revolution. The Mariachi became popular around the world in the early 90s thanks to Mexican movies. Actually, in 2011 UNESCO recognized the Mariachi as an Intangible Cultural Heritage. It is important to mention that Mariachi in its origins was form only by men, but now you can see women performing this kind of music. Nowadays, some people used to listen Mariachi on weddings, birthdays and relevant commemorations.

How was gender in Mexico in 1950?  I have talked to my grandparents about gender during those years, and I did not like what they told me. Women could not vote. They did not have any kind of rights as part of a society. Men did all the decisions within a family. Women could not think about working as men could do it. They were limited for studying, because they should remain keeping house. At this time, most of the above has changed. Women have gained many rights equal to men. They have gained the right to vote, study, and work in same functions as men. Currently over 70% of women are female workers. I think the era of male chauvinism in our society is changing every day more and more in favor of women.

I started with the main milestones of the process that ran for year and a half, studying different technologies and services providers. I summarized the main characteristics of each one of them, pros and cons in a chronological order. Once I finished the explanation I continued with the justification about the selected wireless technology, talking about how widespread it was in the country, the availability of maintenace services, the costs and how should fit in the production process. I received good comments at the end of the meeting and the presentation I gave was the taken as the baseline for further explanations about the project.

Personal Finances is an ongoing issue for me; Since I was a child I used to be worried about it, my family was a normal class: no poor no rich, but I wanted more, and I understood very earlier that if I would be rich in the future, I have to be smart with my personal finances. The basic situation before and now is:  1.-spend less than your salary, it is always possible to reduce some expenses 2.-It's important to have a budget, that help you to see very clear your situation 3-. Pay always taxes, insurances,and mortgage 4.- Finally try to save money for a future emergency, you never know... And remember, money isn't everything but you need it!!!

One of the strictest laws in Mexico is the Anti-kidnapping law. Kidnapping is considered as a crime where a person is taken by force, intimidation or deception against their will to an undisclosed place. This law was updated recently in Mexico to prevent the great quantity of kidnappings that are occurring along the country. Due to kidnapping is considered as a serious crime, this law stipulates severe penalties for violation of its provisions and establishes a prison term of 20 to 40 years for anyone convicted, however if the victim is deprived of life, the prison term could be from 40 to 70 years. I think this law is very important because it has helped to decrease kidnapping victims due to criminals that know how severe will be the punishment if they are arrested, however the authorities have been overwhelmed by increases in violent crime related to narco-terrorist bands, while corruption and incompetence have originated a justice system weak which is extremely frustrating for all the people.

I studied Spanish Philology at the National University of Colombia. Prior to study to become Spanish teacher I would like to learn english language in London. Unfortunately, it was difficult going there because the cost of living is very expensive. So, I decided to study here in Colombia. Afterward, I moved to Argentina and I had the opportunity to work in a publishing house. That helped me to adquire a lot of knowledge and experience regarding grammar correction, literature, writers and all about books. However, in 2008 the world economy fell, the real state bubble burst in United States and that affected many other countries, including Argentina. Consequently. I went back to Colombia and I got some jobs in schools, but that kind of job is a bit hard and unsteady. Because of that, I prefered to change my job. Previously to study at the university, I learn some trades, one of them was knitting and then, I started to knit and sell handmade things. It was a good time because I had my own time and business. Afterward, I moved to another city. In fact, I am living here now and I am supporting an interesting well-paid work that helps me to be even happier than before.%%

One day my brother told me a very hilarious story about an awkward moment he had when he was in highschool. It was during the break when he saw two curly hair people sitting together. He believed that those people were his classmate''s brother and his classmate. They really resembled to each other. Hence, my brother had a bright idea, he ran toward them and pulled their hair. Afterwards, a woman scream voice came out from one of the two sitting people. The thing is that in that highschool not only were the curly brothers, there their mom was too. And of course, she was curly too. It turned out that the sitting people were my brother''s classmate and his mother (the person who screamed). All the people who were surrounding the situation started laughing at my brother, he was ashamed. It was so awkward for him. However, luckily for him, the classmate''s mother started laughing too, so she undestood that my brother was confused. Ever since that moment, my brother does not pull anybody''s hair anymore.

I live in a city where the lifestyle is too quickly and stressful, here in Mxico city is quite difficult to get a good balance between your work and your personal life, but not imposible. However, Inspite of it's hard to get enough free time for doing everything I want, I'm happy with my lifestyle. I work in weekdays, from 9 am till 7 pm. I've two free hours for lunch. Further of those hours, I spend driving one hour in the mornings, and another one at night. I'm a busy man, like to many people who're living here. I don't have enough time for me, but I'm a organized type who can get some free time for do things that I like to do. For instance, in weekdays, before I go to the office, I like to do some exercise in the mornings, then after work I see my mother and my brother. Even on Thursday I usually hang out with some of my friends, and on weekends I see my girlfriend all day long. Work brings me a certain lot of stress but I'm healthy and happy, I can handle my expenses and saving some money every month.%%

In Mexico there's a lot o difference between men and women's roles. Women have to take care of the children, cock and clean the house, whereas men have to go to work and provide money. But is it really that way? Things have been changing for the past decades. Before that, women weren't to be studying after they were 12 years old because their parents didn't allow that, or because women thought it senseless to study more. Nowdays, most women go to the college and have their titles, and even have very important jobs, with the same pay than men. But there still being a lot of discrimination.  The fact is, many woman still living in the traditional way, and they hardly go out of the house. But the majority of them are bending to conclude their careers and find a good job. Sometimes the woman works and the man stay at home (really the opposite way). However, you could hardly see that, now both man and woman play almost the same roles.

I could say the place is perfect to those people wants to live in quiet place,honestly I never heard that there is a performing art close to my province. as I know is in the city 9 hours by car going to that place,tinikling is an old performance art to phillipines culture,its a dance using bamboo holding two person side by side and a woman dancing between while  holding candles,the show is all about the traditional dance, I've watch one time together with my aunt, far but it's well worth I like it very much,frankly,there's absolutely nothing else like tinikling in the world,if I could have a chance someday I can go back with my children to watch a performance culture,

Recently, I moved to another city because I wanted to support a voluntary work. I thought I could get a job soon, but the bottom dropped out of schools in this end of year, and I have to wait until the next year activities start. Anyway, I had to keep a roof over my head, and I worried about what I could get here within my budget. Fortunately, although the cost of living is too high in this city, I got a place where I am living now in a beautiful neighborhood. I spend $150 a month on rent, but the best is that my landlord is very kind. And I always have food on the table because I still have some savings. I am sure that I will pick up some work soon and the things will be looking up when I have and income. Meanwhile, I am busy participating actively in my work as volunteer. That makes me happy.

topic: Applying to be a fitness trainer
text: I recently attended a representation about our team procedures.I have to admire how calm the presenter was.her voice was clear and strong,and she looked at people around the room instead of just staring at her notes.Another thing she did well was to summarize her key pints in the conclusion.I left the presentation remembering each important idea.And her flowcharts were compelling.One of them perfectly explained a procedure I had never really understood before.On the other hand,one thing she could have done differently was to grab people's attention during the opening.If she had given us an interesting fact or figure,for exa,ple,I think some people would have felt more relaxed and motivated.Finally,I would have liked it if she had given more exampled of the payroll procedures.I'm still not sure how to complete a time report.Overall,the presentation was very helpful and well prepared.I learned a lot.

Recently, I have started to think about the things that really matter on life. Many people say that it is health, love, friends, family, and etcetera. Obviously, that''s true. However, it is common that those people are only care about money, power and look nice for others. In my opinion, it is necessary to make aware that the only objective on life is to be happy. It is easy to say, but not to understand. Be happy it is an election, not a condition, not a situation. It's simple, people who want to be happy is happy. When you are happy, everything else it is alright. For me, understand the importance of being happy is a process. Consequently, every morning when I wake up, I express gratitude for all I have. I try to be positive. Actually, when I feel angry or sad, I try activities thats make me happy like listening music. It is clear, life it is not a rehearsal. We have only one chance to live.

Nayarit, a small state, is located at the west coast of Mexico in front of the Pacific Ocean. The Occidental Sierra Madre cross the state through south-north, there on the mountains are living the Coras tribe. This people has ancient rituals to celebrates religious ceremonies, parties and all kind of festivities. The most important celebration take place on Holy Week, during the holy days: People has a religious ceremony afterward start to dance, drink alcohol, chew Peyote, since Thursday to Saturday. On Sunday they go to the Pacific Ocean to baptize children and to make offerings to the sea. The Chaman, a kind of priest and wish, is the ceremonies officer. Like many natives customes, there are a lot of mysticism and mysterious. The religion is a mixed of catolicism and their own believes. However, this people is peaceful and have many poverty. The women working making craftsmanship. The men go down to work in agriculture jobs. This story is not a performing art, but is a piece of culture whose characters are persons.

In Mexico gender equality is improving, now many women have more chances to have better jobs and be more recognize. In the 90,s gender diffrences were more common, men had better jobs and the women only could help in the house. Now women and men work the same, and sometimes women work more than men. Mexico has very important women, that had fight for her rights, and the law recognize the equality that exists today. But the problem is not solved 100%, there are some cases were women suffer discrimination at their works and to acces for some services. Perhaps now we could have a woman as president.

In Mexico , four of every 10 Mexican women over 14 years are part of the economically active population (PEA ) , however the lack of equality with men makes them feel frustrated occupationally , the study sheds " Mexican woman , success and competitiveness " the firm Adecco. 62% of women in the country say they do not have the same recognition as men in professional matter . Two out of 10 women are not considered successful by the lack of job opportunities (59 %), neglect of family ( 22%) and gender preference for certain positions ( 22%) , according to the document. The presence of women has grown rapidly in the productive sectors , however three out of 10 respondents acknowledged feeling ' stuck ' in their profession for lack of opportunities for promotion and salary increases ( 51%) and lack of professional recognition ( 29%). " New generations are facing demands for increased academic preparation and meet certain aesthetic and physical characteristics ". Who knows? Maybe someday we'll have gender equality.

Gender roles in Mexico are changing. In the past most of the people believes that women place is in the home taking care of their child and maintain a neat house. Nowadays there are many women in leading roles in business, art and politics. In 1953 women gained the right to vote and stand for election and despite the majority of Mexico leaders are still men, women has been involved in political and social movements. Although women in Mexico are experiencing some advances in terms of status and human rights, daily life is still fraught with challenges and dangers. There are a lot of chauvinist people that try to denigrate and humiliate women; but who knows? maybe someday we'll have gender equality.

Talking about office politics is a little bit tough for me, because I always have seen people taking advantage of somebody else fails, backstabbing them, obviously. I have been working for 16 years for the same company. Dealing with people, playing the game of office politics. One of the reasons I have been promoted several times is that I work directly with the company owners, a couple of married associates that love to see loyalty people moving ahead in strategies, respecting others, get along with people, working hard every single day, gaining their respect. We were a very small company working in harmony almost without errors. The problem came when the company started to grow up. Some people were hired and they came with other ideas, backstabbing became a form of ascend the hierarchy ladder. When someone in the company is making a good job, everything is okay, but in the other hand if someone makes a mistake, he or she will be seen a weak competitor and there will be a great opportunity to advance. In my point of view that is unfair and that behavior should not be practice by anyone in a company.

Years ago, the law department of our country passed a law that prohibits smokers from doing it on public spaces. In my view Im with that law. I really hate smokers around me. What are they thinking about? They just smoke wherever they want without caring about others. Not all people like the smoke smell. There are special places for that purpose. Even so, they still do whatever they desire. In their point of view, I bet most smokers will be pissed off because of the new law. I mean, smoking is like a drug. A drug that you just can''t be left easily. They try hard to get rid of that habit. Maybe government should spend money and time to help people to get rid of cigarette. That way, there will be no smokers left. I mean, there are good people out there that are friendly and easy to get along. And I think its unfair to push them aside just because their smoking.

Life has been one of my biggest concerns ever of my day-to-day grind. There is no day in which I do not think about what is going on in all of the aspects of my life whether it is social, educational, spiritual, or health. However, even though I think much of what I am doing with my life, I have learned that one of the most important rules to remember is enjoying every single day as it were the last is worth considering. So, my day is always full of goals as they are learning as much as possible in my classes, greeting friends, being organized, being friendly, and being thankful with God for what I have. If I could give any advise to others, I would say: "Enjoy life! It is too short to worry too much for the future, and not worth worrying for the past."

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: Leader. Now a days everyone used it but, do we really know what it means? For me a leader must have the followings characteristics:  Empathy. A leader must be able to understand that the people that are in their team have feelings and problems as anyone else, have good days and some others must have more difficult days than others.  Communicate and guide. A leader must be able to communicate with their team, have to guide them in order to obtain the results he expect from them, reach the goals using only the resources needed.  Motivator. A leader have to know what motivates their team in order to give personal or group recognitions for doing a good job like a Diploma, or give economic recognitions individually or group for goals completed, or a promotion or I don't know, the leader must have to identify what motivates the whole team.

I live in Mexico. I'm 22 years old and I'm still a student, so I hear a lot of points of view about the new law in my city. The new law says that If you are drunk and you drive, you have to pay a lot of money in order to don't go to jail. In my personal point of view this kind of laws are necessary in the city but I think It has a little exaggeration. You only have to drink 2 beers and wait certain time in order to be able to drive a car. My friends are too angry because of the law, they said it is so strict and it is a lot of money. Government says that accidents decreased since this law started and they don't stop doing this. The law works like this: There is a patrol in some place and nobody knows where it's going to stay, in that way police officers can arrest and check any person. They use an instrument in which people have to blow and It says what is the alcohol level. After that, the officer says if you are able to go away or you and your car are in problems.%%

I got mi bachelor''s degree at intercontinental university in 2005. My first job was on teaching philosophy informally. In that time, i was living in Mexico City. Afterwards, i go back to my home in Monterrey and i began to work as a teacher in several universities. Until 2007 I had been taught subjects in psychology, philosophy and culture. I enjoyed to travel around the world and teach about different cultures. Following my initial plans, i decided to study a masters degree in pshychoterapy. It''s start at 2008 and finish at 2011. Since i finished my masters, my professional paths has been growing up. Now, i teach philosophy at morning and make pshychoterapy at evenings. I have really enjoyed my career elections and my next personal goal will be to finish my doctorate in psychology. I think that to teach about what human being is, should be consider  invaluable and very important. My principal goal in life is to open my own office to offer consultation about human process involved in enterprises. I think people must comprehend something: as human being you have to develop in production, but it''s more fundamental to grow in humanity.

In Mexico there are a lot of different kinds of dinner party, I can classify them in formal and informal parties. I am 22 years old and most of my friends' parties are informal, so we could go to the party only with the drink you want to enjoy while the party. There are no important rules about this informal situations, an easy one could be give greeting when you arrive or when you leave, you must say hello and goodbye to everybody. Although If you don't say goodbye anything bad happens, your friends will call you the next day or later to ask what time you left the party the day before. When I go to a formal dinner you should go very-well dressed, and you should bring a small present for your hosts, as a red wine or something to eat, like a pie or cake. You should know how to use the soup spoon, knife, fork and dessert spoon and where they are. If you don't know how to act in a certain situation, you should watch what other people do, and the you can do the same. If you come to Mexico there are a lot of informal parties for young people where you don't have to worry about these kind of stuff.%%

I think I have a wonderful life. Despite the stress from the university and my job, I have free time to go out with my boyfriend and friends. With my job and the university, right now I am pretty burned out and I do not have enough time to do exercise; so I am not a jock either. Dear Janice: I write this note to apologize for Selma's behavior last night. I am terribly sorry for her insensitivness when she insisted on talking about racial issues. I want to explain you the reasosn for putting her foot in it. She has a job as a secretary of inmigration, so, the only thing she cant talk about is racial differences and problems of identity. Anyway she shouldn' have said those awful things about afroamericans as foreigners. She should have realized that she was overstepping the mark. I want to promise you than it would never happen again, and that she is very sorry and would like to apologize by herself, if you give her the opportunity. I hope that you won't take it too serious and we will continue seeing each other. Once again, I wish you could accept my sincere apologies.  With best wishes, Mary

When you receive and invitation for a dinner party its proper to answer the invitation, no matter how time but it has to be before de date. If you accept to go, you don't have to bring a gift with you, tipically in Mexico all the celebrations o partys are paid by the host without any expectation of receive something. If you are going to a dinner party you have to arrive on time, if you are late, you must call or send a text message to apologize for the inconvenience. Once you are at the dinner party, you have to use silverware in the right way, avoid talking with mouth full and interrupt conversations. In Mexico we used to talk about general situations at the table, except politics and religion, this is seeking to prevent a discussion. Whe you think is time to leave, you must thank to the host for all the attentions.

If you are a foreign visitor and you are invited to a social occasion you must follow the next advices for attending an invitation.  Accept and attend the invitation.  Ask the host if you could go with some or take someone to the party. Its well known that mexicans are great hostess and that we accept unexpected guests but some people are very traditional and could take it wrong if you dont ask them first.  Bring some potato chips, soda or even a bottle of wine or alcohol (depends of the occasion).  Try to socialize and show interest in the party.  Must of all and not less important have fun and enjoy it! Its very rude to be sleepy and being somewhere else except at the party.

I have to be thankful because my lifestyle is really quiet and with just a bit amount of stress. But some months ago, the things were not so. I had to work hardly long hours in a big, cold and stressful city and I felt burned out. However, an opportunity comes to move from that city to Cartagena, a smaller and warmful city. I changed my job to work as volunteer as part of a great educational campaign around the world. Actually, I don't get a lot of money but I have enough to get everything I need and even more. I realized that material things are not the most important in our lives and satisfying our spiritual needs is also important. As a result, I turned into a really happy person. My social life has increased because I have many good friends, so I don't feel alone. Also, I am living in the best neighborhood of Cartagena in the downtown, surrounded by beautiful historical places and houses full of flowers. I really enjoy my walking on its romantic streets. Now, I am sure what I want and I feel nothing is missing in my very meaningful life.%%

A year ago we went into a nearby theater to watch a performing at that my mom and I always wanted to see. The name was La seora Presidenta (Mrs. President). We heard about that performing art years ago. People said that it was one of the funniest Mexican comedies, because they talk about politicians and politics in a funny way. Ive heard about it when I was at high school but. A few years in the past, we realized that performing art will be at our hometown. Unfortunately, by the time we wanted to buy tickets, they were sold-out. This time things were completely different because we bought our tickets as soon as possible. The show started at 7 pm, so we hurried to get in there 30 minutes before the show started. Our sits were at the second floor, so we got an amazing view. The actor is Gonzalo Vega who stars both Martin and Martina who is la seora presidenta. As soon as they started making fun of politicians my mom and I just couldn''t stop laughing during the entire show. It was just amazing. I don''t regret paying $150 pesos for the tickets.

In my country as many countries around the world, talking about politics, religion, and sex most of the time this kind of discussion ends up in awkward situations,  where people offend to each other so these are inappropriate topics for conversations that everybody needs to avoid. When I was younger, I remember I was somewhat fanatic in matters related to religion and politics. When I was 25 I changed my religion from Catholic to Christianity and I always tried my brothers and sisters also did, but I think I wasnt doing appropriately because I criticized their religion saying all mistakes that the catholic church has made along the history, but they were offended. Obviously I felt embarrassed; maybe I shouldnt have done in that way.  Fortunately, when that happened, I immediately asked for apologize and they were able to forgive me. I have learned the lesson: Its not may place to criticize people believes and tell them what to do, just what I can do is to be a good model and being an example of life.

I am convinced I work for the best company all around the world. Why can I say it? Well, the office politics are based on deep roots of ethics principles. In fact, it has been our boss who has set the example and rules in behalf of the organization. One of the main statements in order to receive each other feedback is developing sincere love and empathy for the other employees. "Go and introduce yourself, remember when you came here" and "Share the best you have so that the other to get what he does not have " he says.  As a result, that has contributed to build up unbreakable, exceptional relationships among the coworkers. Nevertheless, sometimes difficulties and disagreements arise. Obviously, we cannot bury our head in the sand. Rather, we should apply these useful advices to overcome those issues: First, keep a regular communication with your boss; he has the power to be your biggest advocate. Second, seek the advice of an experienced colleague: There will always be someone who knows more than you. Third, be always well-informed: ignorance regarding some issues could make you a backstabber. Fourth, speak clearly, face-to-face, if you need to express your feelings, but do it with deep respect, don't humiliate or attack anybody.%% Last, love them: they are working with you shoulder to shoulder.

The last six months have been difficult for me. After eleven year,  I worked for the Ministry of Communications and Transportations, but the new goverment replaced a considered number of personnel in all the country. Last year, Mexico voted for the PRI political party, after 12 year that PAN was in the presidence. Many issues had changed as the energetic law,the  education law and the fighting versus the drugs. But nowadays I still without a job. I do have medical insurance, and savings to afford the payment of my debts, utilities and food. My boyfriend pays the mortgage of the apartment. We need to be thrifty to afford this situation until I  get a employment.

In the US, even in this day and age table manners are considered important. This applies even more when you're eating in a formal situation at someone's house. First of all, if you have any special diet requirements, your hosts will expect to be told about them in advance. Do not spring any last-minute surprises. Take a small gift with you. A bottle of wine, flowers or chocolates are all acceptable. Wait until your hosts being eating, or they say it's okay for you to do so; this shows that you are considerate. Don't make phone calls or send text messages while you are at the table. It's considered extremely bad manners. When it's comes to cutlery, the soup spoon is generally on the right, to the outside of the knife; the fork is on the left; and the dessert spoon is above the dinner plate. However, if you are not sure which of the silverware to use, you can employ a strategy I use myself when in similar situations: Watch what others do, and then do that, too. Good luck, and enjoy your meal.

In the US, even in this day and age table manners are considered important. This applies even more when you're eating in a formal situation at someone's house. First of all, if you have any special diet requirements, your hosts will expect to be told about them in advance. Do not spring any last-minute surprises. Take a small gift with you. A bottle of wine, flowers or chocolates are all acceptable. Wait until your hosts being eating, or they say it's okay for you to do so; this shows that you are considerate. Don't make phone calls or send text messages while you are at the table. It's considered extremely bad manners. When it's comes to cutlery, the soup spoon is generally on the right, to the outside of the knife; the fork is on the left; and the dessert spoon is above the dinner plate. However, if you are not sure which of the silverware to use, you can employ a strategy I use myself when in similar situations: Watch what others do, and then do that, too. Good luck, and enjoy your meal.

In Mexican Republic's Capital,  there is a program called "Hoy No Circula" ("today does not flow") This program includes official rules prohibiting the circulation of vehicles one day a week in the city from Monday to Friday, according with their endings on their plates. In other way on a combination of numbers and colors. This program aims to reduce pollution in the Federal District and improve air quality. Circulation is permitted only to official vehicles and ambulances. In my view it's a great idea and if you ask me, I think this program should apply for two days a week. Because in winter thermal inversion increase pollution. The program includes mandatory rule as the vehicle inspection every six months and personally, I do not agree with this decision, because from my point of view there are many old cars and I think it should be checking every month. Well, if you ask me, there are about 4 million of cars circulating in the D. F. and this amount increase every year, so this is a big problem to solve.

Tax reforms are always controversial. Here in Mexico our president proposed some taxes related to the rental income that is making many people uncomfortable. From an economic view to increase the rental income is quite harmful, it will decline the GDP growth, less investors will bring their companies to Mexico and the middle class is the one that would be more affected; the value added tax in my opinion is a better way to collect money because about 50% of Mexican economy is based in informal business (people that dont register their income and dont pay taxes). From a social view weve studied in some courses (Im a current economics student) that the rental income taxes is one of the ways to redistribute wealth and have more equality among social classes that will reduce poverty. I think that this kind of laws are quite difficult for politicians because they have to decide between helping the economy and helping the poor people that voted for them, in my opinion if you help the economy and education you will help the poor people too as a consequence.

Manners are really important for all the countries around the world, not only in some countries. You just need a couple of hours to know if you are with a polite or impolite person, how do I know it? easy, for example: when you are in a restaurant and your friends is always texting or if he arrives late to the meeting at the restaurant, he is an impolite person. Manners are important beacuse when you have them, you are showing respect for the people that is with you. Manners can change with the years, for example: few years ago, talk in the table with the cell phone was not impolite because in these years the mobile phone was not exist!. The manners could change with years but their objective is never going to change, the objective of the manners is to show other people some respect and show, in a particular way, that we apreciate them

In Mexico, even in this day and age, manners are considered important. Here are some important suggestions in case you will visit Mexico, some of this advice applies to another cities and remember, manners have to do with showing respect toward others. - If an elderly person gets on the bus, offer your seat, this advice applies to a pregnant women too. - If you travel in a car with a women always open her door and when she's completely inside close it. - If you visit a restaurant, always leave a tip. - Chew with your mouth closed. - If you visit a church, always take off your hat. - If you receive a visit, always offer food or drinks to your guests. - Always watch your language in front of children. - Wait to eat until everyone at the table is seated and ready to eat. - If you are visiting a friend and need to use his bathroom, remember to put the toilet seat down and flush the toilet. - Always make eye contact when you are talking with a person. I hope this manners helps you on your trip.

We can enjoy this fantastic scene in many different site of Havana but mainly in Old Havana occur impresionant projections. Is wonderful to appreciate the magnificent carvings of unequal materials as bronze, timber and iron. Famous Cuban painter present marvelous pictures. Another occurrence that I like so much are the fantastic choreography that made on the streets. I really enjoy all spectacle about traditionals Cuban dance. The Biennial of Havana is simply fantastic!

When I was young my family and I lived in Mxico City, but my mother used to take us to the Morelos State from time to time, in order to enjoy one of the most traditional dances for carnival named Chinelos. This dance was adopted in Mexico after the Spanish conquest as a native tradition into Christian festivals. I really used to enjoy very much the dance and the elaborated costumes. The Chinelos costume consists of a long adorned robe which usually has a rectangular shape, a mask made of mesh and a very expensive large colorful plumed hat. The dance is a set of repetitive steps and the choreography is very simple where the dancers perform two shuffling steps and then with one shoulder or the other, take a small jump to the right or left; but the best is that the dancers are always accompanied by a wonderful brass band. Most Chinelos dancers are young man and their role is passed from parents to children during many generations. Nowadays I continue enjoying the Chinelos dance when the celebration of my neighborhood takes place every year.

So long ago, when I was young I used to spend so many time thinking about my future, without realizing that my future was right there, right in front of me. I should have stopped thinking about future and just enjoy every they of my life, my real life, which was happening while I was thinking about it. I think it is important to have dreams, but what is more important is to work hard to make things happen. I have worked for the same company since I was seventeen years old, I am now thirty four years old and I think my job is good, but life is too short to spend all the time working on the same place. I feel like I need a big change for me, I mean, my job, my lifestyle.

Dear Sirs/Ladies, I am writting regarding the extra-hour payment for the last two weekend-job. My boss refused to pay the overtime, saying it was not his fault the delaying of the project . I  am within my rights to object to being refused overtime pay. As far as I concerned, the contract clearly states that I should be compensated for weekend work. Furthermore, nothing in the contract,allows you  to refuse to pay the employee for something that is not your fault. I have been working in the company for six years, I am hard-worker, always punctual in my attendance and get my monthly-goals, so I should be compensated for these extra-job. Sincerely yours, The employee.

The Folkloric Ballet is very popular on Mexico City, maybe is not unique, because I know that is popular in other countries as Guatemala and other Latin-america countries. The Folkloric Ballet is a traditional Latin-america dances that emphasize in the local folk culture. In Mexico are very special for theirs special costumes, shoes and dresses which are very flashy and attractive. The ballet works and musical pieces reflect various regions and folk music genres of Mexico. Many of the ensemble's works reflect the traditions of indigenous Mesoamerican culture. One of the pioneers of this dances is the group of Amalia Hernandez, they has a tour widely in the United States and has appeared in over 80 different countries. Also you'll hear Mexican music as "El son de la Negra", "La Culebra", "Las Perlitas" etc. In Mexico this dances were practice since the elementary school in a way to teach the kids about his folk.

Five years ago, my wife and me took a vacation on a cruise and went to the Greek Islands. These trips last about 7 to 15 days according to the Islands you want to know.The ships are enormous with 2000 passengers capacity or even more. The passengers are men and women and childrens of all kind of nationalities. There are many guides of tourists who'll take the tourists to the land tours aboard buses to know the archeological sites and important places as well as the usual places of the island like the stores, the market, the church and the beautiful landscapes. At the end of the day, people comback to the ship at a certain hour to have a supper and enjoy the musical shows with singers and dancers. Then people going to sleep because the ship going on during night to another island, and at the next morning the trip goes on. But the awkward situation ocurred at the fourth day when we woke up at 6 a.m. and after the shower, dressing and the breakfast, get down the ship and aboarding our bus, my wife suddenly told me ' I forget the purse with the money and the credit cards on the cabin under the pillow !%% 'My Godness! 'What a problem' At that moment we felt so embarrassed and made a decision to get down the bus and comeback to the ship to look for the purse. Fortunately, we found it. We lost the tour on that day but the awkward situation had a happy end.

There are some dancings in Colombia whose choreography is one of a kind. You can enjoy it everytime and everywhere in this country. But specially these days of the end of the year we can see the performances in the cities of the Coast because the vacation season is open. Then, the streets become crowded of artists. Music, lights and laughs fill our city with joyness. However, it exists a genre of music unique called "vallenato". You won't listen a music like this anywhere. Its cheerful melody is unrivaled and the lyrics, which are telling true stories about love and pain, are inspiring. Althought it's a very old genre, people continue singing and dancing it at the parties. In fact, if you come to Colombia, it would be nice to know why Colombians feel so proud of this music. Moreover, it's highly likely to enjoy it on the beautiful streets and nights in Cartagena or you can being part of a "vallenato" party by paying a very small amount. It would enriching to live a new musical experience, wouldn't it?

In Mexico City, since 2007,  elective abortion during the first 12 weeks of pregnancy, has been legal. However, abortion debate exists. The abortion debate refers to the controversy surrounding the moral and legal status of abortion. Pro-abortion groups argued that illegalization of abortion increases the incidence of unsafe abortions, and increases maternal mortality. From an ethical point of view, usually surrounds the issues of whether a fetus has rights, in particular a right to life, and whether the pregnant woman's rights over her own body justify abortion even if the fetus has a right to life. In my view, embryos have the potential to be a person, zygotes and fetuses have a right to life. Personally, to my mind,  if embryos had the oportunity to decide what they would like to do, they would choose the option to live and to know their mothers, no matter the conception circumstances. They are potential humans being...

It was an awkward situation that happened yesterday. All started the day before yesterday when a colleague realizes that there was a problem with a production line. This problem was serious and one of the options was to stop the production line to avoid wasting millions in raw materials but in the other hand it also implies to lose millions in sales. When my colleague was telling me the information he was as worried as Id never seen him before. When I receive the information, my first thought was stop the production line but then I realized it wasnt my right to take this decision alone. By the end of the day the note had spread out through the whole company but none wanted neither to make the information official nor to take a decision. Finally somebody give the order to stop the production and it was like lighting a match in a gas station. Today all the options have been analyzed and we are just about to take a decision but the situation wasn''t as awkward as the first person who gives the order had thought it was going to be.

Growing up in Mexico means to be proud of your roots, one of the most amazing spectacles that we have here is the charreada that is similar to the American rodeo, where you could see the charro performing tricks with the rope, the horses and the calves. The charreada is considered the national sport; the event is divided in 8 competitions for men and 2 for women. Some years ago I was trying to do something different I traveled to the desert and I rode a horse, I absolutely loved it, so a friend of mine invited me to go practicing the escaramuza, it''s really quite difficult because you have to learn to control the horse, and you have to perform choreographed pattern while the horse is galloping and you use a special saddle that put both of your legs in one side of the horse and you are wearing an enormous dress. Definitely is a spectacle than you have to see once in a lifetime!

topic: Buying a painting for a friend
text: Leadership is a complex quality. I had had to lead people since I was very young y I had learned, in good and bad situations, that a good leader has to adapt to circumstances: some cases you need be autocratic, some cases you need be conciliator, always keeping in mid the result that you want to achieve. In my opinion, a successful leader is able to drive to people toward goals considering the personality of each member of the team, so you have to adapt your leadership style for each person, because each person is different from the others, and a leader needs to be effective with all and with one. For that, the leader has to realize the strengths and the weakness of every single person in his or her team and take advantages from them.  Additionally, the leader has to drive her o his team as well her o his bosses, a good leader should have the power of the influence for to others: team, partners and bosses. A leadership theory says that a good leader only leads her o his team at 25% of the time, the rest has to lead her o his bosses and partners.

Some years ago, my father was invited to a wedding that would be carried out outside of the city, so he quickly prepared himself the clothes that he was going to wear during the event, including a pair of black shoes. The day before the wedding, he met my cousin to travel with him and his wife by car, in order to attend the event. Everything was fine and they stayed that night in a hotel, however the next day when my father was dressing himself, he realized that his shoes were different one of each other. That was really an awkward situation, but he decided to use them and try to buy another pair if possible. Fortunately, along the way to the church, they found a shoe store and he was able to get a new pair of them. At the end everything seemed to be ok, but the only issue was that the new pair of shoes wasnt as comfortable as an old pair.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA, or the North American Free Trade Agreement, was created 20 years ago to expand trade between the U.S., Canada and Mexico. It's secondary purpose was to make these countries more competitive in the global marketplace. It has been wildly successful in achieving this. NAFTA is now the largest free trade agreement in the world . One of the problems with NAFTA is that it's reduced U.S. jobs. A second disadvantage is that it has exploited Mexico's farmers and its environment .Rural Mexican farmers could not compete with low-cost corn and other grains that were exported by subsidized U.S. farm corporations. NAFTA put more than a million Mexican farmers were out of business. The ones that remained were forced to use more fertilizers and farm marginal land, resulting in more pollution and deforestation. Labor was cheap in Mexico because they had no labor rights or health protection. Thanks to NAFTA, nearly a third of Mexico's labor force were in this "maquiladora" program. I live in The City of Juarez, Chihuahua, Mexico. I grew up with this program and I find this so unfair. NAFTA called for unlimited transportation between the three member countries. This would have created a dangerous situation, since Mexican trucks were not up to U.%%S. safety standards, and Mexican roads could not have carried the heavier U.S. trucks. This problem was averted in 2008, when Congress prohibited this portion of NAFTA from ever being carried out

Corporate social responsibility its more and more important for companies. Since companies are or have CSR can develop employee satisfaction. By definition, CSR is active and voluntary social, economic and environmental improvement contributions by companies, usually the goal is to improve their competitive position and value-added value. The overall assessment system organizational performance in these areas is known as the triple bottom line. he main ethical responsibilities of the company with the workers and the community are: Serving society with useful products and in fair conditions. Creating wealth in the most efficient way possible. Respect human rights with decent working conditions which promote safety and health and human development and training of workers. Ensure the continuity of the company and if it possible, achieve reasonable growth . Respect the environment wherever possible avoiding any contamination by minimizing the generation of waste and more efficient use of natural and energy resources. Strictly enforced the laws , regulations, rules and customs, respecting the legitimate contracts and commitments . Maintenance of business ethics and anti-corruption . In order to mention someones...

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Mr. Madrid, I'm writing you to update on our progress. Clearly then that we will need to pay over time to 3 engineers to finish the project. However we identified the software problem on the models we will need to correct all the affected products and according with our customer we need to pack them all by the end of the month, that give us only 7 workable days, so the only way to reach the goal is work extended hours and on the weekend. Erasing unwanted memories would sound crazy. But is now possible using the electroconvulsive therapy (ETC). Researchers found using this technique the person will forget troubling memories. This method was used to treat severe cases of major depression.  ECT induces seizures in the brain. It isnt clear why it works to alleviate symptoms of major depression, but it is so effective. The research published in Nature was conducted with people undergoing ECT for major depression. They were told very troubling stories. For example one about a child whose feet were amputated. A week later, just prior to receiving ECT, they were asked to recall these stories. The idea was that remembering the details would shift the story into a very recent form of recall, where it might be temporarily unstable. This may mean that people who have gone through terrible trauma in war or in accidents can have the memory of those events removed by bringing them to mind and then using ECT to erase them.

It's difficult to talk about Corporate Social Responsibility because if we consider that nowadays we are living in an era of globalization and multinational corporations; most of them' obviously are focused on generating profits. However, as result of that situation, they are affecting the environment and the social welfare as well. Fortunately, at the present more and more companies are adopting CSR, although some people say that it's just a marketing device used for companies to improve reputation of their brands. Particularly, I think corporations should not be only responsible to their shareholders; its important to have ethical principles and moral to conduct business in the wider community and make good for the society, but there are powerful arguments on both sides of the issue. On the one hand, companies only are responsible to its shareholders, but on the other hand, community is letting them to do business. For that reason they should contribute to the sustainability of the environment and support the local community by taking actions based on ethical values for the benefit of society.

I think to be a successful person you need a lot of traits, but an extra thing that you need is good luck. However, you should to work on it, because you wont find the success in front of you. So, how do you can achieve your personal goals and become in a successful person? Most important, you have to be a persistent person. Think about what it is that you really want to do with your life, and go for it. Once you know what you want, stay focused. Don't let anything or anyone stand in your way. Don't take 'no' for an answer or let failure or mistakes along the way stop you from realizing your objective. It's your life and your dream, be it big or small. You will find a lot of obstacles during the way; take them like an opportunity to learn and avoid them the next time. Good luck.

Crime is among the most urgent concerns in Mexico, as a Mexican I'm very worried. Mexico has experienced increasingly high crime rates, especially in major urban centers, and is repeatedly marked by violence. I consider that drug trafficking were the main problem because it is the cause of another crimes as kidnapping, violent robbery, extortion, car thefts etc. The Government has changed some laws in order to reduce the crime, for example the sentence for a kidnapper or a blackmailer is a life sentence. Another thing that the Government did was the installation of thousands of security cameras around the city and a very large expansion of the city police force nowadays Mexico City has one of the highest police officers in the world with one uniformed police officer per every one hundred citizens, with this increase, violent and petty crime rates both falling significantly despite the rise in violent crime in other parts of the country.

There are three building in Mxico taller than 200 meters, two of them are located in Mexico City and the other one in Guadalajara: Torre Mayor (Main Tower), Torre Ejecutiva Pemex (Pemex Executive Tower) and the Riu Hotel . All of the buildings are measured from the main entrance to the top and include the spires, but not the antennas. Personally, I like buildings with character, more than tall buildings of course, this doesnt apply to New York, which I consider one of the most beautiful cities in the world with all the skyscrapers. Therefore, I like  buildings like museums, with their own personality. In Monterrey, my city, there is Marco, the Contemporary Museum of Art, which, despite the impressive collection of art that it houses, is a masterpiece in itself, with the solid structures, patio, and with all the elements that take us to the colonial times. I like also a beautiful hotel located in Mexico City (Camino Real Polanco) because its architecture includes various Mexican elements such as the patios, the tall windows and ceilings, bright colors and very vivid ambiance.

Mexico City is a blend between the history buildings and the contemporary ones. Mexico is a city created for the spanish people who came here in the 15Th century. They wanted to construct here the new Spain and because of that, they built over the arquitecture of the aztecs. Nowadays we can admire, mostly in the center of the city the ruins of the Tenochtitln city (this is the name of the ancient city). In the old city there was a lot of religious temples but they were covered with catholic churches. In this century we have modern buildings in some places of the city, so like I said before you can admire both. If you come here I recomend you to take the "turibus" this is a bus which can take you to different places of the city and they explain you the history of them and you can learn a lot of it. You can down of the bus where ever you want, it has a lot of stops around the city, but I warn you, this is one of the largest cities in the world !

My town used to be attended by local grocery stores I mean, mom-and-dad business for many years, but on 1994 was the year of globalization, due a international agreement that the government made between Canada and the U.S., so many big-box stores started to settle on the city and the people welcomed them in a positive way, it was the hip by then, everybody wanted to do shopping on these super-stores, due to the TV spots, as I was saying, all of these big-box stores hired many people and at first everything seemed to be good, because all the people had a job, and by then low vandalism activities were reported, and low bad social behaviors reports were almost cero. These two bad activities started to increase due that many people started to immigrate from different parts of the country, because of the high demand of work force by then, nowadays our city has growth in a high way, that now we have a lot of work force, instead of work demand, and this effect brought our city high poverty index,  low paid work positions and all mom-and-dad business are closed, due that they couldn't compete to these big-box stores

In my city where I live there are a lot of types of crimes, that these could be consider as petty crimes. However it doesnt mean that everybody can do such of crimes because if you get caught, you may be fined or punished in prison up to 6 years. There is a pilot project where all the traffic police establishes a point where they stops everybody whos driving a car, then they start by testing all the drivers first, with a few questions, and if they think that theyre in an alcoholic status, finally they do the last step which is the alcohol test. Its a kind of a alcohol test level, everybody blows into this alcohol test level thing, and if you get overpass the minimum levels, you may be put in prison up to 36 hours and plus a fine valued to $400 dollars. Before these actions, many people were allowed to drink lots of times and nothing was happening to them,  due the new law regulations now people stopped doing this illegal activity and no recidivism is applying anymore. Better life for everybody because, it lead to cause less car accidents nowadays.

topic: Talking a friend out of a risky action
text: Good night everyone and welcome to this special party for Alexander and Albert. My name is Joshua: a close friend of this young couple.  Id like to offer a toast for this cute and lovingly couple who had endured a lot of hard times together. Thanks everyone for coming to this special moment. Since I met these two guys, they seemed to be happy at each others side, even if they were just friends. Even so, they seemed to be connected; like they needed each other to survive. Alexander and Albert met outside the city, at that special bridge we know very well. Both of them had experienced a lot of painful moments. Albert was about to commit suicide when Alexander appeared in the middle of the darkness and managed to save the young wolf. Thats how the love for each other grew. Alexander is a smart, nice, gentle and understanding person. He is always there whenever you need help. Albert is an enthusiastic and sometimes crazy wolf that makes others to push themselves to the limit and never give up of your dreams. Albert and Alexander I wish happiness to both of you. Lets toast to these two.

I would define green business as the company that, in order to get economic growth, consider essential the social growth of its employees and users of its products or services, all from the sustainable use of the resources used: water, land, energy, etc... The first and last green company goal has a threefold aspect: creating economic value from social values and environmental. The green business, not only has to achieve the satisfaction of their partners or shareholders through customer satisfaction, selling more, but also it has to earn legitimately the satisfaction of every single part involved in the business activities, starting with its workers, following for its products or services and ending with the "satisfaction" of the environment it occupies, natural resources used and waste generated, care for the environment as his own. Finally, a good green company should try their best to communicate their good work and thereby help generate a sustainable consciousness that affects sustainable consumption. No, I don't go out of my way to buy green products, but I try to help the environment using the 3R's: Reduce, recycle and reuse.

The architecture in Mexico is diverse, major cities has a different architecture, but the most fascinating buildings are in Mexico city. Mexico city is one of the most populate cities around the world, and has a lot of interesting places. One special building is the arts palace, this located in the downtown and is a white stone building composed by 3 dome. Near, around 3 blocks to the south is Latin American tower, in the 90''s was one of the biggest buildings with 44 floors and all outside cover by glass. Now there are a corporate offices with buildings taller than Latin American tower, but this was one of the first and already survived to a major earthquakes, more than 8.5 grades. The last interesting place for me is the government palace, this is located in the downtown, has a really interesting architecture mixing many styles. Some of the areas are housing a collection about Mexico history. The only thing that you have to consider if you want to visit those places is the heavy traffic in the city.

Dear Mayor Bleak, I am writing to you to express my concern about the new superstore that is planning to open in our area. I strongly believe that this will force many small business to close down. If you, someday, come to Guadalajara for vacation, I promise you wont regret it. I can tell you that you would like to stay a few days more. The place is full of old Spanish fashioned houses from the 18th century. Most of them are centered at the downtown, next to the main cathedral. Some of those old fashioned houses had been turned into personal business by people that rent or live there. The most beautiful buildings had become museums or theaters. For example, we have the natural museum located right behind the Guadalajaras cathedral. Its main attraction is a huge mammoth skeleton right after the main entrance. 200 meters from the museum, we have the Degollados main theater: The most important of the city. This place has any sort of events, like operas, orchestras or plays. There are a lot of interesting buildings around there that even I havent seen or went, but Ive heard about them. If you come over here, just let me know and we will look for them.

I have no a deep knowledge about Superstores, however along my life I have realized how some rural Mexican communities have been more adversely impacted by the discount and prices offered by mass merchandisers. In this particularly case I am referring to Wal-Mart.  Nowadays, rural population is being strongly attracted by these new malls because their ease of access, low prices, great variety of products, controlled climate, parking space and their extended shopping hours. As result, sales for business in rural areas are decreasing dramatically while  superstores are operating in specific areas for a long period of time where people regularly attend and consequently cause losses of sales to  smaller competing stores. On the other hand, community is benefited through substantially lower prices, besides other important factor is that most of the superstores are offering credit to their customers. Currently, I think that people spend 80% in superstores and only 20% in local small business, so if the trends continue, local competitors will disappear in a short time.

A couple of years ago we try to change the route of the company by trying to assume a Social Responsibility with our own employees and with the Graphic Design community. We try to become a company that cares about human resources and understand that is our most valuable asset. We care for their well being and development.
