The Torre Mayor is a skyscraper in Mexico City. It's the tallest building in our country with a height of 225 metres to the top floor and 55 storeys. Located at Paseo de la Reforma, it was built by Canadian-owned Reichmann International on the former location of the Cine Chapultepec.Construction work began in 1999 and was finished in late 2003. Due to Mexico City's high propensity to earthquakes, the tower incorporates several anti-earthquake measures. Torre Mayor is one of the strongest buildings on Earth in terms of earthquake resistance, being designed to withstand earthquakes measuring 8.5 on the Richter Scale. Until 2010 it was also the tallest building in Latin America, when it was surpassed by the 236 meter high Ocean Two in Panama City.Since then, multiple new buildings in Panama City have exceeded it in height and the Gran Torre Santiago in Santiago (Chile) has topped out at 300 meters. In 2003 Torre Mayor surpassed by less than one meter the 225 meter (738 ft) high towers of the Parque Central Complex in Caracas (Venezuela). The Torre Mayor was developed by Canadian businessman Paul Reichmann, who also maintains part ownership. It is also part-owned by a group of institutional investors.%% The building was designed by the architectural firms of Zeidler Partnership Architects and Executive Architects Adamson Associates Architects, both of Toronto.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: The last year United States and Colombia made an agreement in order to reduce tariffs in import and export products. That is what we called "free trade". This agreement caused a big manifestation in Colombia due to the fact, merchants and entrepreneurs will going to lose privileges and their products will no longer be the choice for the buyers. According to the U.S Colombias economy is the third largest in Central and South America. It will create a new market for the entrepreneurs and traders and also is going to expand/promote economic for both. In addition, this kind of negotiations help to show more about our home products, culture and folklore. However, Many people in my country don't agree with it. Personally, I have to say that having the possibility to expand our market to new places is very positive for everyone. For example, people who travel overseas will be able to find goods from their native country. On the other hand, if that is not possible, they shall be obliged to consume food and goods that maybe they don't want to try. Moreover, giving the opportunity to dealers from outside show their products, will open our mind and perspective about the world and what they have to offer us.%%

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: It is too difficult to have a clear communication in my country. People has to be very careful when they communicate a message because verbal and non verbal communication has to match to get credibility. For example, if somebody said that something is pretty but said with a strong tone of voice and a rude facial expression the listeners would not heard that something is pretty, they might hear something like, it is pretty, shut up your mouth. Another challenging about communication in my culture is that people are not allowed to talk straight to the elderlies. In my country the youngers are raise to respect their elderlies and do not go against their ideas, even though if the elderly is wrong. In my country, for the elderly, it is important the way that you talk to them. We use a special tittle that shows respect, and if it is not used, the other person can be considered rude or the elderly can hear the message but he would not take the message seriously. I think the most important features about communication is having to match the verbal tone with the non verbal communication, build credibility and being willing to listen more than talk.%%

Mexico city its a huge metropolis where almost 70 percent of electricity is generated from fossil fuel resources which generates a lot of emissions, causing both local and global environmental problems, so I think wind power would be a good option to be adopted in my city because this kind of energy could be really beneficial to all the country because it doesnt cause air, soil or water pollution and its cost and maintenance are relatively low. Due to wind power generation consists in the conversion of wind energy into electricity by using only wind turbines; we can say that wind power is the least expensive of all other forms of renewable energy. The cost of construction of the turbine, installation and transmission facilities would come to only 80,000 dollars, however once they are placed there is only a little cost of maintenance. Another important reason to use wind power is that the generation of it doesn''t produce carbon dioxide emissions, which is important with our concern over climate change.

I still remember the delicious vegetarian lasagna they serve a month ago at their place. Please raise our glasses to Karla and Peter, to wish them a happy life together!

GREEN BUSINESS I think green business is mainly about being responsible with the environment. However, the subject could be quite confusing, because many unethical businesses or government agencies or political parties claim to be green just for money or as a mean to get the voters favor. Environmental friendly products are the ones produced with the minimal amount of natural resources and in an efficient way. Some examples of this are: products made of recycled materials, products made using sources of renewable energy or with biodegradable packaging. I dont go out of my way to buy green products, but I do pay a little more for greener products than for conventional ones. I also try to purchase the biggest presentations to avoid in excess packaging and I take my reusable bag to the supermarket. I also prefer to wash the dishes instead of serving in plastic or paper plates, no matter how many people attend a party at my place.

Nowadays, the global warming is already hurting the worlds most vulnerable populations, so I consider Green business should be important for everybody in order to slow the effects of Climate change. Fortunately, as result of this situation, many corporations are adopting a positive attitude towards sustainability by following best policies and practices to improve the quality of the life for their customers, employees, communities and environment. Particularly, I dont go out of my way to buy green products, but I have assumed the commitment with my planet and future generations by using renewable resources and applying all the time the three Rs rule. It means Reduce, Reuse, and Recycle, I that way we must Reduce our consumption and waste, Reuse what we have, and Recycle everything if possible. Its not easy to buy green product because they are more expensive that others, but we should sacrifice some luxuries to buy and incorporate them into our everyday lives in order to produce a positive impact on the environment.

I want to let people know that big-box stores are not as bad as they have been visualizing it. They are opportunity for this town to start walking to the next level. If we want to be in a status quo for life, then we can choose not to receive this kind of superstores, I want to reiterate that we are in a hard economic position right now and we need this incentive to our town economy. I would like to make sure everybody is analyzing this option in a very objective way, not just because we have been a pretty conservative town, but because we are now looking into the future of our community. It is very important to consider that big-box stores are going to bring us more benefits than cons. First, we are going to have better prices in town since competence will work by itself and also these types of stores can lower their prices since they buy products in really big quantities. Second, we are going to have more variety of products. Third, these stores are going to increase the employment rate of the town since they are going to generate several new positions to be filled by people of our city. Finally, please let's give us the opportunity to be a better town, one more modern and open to new things; we are not going to regret that attitude. Thank you, Everardo

Some years ago, my father was invited to a wedding that would be carried out outside of the Mexico City where we were living, so he quickly prepared himself the clothes that he was going to wear during that event, including a pair of black shoes. The day before the wedding, he met my cousin to travel together him and his wife by car, in order to attend the party. Everything was fine and they stayed that night in a hotel, however the next day when my father was dressing himself, he realized that his shoes were different one of each other. That was really an awkward and funny situation, as consequence he decided to use them and try to buy another pair if possible. Fortunately, along the way to the church, they found a shoe store and he was able to get a new pair of them. At the end everything seemed to be ok, but the only issue was that the new pair of shoes wasnt as comfortable as the used ones.

The kinds of energy that my city will be more benefit the most are tidal energy and wind power energy. Tidal energy is an energy formed with hydropower that converts the energy of ties into electricity. The tidal waves. The cost of implementing this energy in our city is low. The total cost of implementation is $60,000. Another good option to this city is the wind power. This kind of energy is the conversion of wind energy into electrical energy by using the electricity generated by the wind turbines. This system is perfect in this city, as well, because this city has a huge amount of available land to install the turbines, and also has specific areas with strong winds. The cost to install the wind turbines would be around $80,00. This include the cost of construction of the turbine and transmission facilities. A negligible fuel cost and relatively low maintain cost are the features that makes me think that this is a good option to our city population for save money in their electric bills.

I live in Guadalajara, it was founded in the 14th century. Since then to know, we find different types of architecture. Many styles converge to build the occident pearl. From the Barroco and Gothic buildings, to the modern skyscrapers  style. If you could divide the city on circles, you can see how it was constructed as time belts. On the center, the Barroco style represented by our Cathedral, with their curved arches and celebratory spires, constructed when the coloners arrived to the foundation of the city. The Espiatorio, a Gothic Church with their pointed arches surrounded by straight stone lines. There are not too many high buildings in the city. The architects build it on the edge of the city, tall financial buildings and the new ones, 200 meters from the main entrance to the roof, combining office, apartment and commercial stores, like if it was a total city, the owner's or renters don't need to go outside to do what they have to do.

topic: Hosting a group of foreign buyers
text: In my country people tend to communicate directly to one another than indirectly, another important recommendation is not to stay too close to the person, as this is considered rude because you're invading their personal space, so be aware about that. Gestures as in many others countries are used, but what I recommend, is to do a little research about the place you're going to visit. For example, the place where I live snapping your fingers it's considered very offensive, whereas in another places in the same country is considered normal. Due to diversity of cultures all over the country, many indigenous are living now in big cities, and they can communicate in their dialect and at the same time they probably can talk in Spanish and even English, so most of their cultures communicate with gestures and they  use a wide range of gestures including bad gestures and good gestures, be aware when trying to use gestures near them, as they may think that you're talking about them and they may try to appeal at you. In overall many different verbal and non-verbal communications are allowed in my town, but just be careful when doing gestures to the people.%%

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: ASEAN stands for the Association of Southeast Asian Nations. This is an agreement that was signed in the late 1960s by five countries: Indonesia, Malasya, the Philippines, Singapore and Thailand. Since that time, several more countries have joined ASEAN, including Vietnam and Cambodia. ASEAN is mainly concerned with free trade and improving the economies of htis member countries. Trade among the members has increased significantly since the agreement was signed. ASEAN also focuses on environmental issues like controlling air pollution and keeping the ocean clean. Almost all of this members have coastlines or are islands. The agreement also tries to promote peace in the region. The members believe that closer economic cooperation and increased trade will make war much less likely. ASEAN should not be confused with another acronym, APEC. APEC stands for Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation. This agreement was signed into law in 1989 and has more than 20 members, including the US, China and Australia. APEC also promotes free trade. One of its other main concerns is improving the living standards and education of people in Asia-Pacific countries

Dear Miss Hamilton, We've selected an ad campaign for Century watches. I hope this decision also likes you such as us. Allowing for the fact that Century is a classic and well known brand, famous for his quality and splendid designs, we choose a campaign in which the glamour be the most important thing to capture all kind of people. We think in the slogan "Your Glamour; your Century" and considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity into the campaign. However we decided that the quality of the watch was more important that celebrity endorsement, for this reason, we determine that the ad would feature a beautiful woman wearing a beautiful dress with a gorgeous Century watch surrounded by a lot of important people being jealous of her. We think that with this ad, we could be one of the top watchmakers worldwide. Hoping hearing from you and waiting for your decision. Kind regards ...

text: Mr. Charles Parks: About the sales plans you aske me for, I attached a spread sheet and the indications. I divided it ont three steps: Cold calls;  each supervisor has to fill the schedule considering the clients who doesn't buy anything the last three months, make sure how the speech is used as the client's necessity and do at least five calls each day. Meetings;  tracing the calls and the commitment with the costumer, assuring the solution of the problem or given the client's expects, that's what we call "certifying the quality service" Close sales;  just you have the order, send it immediately, track it and pull it or push it any time stop on the supply chain process. Furthermore;  we need support on prices, our competitors must not be more than five percent less than us. Promotion, each three months we have to reach a new surprise for our customer. And 98 percent on fill rate, all we offer will be on our stocks. Any questions, let me know.

I think it's a safe bet that the cost of traditional energy going to go up - making green business ideas related to saving energy and cutting energy costs also a safe bet. Building a solar panel or wind farm is a great idea, but probably out of most people's league. But that doesn't mean you can't start a green business involving renewable energy. For instance, solar water heating systems can provide significant energy cost savings (providing up to 70% of a family's hot water when properly installed, according to some distributors). In some provinces, such as Ontario, residents can get zero-interest loans or rebates for installing solar water heating systems (PowerHouse Program), making these systems even easier to sell. If this green business idea appeals to you, Natural Resources Canada's Solar Thermal information is an excellent backgrounder. Two green business ideas stem from this experience. One, as a retailer, I would definitely start stocking eco-friendly products. Two, green products would be an excellent theme for an ecommerce website.

In Ecuador, every city has its own architectural style. However, the city that I prefer the most, is Quito. This city was colonized by the Spanish in the early 1800s, hence it has buildings which are about two hundred years old. Quito, that is the capital of the country, can be divided in three different regions, the historical downtown, the suburbs, and the comercial area. The historical downtown buildings have an old european style of construction, you can see big houses with a courtyard inside, very tall roofs and a lot of rooms. The suburbs, encompass modern houses and buildings. The majority of houses and buildings has flat roofs and its design is based on a minimalistic style. Regarding the comercial area, it has a lot of old buildings, with antennas over the roofs, flagpoles hanging from the windows and very dirty sidewalks. The aesthetics of this zone is cinerious because the buildings are painted with opaque colors. In conclusion, the Quito comprises different styles of buildings on its territory that I classified in three: colonial, modern and comercial.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA is an agreement signed on November 1993 between Canada, United States, and Mexico. The objective for this agreement is to promote commerce and investment for North America. Many companies are situating their operations on Mexico border for many factors, cheap labor, not too much restrictive laws about environment. This is not in balance on a win win situation for both countries, Mexico and United States. US  Mexico employs for communities, but there are restrictions to Mexico for complete 100% this agreement. For example, transport of goods, US freights are allow to  entrance in Mexico without handling in the border, but Mexico transport can not because union drivers on US, so there is an extra cost for handling materials in the border. Other point is that importations into US from another countries like China can get access to Mexico using NAFTA, this is put in risk Mexican producers for raw materials like plastic, steel.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA (North America Free Trade Agreetment) is a free trade agreetment signed by Mexico, The United States and Canada. the main porpuse of the trade is to allow the enter of imports and exports beetween the countries without any tariffs. However as we learn in this lesson Mexico and Canada are major exporters of raw materials. becoming a big issue for Mexico, i.e you could think that theres no problem with these situation since the NAFTA was signed, but as raw materials you need to pass some formalities before thay could enter to the countrie, for example if you export food it has to be free of germs.In the majority of the cases it''s free of germs and bugs, but when it''s contaminated the food, isn''t allow to enter. These is another way to protectionism and for exporters of raw materials isn''t so convinient, a solution could be to develop their own companies to process the raw materials into  final products.

Guanajuato, a colonial Mexico''s city. Located in the midle of Mexican Republic, this city is famous by its architecture, history, culture and artistic activities. As you arriving, its first actractives are narrow streets though tunels, and alleys. The city was builded between hills, mountains and ravines. In addition, since 17th century, there were worked some mines. At the downtown, meet the Granaditas'' Alhondiga, a kind of warehouse in the colonial age. It is a big rectangular three floors building of rocks and mortar. At this place had an important Independence battle. Actually, the building operating as the Regional History Museum. La Paz Square is the most ancient of the city. In this place are located the buildings such architecture, the most beautiful like. Point up, the Church''s Nuestra Seora de Guanajuato, of baroque style. The Municipal Goverment Palace, constructed in 19th Century. The Rul Count House, the Legislative Palace and others, all of them builded in 18th century. The architecture is baroque and neoclassic. Other buildings to see are Juarez Theater, University of Guanajuato, Diego Rivera House and its markets. The most important yearly event is the Cervantino International Festival fulfilled in October, participating distinguished artists of opera, theater, music, dance and other arts.%% I like this city and its culture.

Talking about CSR and companies is always controversial. In Mexico there are many companies that use CSR only for publicity. That means, those companies donate money on a big TV shows, make notice on news papers, and at the end they can deduct those donations from taxes, so those are not true social responsibilities. For the companies that I work make an annual event in a local communities to provide food and clothes to the people, in this case there are no news or TV, but I don't think this is well enough. In the other hand, the customer that we have has a complete program of CSR, they are interacting with the community integrating people in to the supply chain process, they are constructing schools, hospitals and providing a stable job. This is a real case of social responsibilities and commitment with community. This was not an easy task, they are working on this program for 7 years and there are still things to do for many years. This is how mining business work in my country.

Nowadays, businesses are concerned in matters that go beyond the simple idea of selling. For instance, CSR has become a main topic to be discussed by the board members. What does CSR mean? Why is it applied? CSR is an abbreviation of corporate social responsibility. It refers to the way that a company rewards to the community. In other words, it is how the people are compensated for the possible damage that could have been done by the operations of the company. In these days, CSR is more frequently applied because the companies are pretending that they are concerned about society issues, but they use CSR as a marketing ploy. In my honest opinion, for example, if a company is really interested on donating a certain amount of money for security purposes in a run-down zone, which is the necessity of including the company''s name if it is "charity". Conclusively, some people can either, agree or disagree with me, but what is true, is that there is not a single company that is most concerned in solving community problems than in making money.

RENEWABLE ENERGY I believe that the renewable energy more suitable for my city is solar power energy, since the great amount of sun we have practically all-year long. I am a believer of this, but maybe solar power energy is not widely used yet due to the initial investment that it is necessary to do. Despite the initial cost, I am firmly convinced of the great savings that we can have in the long term. Provided that we really want to be more environmentally friendly, there are ways to obtain the money for the project, such as green-credits issued by the government or you can start with half the solar panels. I know some people whom, after installing the solar panels in their houses, have always received an energy bill on the amount of zero, or even in negative numbers! It is like a dream make true: no more bills to be paid -at least regarding energy.

It's important to define success when we are talking about it, in a manner of speaking it can come from a professional value or a human value. For me, success is the result of accepting many good and bad decisions we take. Being resilient, having disciplines, being persistent, being creative, managing people, know how to motivate people. Being confident. From people i know, some of them relatives to me, i would say that the most important thing about being successful, is to know how to do what we think, is to know how to make real what we create. Persuade people to live with passion as we do, and motivate them to do better than us. I think that disciplines make us to act consistently and therefore, to land our ideas. Finally, happiness, love and passion would be the first three ingredients i certainly would suggest, because people who are like that, they are the most success people i've ever known.

A green business is a company focused on the production of services and goods that are friendly not just with the natural environment but the human health. This world wide current involves: the production of non-chemical food, conservation of biodiversity and its habitat, protection of the natural environment and the global warming as well. I think that it''s complicated to give an opinion, because sometimes the green product term is misunderstood. For example: there''s a product considerate as green product based on its environmental performance; it would be a product friendly with the environment and the company that elaborated as well. But the process to elaborate the product could contaminate the environment. Therefore the company or business can''t be named as a green business anymore. Then, the elaboration of a green product doesn''t make a green company. Personally I don''t go out of my way to buy green products, only if I find them at my favorite store.

text: Dear Mr. Larkin, The reason I am writing is to invite you to our 5th company anniversary. As everyone knows, we are leader in textile and glamour. So, we're offering an exceptional fashion show with our best team- designers, models and workers- in our club. The concept this year is going to be "Colombia" . You'll have one of the best experiences ever. Best regards, Lina Hurtado. Sarish andamp; Co. Mannager.

Youll never believe this, but the most incredible thing happened today! I was sitting at my desk thinking and staring out the window. Then suddenly I noticed someone on top of the building next to mine. It was a man trying to ride his unicycle across the narrow edge of the skyscraper. I screamed and called my colleagues over. It was so alarming, we didnt know what to do. I mean, the stunt was great but terribly dangerous. And everything happened really fast. What he did was, he got on the unicycle and rode it for a few meters. Then he lost his balance. He landed on the roof, but his unicycle fell all the way to the ground. The guy really lucked out. Nobody on the ground was hurt by the unicycle. A big bowl of pudding that Puddilicious was using for marketing was broken by the unicycle, though. That made a huge mess in the street. A lot of money will be needed to repair the damage. It was the stunt mans fault, so I guess hes in a lot of trouble. Anyway, I thought youd like to hear that story!

Hey, everyone! Listen to me! I think the best thing we can do in a situation like this is to talk some stories. This way we can distract ourselves from this hard moment.  I heard some time my mom talking about a strange experience in an elevator. The problem in that occasion was that while a college girl and a boy were going down to the floor where the university's library was, they got sucked because the electrical energy in the university was cut on purpose since engineers were checking some machinery that had to be worked without energy. The problem is that it was 10pm and the electrical technicians never checked the elevators. The boy and the girl were talking about their careers, subjects and their hobbies, they never get upset or desperate, they just talk and talk all the time and left the destiny make its work. They were in the elevator for all night long and finally were rescued at the next morning. They started dating and finally, when they finished their college got married and now they are a wonderful family.%% You'll never believe this, but one of their 5 children is now telling you the story.  Hey! Look! Can you hear that? They are open the elevator's door, we have been rescued!

topic: Writing about a disaster relief effort
text: Dear Katy, I just want to let you know that I am still working in the project that you assigned. This project is challenging to me because I am not familiar with the topic. I had to read around 20 journals to get in to the theme. Most of the journals address the same issue. I think I have most of the information; however, I need more time to revise it and add more if it is need it. Honestly, I do not feel confident with this research because most of the foundings are still in observation. I think the results of the journals does not apply with the target population that your agency has. The journals mentions that results can be seen after months of the program implementation. However, in my personal experience, I do not think that the people from your organization wants to wait that long to see the desired results. Well any ways, I will report my findings and make the presentation as you requested.

I think there are more benefits having a big-box stores in the communities than cons. When there are superstores around the neighborhood, people have more choices of food and better quality. Also, superstores bring better prices that small groceries stores. Superstores delivery the week discount to your home and also tries to compete with the rest of the stores. Mom and pop stores does not do that. This small stores only offers products that are need it at that moment, with high prices. On this stores, usually the fruit and vegetables are not in their best quality. Normally, superstores guarantee you that you are buying quality food; also, this type of stores helps you to save money because they force you to do all the week or month grocery shop at one time, which it is a good strategy to save money and time. I really like having a superstore nearby home, however, also I appreciate having a mom and pop store nearby home because you can go to buy what you need in kitchen emergency situations.

How life will be in 25 years in the future? Mm. Thats an interest question. Well, since telecommuting is in the running, most offices will be empty. Companies will no longer need huge offices or even the office itself. Workers will be able to work wherever they want, whenever they want. Just imagine waking up in the morning, preparing your favorite drink like coffee, hot chocolate, etc. and work using just a computer and internet access. No more traffic jams, no more getting late. Also, laptops and desktops pcs doesnt stand a chance against mobile devices. They will replace them and theres nothing to do about it. Related to education, E-learning technologies will most likely be used for more and more students. Soon, classrooms will not be necessary anymore. Students will be able to study using his smartphone or tablets and download the resources into them. About traveling, you wont need a passport anymore. Your eye will be scanned and voila: no more papers needed. Isnt it amazing?

The crime in Mexico is such a big problem that Mexicans deal with on our daily basis. Recidivism has increased mainly, on purse snatching, kidnapping, sale of narcotics and murder, due to the cartel war. The low rates of apprehension and incarceration increase the problem. Many causes have been the reason for the crime but the most important are: drug trafficking, gun trafficking, unemployment and a bad management of the authorities. Sadly, the impunity levels make this country, some kind of crime paradise. Beyond the childhood, economy, culture or education, the incentive seems to be the almost zero possibility of being arrested and punished for any crime. Moreover, there are more than 400 prisons without the minimum operating conditions. In addition, the corruption and violence are the way of life. Prisoners don''t have any adaptation program or activities as sports or medical attention. Unless the system change they don''t have opportunities for rehabilitation.

That way, I will save more energy and money which is great for my country and my pocket. Moreover, I have been recycling many years ago, my parents always inculcated the way as I must separate the trash and explained to me how important is this fact to the environment.

The crime in Colombia is made when somebody break the rules written in Colombian constitution. At present, the most common crimes goes from petty crimes as purse-snatching to most serious faults as extortion and kidnapping. Everyday you can hear about different kinds of fraud or scam. People had learned a lot of tricks in order to take things from innocent people. The police is punishing very hard these people and the Government has been working in creating new laws in order to put them in jail for several years. However, you can see how they commit a crime without being incarcerated. Nowadays, the crime in my country has decrease due to the fact, the economic is growing up very fast, and also the government is providing free education for kids and grown-ups with economical disadvantages.  Nevertheless, People must know that Colombia lives an internal conflict with rebel groups who are fighting against the government and entrepreneurs.  Currently, The president is trying to get into an agreement with the rebel groups to stop war and make the peace.  It seems that is working up very well. Personally, I am being very positive, having always in mind that Colombia is gonna get better in few years.%%

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: since my point of view I totally agree with free trades between the countries,but before we make a agreement we must check out some points. 1.-Level of economy between the participants: for example all the participants must have the same economic level otherwise the richest country would take advantages over the poorer countries 2.- Education: both countries need to have the same level in education on their citizens in order to leverage all advantages that offer the trade, for example, if there is a subsidy the applicant must know how to fill up the documents otherwise the request will be denied. 3.- Sectors: traders must be careful with every sector they are negotiating for example if a country is producing grains to lower cost so they need to negotiate free entrance in order to take advantage of it but on the another hand if they are producing to a higher price that the other country they must to avoid the free entrance in order to protect the branch of national production This 3 points are very important to take into account when countries want to celebrate a trade

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: Mexico has a lot of free trade agreements with many countries, almost every one reduce import and export tariffs. The most important one is called NAFTA. It's an agreement between Mxico, USA and Canada signed in 1992 and started in January 1994. In the same time, in Mexico, some people rise in arms because they considered this kind of negotiation will bring to our country a decrease in jobs, flooded with imported goods, destroy the factories and our land will depend from the external nations decisions. After 20 years operating this contract, its farthest than real what they predicted. The economy increase, the opportunities to work rise more than expected, and the risk of national economy bankrupt is lower than before. I'm sure there is a lot of thing what this agreement didn't solve as it supposed to did, like inequality incomes on the people, the disparity between social classes, and the lack of opportunities to many people, especially in towns where they don't have enough schools and financial companies who helps them to improve their possibilities.

The historic architecture of Zacatecas, in Mexico, is marvelous. The city was named cultural heritage of the humanity by the UNESCO, and its well known for its old buildings;  every building has its own history and style. For example: The Cathedral, which is located over the ruins of pre-Hispanic temples, the current structure dates from 1752 and has a faade of sculpted pink stone. The best time to appreciate the face is in the late afternoon when the sun shines directly on it. This church is considered to be one of the best examples of the Mexican Churrigueresque architecture. I guess that this temple is the most photographed in the city. Just in front of the Cathedral is located the Calderon Theater, it was built in the late 1880s. The building has a French style facade and a hall with large mirrors made in Venice. The acoustic and seats distribution are excellent, you can enjoy any kind of performance. If you come someday, I have no doubt that you will fall in love of the city.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: A few days ago, my son and I went at my oldest high school. He was on a math competition, when we arrived I told him how pride I was about my school. He just told me how awful was the building and the local place where it was located, then I decided to compare whole to whole, my old high school versus his new one's. Old: Located at poor section in the city, Almost 10 classrooms grades from A to J, 40 to 50 students per grade, five class hour per day, there is no course cost, the teachers was so informal. Almost every student use the bus as is primary source of transportation. New: Are located in the highest economic section of the city. They have just 3 classrooms, 20 scholars per classroom. We paid a monthly contribution to upgrade and maintain the school. To get the job, the entire teachers have to make an exam and the lowest preparation level was bachelor. Almost every one goes to the school by his or father's car. Even the difference, if the student wants to improve his self , he will reach his goal no matter on it.

I really admire my father. I think he is the most succesful man I know. Even though it is not easy to pinpoint what kind of traits and habits make someone succesful, the excellent fruitage coming from his wise way to live has helped me to realize why he is who I would like to imitate. His childhood in the countryside was not easy: he had to suffer many overwhelmed adversities. But later, when he was 18 he moved to the city and got a job in a big company. He started as a common worker. That help him to get lots of practical, hand-some experience. Then, he gained the respect of his coworkers. Even more, he was worthy to receive the trust from his boss. At last, he became retired after 40 years being the most trust-worthy and beloved employee in the company.  Therefore, I wonder, what helped him to have such success? I have seen the answer all days of my life: The love for his wife and his sons moved him to get up quite early in the morning for long long time to bring something to his children's mouth. It was not the money the most important thing in his life.%% It was happiness. He got it and money too, to enjoy some of the life pleasures.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA On December 17, 1992,  President Bush, Mexican President Salinas, and Canadian Prime Minister Mulroney signed the historic North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) NAFTA is a formal agreement that establishes clear rules of commercial activity and investment between Canada, the United States, and Mexico. This trade agreement entered into force on January 1, 1994 and it has gradually eliminated most tariff and non-tariff barriers to free trade and investment between the NAFTA members in order to eliminate restrictions on the flow of goods, services, and investment in North America Since NAFTA came into effect, trade and investment levels in Mexico have increased, bringing strong economic growth, job creation, and better prices and selection in consumer goods. Mexican businesses, consumers, families, workers, and farmers have all benefited with better access to materials, technologies, investment capital, and talent available across North America Nowadays, NAFTA market is integrated for almost 400 million people with $6.5 trillion worth of goods and services annually. Mexico is the world's second largest importer of U.S. manufactured goods and the third largest importer of U.S. agricultural products

The environmental footprint of a business has to do not only what it does, but also involves your entire supply chain. Therefore, in Bimbo will work with our new actions more effectively in reducing our environmental footprint generated by our production processes and our suppliers. For transportation, we are working on initiatives and programs of clean vehicles. Our actions in this area focus on three areas: Clean Transportation, Optimization of Distribution Network and Fleet Renewal. In Bimbo they have a methodology to classify, quantify and separate all waste generated within the facility in order to they can be separated, in order to take advantage of those that are still useful for other processes and identify our areas of opportunity to reduce thereof.

The CSR is known in Mexico as Responsible Company. In my own opinion I think companies does have a responsibility to society, and the CSR is good because is a strategy to increase the profits and growing in a very competitive field. Companies don't just help the social communities in need but generate benefits for themselves. Therefore they care about not damaging the natural environment where the company operates, using renewable energy and recycling. For example there''s a building company named: 'Green Building'. This building spends 30% less electrical energy than a conventional building, also have a system of recycling water. In another case they set social projects that involve kids, and teenagers between 10 and 18 years old. In this program the bulling, drugs, sexual education, and other topics are given by professionals in the area. And for last there''s another big company that in 2012 adopts several social programs to avoid the underfed in low resources communities, like donation of pantries more over than twenty three thousand tonnes.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: I have a friend who's attending a university in Australia. We often discuss the similarities and differences between university life in Australia and in our own country. One of the big differences is flexibility. My friend tells me it's fairly common to change your major in Australia. This is not the case in my country. Unlike the Australia system, it is very difficult to change your major makes sense to me, as a person's interest might change as well. Another thing my friend tells me that's different is the level of formality. Even though professors are respected, they can also be your friend. Students in some classes actually socialize with their professors. By comparison, I don't think I would ever go to a party or restaurant with one of my teacher. Just so you don't think everything is different, my friend tells me that students in Australia are pretty much like students in our country. They want to get a good education so they can get a good job and have a happy life, but they also want to have fun. That's something I think we can all agree on.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA After years of negotiation, NAFTA began on January 1, 1994. In other words, Mexico, Canada and United States have been trading under this agreement for the last 20 years. I am very familiar with this situation because I live in Mexico, very close to the border with the United States and I have been experiencing a low of tariffs in goods, but, regarding the free trade of people, since NAFTA does not include that, we still have to use our visa to travel to the USA, like any other person from any other country. A few days ago, a friend of mine asked if it would be possible to form a zone in America as the Euro Zone, but we all conclude that it is not feasible at least in the short or medium term, because of the economic disparity among the countries.

I am really proud of the historic architecture of Mexico because it relates different historical epochs; however there is a wonderful and harmonious contrast with the modernism and avant-garde of the modern architecture. Nowadays, we can find in Mexico City several pre-Hispanic ruins and colonial buildings as well. Mexico Citys down town is well known as the place of the palaces because it has incomparable monuments and scenic panoramas of the National Palace and the Metropolitan Cathedral which includes a baroque and spectacular neoclassical facade and five separate naves and beautiful side chapels. Metropolitan Cathedral is the highest representation of the Roman Catholic Church in Mexico. There is also a small and enchanting colonial neighbourhood named Coyoacan which is representative of the old Mexico and includes several famous museums. On the other hand, Mexico has several modern and intelligent buildings like the Soumaya Museum, Twin Towers, World Trade Center, The trousers and hundreds of skyscrapers which are a real monument to engineering. In my opinion, the most representative building in Mexico City is the Azteca stadium. It was built in 1968 however nowadays it is a vivid demonstration of Mexican's devotion for the soccer.

A very successful person I admire is my dad. Nowadays, he is the boss in a petroleum company. In my opinion, he succeeded in his professional career because he is humble and persistent. Despite beeng the boss, my dad treat others very friendly. He makes them feel safe and confortable in the office. For example, once an subordinate employee made a mistake, and he was going to be fire. Hence, my father argued that he, as the leader of the filial, had part of the responsability, therefore, the board should fire him too. Thus, the board forgave them. Ever since my father was a child, he struggled in order to thrive. For instance, he always worked while he was studying, even when he was in high school. Additionally, to pursue a master''s degree, he earned a scholarship after being chosen among thousands of people. In conclusion, my father''s success besides inspiring me, teaches me to be humble and persistent.

topic: Writing an article about a superstore
text: Bank in Paris robbed by magicians in Las Vegas- A show hold in MGM Grand at Las Vegas was the place to steal a Bank in France. Las night show at the MGM Grand at Las Vegas, Nevada, presented how the Four Knights, magicians, stolen the Capital Bank in Paris, France. With more than five hundred persons in the show, the presenters showed all the people how they transport to Paris and draw the money out the bank. The magicians hypnotized a person from the public and sent him by teletransportation to Paris, he appeared just inside the vault, and he took the money and brought it back to the MGM Grand. But the robbery did not finish there, after the person came back through the teletransportation machine, it started raining money in the whole theater, so the audience could get some euros to take to home. FBI and Interpol are performing an investigation about the case when they found that the Capital Bank of Paris actually was stolen, so they are interviewing every one of the four magicians to solve this case and get the responsible for it. Reporting at Las Vegas, Nevada; Everardo Hernandez Sagarnaga.

I'm concerned about the enviromental warming, popullation and fosil fuels products that make damage to the earth. So I think we should use some strategies that could help our planet, and we could start with our city. We can implement solar power, if we do so, energy from the sun gets converted into thermal or electrical energy. Solar panels are used to absorb solar energy from sun's rays. Our city always has lots of sun light, and with a high quality solar panels we can have a very good energy production to our homes. We must consider the intallation and maintenance cost but if we think about it, through years we will have a great benefit about it. We could think to use Hydroelectricity or Tidal energy, but in our city we do not have water or sea to implement this type of renewable energy. Wind power sounds interesting but we need areas with strong winds and wind turbines installations, so the cost could be higher than the solar panels installation.

I think nowadays its possible for any one to become a successful person; all depends of our passion for the work and vision. Along my life I have known many successful people and I have realized how their specific way of thinking and acting are. Normally, traits that distinguish successful people from ordinary people can be noticed immediately because most of they are persistent, positive and optimistic in all the things that they do. I think optimism is an important factor to get success because it helps us to get the most out of our actions and its useful in every situation from work to life.  Probably, the key is that successful people do not let negative events alter their ego and they always go ahead until the completion of their goals. So, if we want to see results, we have to be persistent and try the things for a long enough period of time. I think it is the best advice that we can give our children.

I think in all countries killing a person by an accident is considered an unintentional crime and there are penalties for that. There was one case in my city, where a student kill a person by accident. A student was driving on a road when another car tried to cut the road and made the student to move to the other side of the road. Because he did a kick movement, the car rolled over to the contrary way road and fell on the top of another car. This killing two ladies that were inside of the other car. The police incarcerated the student and did not wanted him to let him go even for a bail. However, citizens meet together and collected signatures to release the student. Citizens push the government for his release that event the local university intervene on this issue. At the end, the police release the student and declare that it was not his fault and they declared him innocent. They were looking for the car that cut his way and make him to cause the accident.  The interested part of this story is that the parents of the two girls also support the decision of the judge on declaring the student innocent.

text: Dear, Mr. Jim Bacher. According with your instructions, given in the meeting held on December 20, 2013,  regarding the International Convention Center project, attach you can find all technical information, as well as, budgets and construction programs, in order to be in position to start the bidding process. These information include: Electric, plumbing and gas projects, Lamp dimmer system, support points project, lay out of equipment and all related to landscape design. I consider is very important to accelerate the reviewing and authorization of the projects, just to avoid delaying of the opening, programed for March 10, 2014. I will be in San Jos del Cabo on January 05, 06 and a half day of 07, I would like to meet you and plan all the logistic to comply with this important task. For any doubt, do not hesitate to call me or send me an email. My best regards, Mr. Jorge Ayala Len

My city has a unique mix of old and modern arquitecture, downtown buildings are colonial, mainly spanish baroque with arab influences, it was declared a world heritage site by UNESCO in 1987, some of the buildings are decorated with talavera poblana, wich is a kind of white and blue pottery originated by moors and brought to Mexico by spaniards; one of the buildings that uses tiles of talavera poblana is la "casa de los muecos", a beautifully decorated building that nowadays is a restaurant, and a museum which belongs to the major university in the state, also one of the main buildings in downtown area is the cathedral, one of the tallest colonial cathedrals in Mexico, the height of the towers is 74 meters, its construction began in 1575 and finished in 1690; on the other hand, Puebla has its modern area, a sector called Angelopolis, the developement of the sector began with the construction of the main mall in the city in the1990's, which the zone got its name from; the new skyscrapers, parks, schools, universities and residential areas are sited here.

Research Center Atmospheric Science; These comparative sheets show us the different meteorological measurement in Guadalajara, (the city where I live). Solar Energy and Wind speed. As you can see, Isolation (the solar irradiation) is an average of 5.5 kilowatts hour per square meter. Considering common houses has a television, a refrigerator, 8 light bulbs and some appliance like electrical oven, pc, wash machine and a water pump. It's consumes approximately 5 to 8 kWh each day. The electrical local company has a program who can buy you the electricity you don't use and exceeds what you need. Furthermore, during the day (morning and afternoon) where you dont use that energy, you can sale it to the electrical company, and during the night, where your needs are higher, you could buy it exactly what you need; it reduces your technical investment as is in saving the energy (batteries). Here we don''t have the death penalty, (electric chair,hang, lethal injection,...) no matter if the crime has been a serious one! We don''t have a court or a judge who can see the evidence neither. But we have the life impresionment !! In my particular point of view I don't think the prisoners can be reformed !!! For that I agree with the death penalty for mayor crimes: killers, violators, raptors, Why we should maintaining a person in jail who are not good to the society ?, I mean, someone who can give anything good for anyone?. Even we could be in risk, because this person could continuing commit a crime!!!. But unfortunately in my country the law hasn't been applied correctly, for that reason innocent people may be punished unfairly...

topic: Writing an article about NLP techniques
text: i use amazon.com as my personal site of e-commerce. Selling on Amazon is relatively easy. Create a mobi file and upload it. Add a cover photo and a description, set a price, and youre pretty much set to go. Its that setting of the price that initial ruffled my feathers. See, Amazon is trying to drive down e-book prices by creating an incentive to price the book under $10. Normally, you only get 35% of every book sale but if its under $10 then you can get 70% of every sale. Sort of. First, if its under $10 then you also have to pay a delivery fee. The larger your book is, the larger your fee is. The other catch is that you only get 70% if the book is sold to someone within certain countries. For books sold to people outside of that list, you still only get 35%. In my case, pricing the book at $9 on Amazon made sense. I sell the book for $15 on my own site but that includes PDF, ePub, and mobi formats and includes screencasts. On Amazon, you just get the mobi version. I felt this was fair.

What traits and habits make a person successful? Is a hard question and I think that in order to be able to respond it, we have to define success first. But again what success means to me is not what everybody might think it is. We can have success in all areas of our live, business, academic, social, spiritual maybe in our love life or physically. And we can focus only on one type of success and work forward to reach it. However, at least for me, I prefer define success as a whole conformed by well being in all the areas that I mentioned before. I can''t imagine myself being happy with lots of money but bad health and no friends or family. The people that I consider successful have similar traits, each and every one of them have a lot of discipline and determination, discipline can make a huge difference, they also are very kind and sociable and I think the most important trait is to be passionate and stick to your believes.

topic: Attending a seminar on stress reduction
text: My best friend attended a university in the United States. she told me that there were several differences between attending a university in the United States and Mexico. In the universities in the United States, you have the option of being a part time student. Different from the university in Mexico, you have to follow the full time curriculum if you want to complete the educational program. Another difference is the cost. The tuition in the United States Universities are high rocking , in contrast to Mexican's university, the tuition are high but are still available to pay for the lay students.  In contrast to the United States Universities, students that attend to universities in Mexico, have to pay their tuition in cash. Most of the students that attend any United States Universities, have the option the tuition in cash, or get a government loan or waiver. This option allow them to pay off their educational loan after the students graduate and get a job. Even though education in Mexico is cheaper, not all people can pay their educational tuition. In contrast to the United States, education tuition is so high expensive but there more options, so there are no excuse for American residents to get academic education.%%

The Nautilus house has one of the most unusual architecture I have ever seen. This whimsical design carries forms of aquatic life to architecture. Its forms are inspired on the nautilus shell, creating an original building, atypical and very attractive to our eyes. Completely integrated with its natural environment, this construction has spiral lines that convey the sensation of relaxing habitat. The multi-colored crystal provides a several variety of chromatic tones of the light from the outside. Furthermore, the house hasn''t internal divisions, gaining a harmonious space. It is an expression of free space, in which the main concept is that inside is an organic home. It has been constructed with ferrocement for a double purpose: first, the structure is an earthquake proof house, and second, doesn''t need any kind of additional maintenance. The Nautilus its surround by many high buildings except on the west, from where you can see a beautiful view of the mountains.

Mexico City is the largest city in the world. Despite its large size and its sixteen million slums, the center of Mexico City is actually very clean, and the architecture matches that of European cities. During the 1985 earthquake many of the historical buildings in the city were severely damaged. Unfortunately due to a lack of funds, and possibly a lack of government interest, many structures haven't been repaired, therefore many buildings appear to be condemned. Despite all this, every skyscraper in Mexico City is a monument to engineering. The tallest buildings in Mexico City are: with 738 ft. the Major Tower and with 801 ft. the Reforma Tower. Another taller buildings are: - Pemex Executive Tower with 693 ft. - Riu Hotel in Guadalajara with 705 ft. - Altus Tower with 640 ft. - Latinoamerican Tower with 597 ft. (this is one of the oldest buildings in Mexico City was constructed in 1956).

Mexico City is one of the most populous cities in the world. Is modern and cosmopolitan, multicultural and packed with historic sites. Mexico city is an exciting blend of Aztec, Spanish colonial and modern art and architecture. It''s also a huge, sprawling city. The Plaza de la Constitucin or Zocalo is the city''s main square. The Presidential Palace, with its famous Diego Rivera murals, is a beatiful place to visit. The surrounding Centro Historico (Historic Center) is a virtual museum of colonial architecture and was built on ruins of the Aztec capital, Tenochtitlan. The Templo Mayor, one block from the Zocalo, is the most important relic of that period. The Torre Latinoamericana is located near to the Zocalo and is one of the tallest buildings in the zone. It is an amazing building built more than fifty years ago. It has an unique observation roof that takes advantage of an equally privileged location in the heart of downtown Mexico City. We can forget the Palacio de Bellas Artes (Palace of fine arts) that is the most important cultural center in Mexico City. It is located on the west side of the Historic Center of Mexico City.

In my city home the tallest building has only 5 or 6 floors, so Id rather talk about another building. Let's talk about the tallest building in my country: La Torre Mayor. It is 230,4m tall, it has 55 floors and 13 levels to park 4 of them underground. Its construction started in 1999 and ended in 2003. This building is equipped with 98 seismic dampers and 252 piles made of steel and concret buried 60m deep, it can hold out winds of 257 Km per hour, its glasses are 2.5 cm. Thick, so it is considered one of the safest buildings in the world and the safest in Latin America. This tower is considered along with the U.S. Bank Tower, one of the world's toughest skyscrapers, and also has the most global seismic tolerance, having a maximum tolerance of 9.5 on the Richter scale, He has endured two earthquakes, one in 2003 of 7.6 degrees on the Richter scale, and in April 2007 of 6.3 degrees on the Richter scale. I'm very proud of this building, and pretty sure that you going to feel safe visiting it.

Dear Alexia, We've selected an ad campaign for Century watches. I'm sure you'll love our choice of campaign. Many people think that Century is a classic and well-known brand, because it featured some of the most famous stars in advertising campaigns. While this might be true, it's also true costumers prefer Century because it's simply the best and most reliable watchmaker on the market. That's the reason why Century has managed to be one of the top watchmakers worldwide. Considering Century's trend of working with famous stars, we contemplated the idea of bringing a celebrity into the campaign. We decided, however, that the quality of the watch was more important than celebrity endorsement. For this reason, we decided that the ad would feature a man playing golf, simple and crystal-clear, but with slogan that delivers an efficient message; a message not directed to stars and athletes, but directed to each and every man that will read an look at our ad. "Golf and Century. The perfect team. "It's a simple message that everyone can understand. I look forward to hearing from you regarding our decision. Best regards...

Technological advances are increasing continuously, so its very complicated to know how will be our life in 25 years, however taking in consideration current technology that we already have. Undoubtedly it will be integrated in most of the devices that we use every day and new discoveries and functionalities will be implemented in our homes. Probably places with major impact could be the bathroom and the kitchen because we have there many useful devices with the most potential for futuristic changes. For example, talking about the bathroom I can imagine how future toilets would be able to test urine for sugar levels, take your blood pressure or weigh and upload that information in the cloud to be sent to the doctor. Our fridge will also be able to scan our body and tell us what's inside and make a recommendation about what we should to eat or just to create a grocery list and send it to the supermarket automatically. Besides, with future advances in computing and nanotechnology we will have the opportunity of seeing new developments in medicine and science.

The skyscrapers of Mexico Mexico is a country where pre-Hispanic ruins exist in harmony with colonial buildings,  We have breathtaking  pyramids, temples, and sculptures.  If you visit Chichen-itza in Quintana Roo, you will love this magical place. With the arrival of the Spanish were introduced architectural theories of classical order and Arabic formalities, to build the first churches and monasteries  The zocalo plaza in Mexico City is 57,600 metres' (240 m  240 m), making it one of the largest city squares in the world. We also have the cathedral, is one of the oldest and largest Roman Catholic cathedral in the Americas, And its called, The Metropolitan Cathedral of the Assumption of Mary  Mexico City had its first 40-story skyscraper almost half a century ago when The Torre Lationoamericana, the tallest building in Latin America at the time, was built.  Today, there are hundreds of skyscrapers challenging nature in Mexico Citys skyline. The Torre Mayor , The world trade center, and many more skyscrapers invade the skies of my beautiful Mexico..

Some of the most common petty crimes in Mexico are the pickpocketing and purse snatching. These kind of wrongful acts mainly occur in the crowded metro (subway), taxis and buses. However other thieves commit crimes against visitors in airports and public transportation stations, especially in urban areas. Petty misdemeanors generally are punished by up to six months in jail, a fine of up to $1,000 or both. If there is recidivism, the felony is punished by up to three years in prison and a fine up to $5,000. Juvenile delinquency in Mexico is punished in a different way and youth aged 18 and under are sent to the Juvenile Detention Center for rehabilitation where basic education and various trades like plumbing, electricity, automotive mechanics, bakery and cooking are offered to inmates in prison, although I must honestly admit that most of them gain experience there to commit more serious crimes when they are free. Even sceptics claim that in many cases, prison education only produces more that better educated criminals, which is really disappointing.

I really admire Fusajiro Yamauchi (NINTENDOs father), for more tan 50 years his only product was a board game called Hanafuda. Afterward in 1963, his grandson attempt to make some money investing in a taxy fleet, following that he tried to managing a hotel chain, subsequently he bought a restaurant and finally, he tried to own a TV channe. he failed in all those business, but he never give up, he is very persistente and an amazingly resilient person. He got a lot of debts, but in 1970 they focussed in a new product: the console video games. In 1975 they started to produce the first video games that turned the Nintendo Company history. In 1983 was born the very first 8 bits console Nintendo Entertainment System (NES), and with it Mario Bros y Donkey Kong. I think that Fusajiro Yamauchi and his grandson knew that every failure comes with a teaching.

Crime in Mexico is a common situation, this is a consequence of drug dealer battle for territory. North area of the country has been affected by crime. Is common to read on the news about murder, kidnapping, extortion. Those major crimes are punished by jail for at least 18 years, in Mexico is prohibited capital punish like letal injection, but in my opinion in this cases will apply. Other minor crimes are not punish by the law, for example minor thief with not violence on people under 18 years, teenagers just receive an advice and their parents need to pick up them at the court. But in case that in the thief is involve weapons, murder, or violence, they will go to preventive jail, and at the age of 18 receive a sentence in some cases for the crime. Those crimes are related to the common people, but all crimes that companies and government are doing, like taxes evasion, they move millions of pesos without punishment.

well there is not to much to write about mi city, I live in monterrey and its just and industrial city, buildings are just slowly appearing. However, I can talk about my country, especially about Mexico city. Mexico City is the oldest city in Mexico, It was there where the first inhabitants settled to establish the first cities. In this period we can name some tribes like aztecs, mayas, etc. All of them with the type of architecture. When Spanish arrived to conquer the country, all these architectural elements got mixed and now a days we have a wondrous blend of styles. Mexico city is characterised for both, traditional and contemporary architecture. In the north and east we have all this colonial style where you can see old buildings, churches, monuments, theatres, and countless things. If you go to the south, you will see the modernity in all its expression, there is located "Santa Fe", a new area or neighbourhood where its all covered by new, modern and beautiful buildings. We have building all over the city, It is just kind of jam-packed with all these skyscrapers really close each other.

text: Mexican and American schools have a lot of differences between them, but I will summarize at least the three main differences between countries. First of all, in Mexico the teaching system applies for all students of the republic, whereas in the U.S. each state is independent and depending on the status if its a poor state or a rich state, so all students will have different chances of success depending to what state they belong. Another difference, is that while in Mexico the days of calendar for school are 208, in the U.S. only have 180 days, so this means, that at least students from Mexico are allowed to learn more from school than U.S. students due the days of calendar, this isnt a guarantee that Mexican students get the best teachers at all. And finally, I see an advantage from students in the U.S. regarding that they get better facilities, plus transportation and they have better standards of teaching qualification, that the government applies to the professors in order to teach, whereas in Mexico a lower standard of qualifications are implemented, due that there arent many evaluations methods to the professors, and the way the professors get the teaching positions is unfortunately by inheritance.%%

Lets begin for recognizing that every company does some harm to society. Therefore, it is mandatory to give back to people as a mean to compensate for such harm. A good point to start is by acting in internal issues like paying a fair salary to all the employees or promoting people to positions according to its competences and skills, no matter if there are men or women, or how handsome they are. I have heard of some companies that excel in CSR, among them we have Starbucks that purchase the coffee grains only if workers used to recollect the crop are well compensated and if the children of the workers received a healthy breakfast daily, at no charge. I have a hope: companies begin doing CSR for an economical reason, but after a while, they do that because they really care about making a difference in the world.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: NAFTA stands for The North American Free Trade Agreement. With this agreement, Canada, Mexico and The United States works their best to eliminate trade and investment tariffs. So far, this agreement has help Mexico to eliminate tariffs in exportation material to the United States, more than one half and more than one third of the United States. This agreement has had several benefits in Mexico. For example, there was an increase in the maquiladoras in Mexico. Furthermore, there were more employment available for the Mexican citizens. The low tariff for new maquiladora investors attracted them to set a maquiladora in Mexico instead of other countries as China or Thailand. Also, because this agreement, the United States concede temporally work permits for people who wants to go the United States to work or study. This is a good opportunity to people from low income  countries or cities that want to work just to make money and return to their cities to invest their earning made in the United States.

text: Canada, United States of America and Mexico, their respective leaders signed The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), begining operations at January the 1st on 1994. The objetives to get on, were: eliminate obstacles to trade and make easy the traffic of goods and services among the three countries; promoting the loyal free competitivity over the zone, increasing opportunities of investment on the territory, protecting the intellectual property rights, creating procedures to get the best managing and strengthen for NAFTA, expanding benefits to the three trade partners. In fact, NAFTA had been succesful for all the three countries, in consequence, Presidents recently decided not opening to none negotiation, such it is, worked satisfactory for partners.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: FTA EU-MX Free Trade Agreement between Mexico and the European Union, is a trade agreement signed in the late 1990s, in the city of Brussels. On the middle 2000s the agreement came into force, and taxes applying to a large quantity of importing goods were eliminated or reduced. The goal of this trade agreement is to establish a framework to encourage the development of trade in goods and services, taking into account the sensitivity of certain products and services sectors, and in accordance with relevant world trade organization rules. To achieve this goal, the Joint Council is responsible for deciding the arrangements and schedule for the liberalization of duties and non duties barriers to trade in goods. The decision shall include: - Customs duties on imports and exports. -Quantity restrictions on imports and exports. - Safeguards and monitoring measurements. - Customs cooperation. - Standard and technical regulations. - General exceptions. - Restrictions in case of difficulties.

Chiapas governor in Mexico, Mr. Manuel Velasco said last week that is very important to work with the society and the federal government in order to establish a new educational reform to ensure that it archives the established objectives according with the president Enrique Pea Nieto. Currently Chiapas is placed as one of Mexican states that has the biggest challenge to improve the academic quality of its schools and reduce the dropout rate. Velasco said that during his administration has launched many programs to provide incentives to stay at school, as well as to improve educational programs, including support for feeding. Additionally Mr. Velasco stressed that with the new educational reform, tutors and teachers will be able to determine priorities and decide how to use economical resources provided by the federal government, resulting in major changes in the ways school system go about making policy, generating revenues, spending founds, training teachers and helping to improve the quality of education in Chiapas.

In Mexico, it doesnt matter if you are minor or an adult. Whether you commit a petty or a serious crime, you''ll always go to jail. The place you''re sent depends of how old you are. If youre under 18 years old, you''ll be sent to juvenile detention. If youre an adult youll definitely sent to prison. Also, if you commit a petty crime, youll have to pay a fine or spend a few days in prison. But if you commit a serious crime like drunk driving youll have to pay a fine of $1,200.00 or spend 36 hours under arrest. Whether is a petty crime or a serious crime judges will not erase your personal crime record. It will always be there forever. Even so, there are still companies that hire you even if you commit those crimes. They are interested on what you can do well, instead of looking about your past.

topic: Writing a brochure for a museum
text: LAFTA The acronym LAFTA stands for the Latin American Free Trade Association. It was created in 1960 Treaty of Montevideo by Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Mexico, Paraguay, Peru and Uruguay. The signatories hoped to created a common market in Latin America and offered tariff rebates among members nations. In 1980, LAFTA reorganized into the Latin American Integration Association ALADI which now has 12 members. The Latin American Free Trade Association was one which came into effect on January 2, 1962. When the trade association commenced it had seven members and its main goal was to eliminate all duties and restrictions on the majority of their trade within a twelve-year period. The goal of LAFTA is the creation of a free trade zone in Latin America. It should Foster mutual regional trade among the members states, as well as with the US and the European Union. The LAFTA agreement has important limitations: it only refers to goods, not to services, and it does not include a coordination of policies. Compared e.g. to the European Union the political and economic integration is very limited. In other words, It isn''t a real free trade.
